title: Structuralize Ruby loop control and for lowering
description: Observed: TFor, TBreak, and TContinue still cross raw Ruby or print-and-reembed boundaries, and the for-loop iterator temporary bypasses the request-local name allocator. Objective: represent ordinary Ruby loop control structurally, preserve Haxe iterator evaluation and control-flow semantics, and remove the bounded raw lowering debt.
design: Use Pattern B and the existing structural RubyAST. Add closed no-payload break and next expression forms because Haxe typed break and continue carry no values. Build TFor directly as an iterator assignment plus RubyWhileStmt and loop-variable assignment. Select the existing runtime iterator intent through compileExpr, allocate the iterator local through the request-local collision-safe allocator, and keep the printer formatting-only. No LoopPlan or semantic IR is admitted because there is one fixed lowering strategy and ordinary target structure retains every required fact.
acceptance_criteria: Exhaustive children, validator, printer, token coverage, and inventory all recognize the new nodes; TFor no longer renders or re-embeds Ruby; focused tests prove one iterable evaluation, break, continue, nested loops, and adversarial local-name collision behavior; maintained generated-output and runtime gates pass; full npm test and canonical exact-SHA CI pass; bead closes only after push.
notes:
Architecture-template application: this is a vertical core-lowering migration. The source facts are loop variable, iterable, and body; all remain directly representable as Ruby assignments, calls, and while. Generated output parity is expected for healthy programs; the collision fixture may intentionally improve output.
Evidence refinement: Haxe 4.3.7 normalizes ordinary authored for loops, including Context.typeExpr input, before RubyHx sees them. The focused build macro therefore assembles a valid residual TFor from Haxe-typed variable/iterator facts and proves its structural output directly. The former position-derived double-underscore temporary was isolated in practice, but no historical collision was reproduced; the shipped design instead makes collision handling an executable request-local allocator invariant with a target-private hx_iter name and an adversarial pre-reservation test. The migration removes seven classified sites (five raw loop-control/for nodes and two print-reembed iterator sites), lowering the inventory from 318 to 311. Focused AST, loop runtime, formatting, decomposition, and inventory gates pass. No LoopPlan or general IR was admitted because ordinary Ruby structure retains every required fact.
Final-tree local evidence: full npm test passed, including all 32 examples, snapshots, 715-file Haxelib packaging, and gem packaging. REQUIRE_RAILS=1 mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime passed through components, controller params, Action Mailer, Active Job, Active Storage, Action Cable, integration, and interop. Playwright passed 15/15. Production passed 26 runs/231 assertions plus eager load and release archive. Haxe formatting checked 647/782 files, npm audit found 0 vulnerabilities, ruby-advisory-db checked 1,224 advisories and detected the vulnerable control, gitleaks found no leaks, and git diff check passed.
Hosted implementation evidence: canonical main run 29681012077 is bound to 8473e5a81889e321cad702cb14cc01aa0aa6af5d and passed all 14 jobs, including Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0 compiler matrices, Rails runtime 3.3/3.4/4.0, browser, production, packaging/release contracts, formatting, Node compatibility, and security. Final job 88180018806 analyzed the refactor and reported no release; semantic-release emitted “There are no relevant changes, so no new version is released.” Independent hosted checks found no v1.10.3 tag or release. Immutable v1.10.2 remains latest with the four expected ZIP/gem and SHA-256 sidecar assets.
close_reason: Structural Ruby loop lowering is implemented, pushed, locally verified, and proven by canonical exact-SHA CI; release policy correctly produced no v1.10.3.

title: Add exhaustive RubyAST child traversal contract
description: The live Pattern B pipeline has explicit RubyAST validator and printer switches but no authoritative schema for every immediate AST child and statement-body scope. Add the smallest output-inert child/traversal contract before introducing more structural nodes so future validators, analyses, and transforms cannot silently miss children.
design: Add exhaustive immediate-child mapping for RubyFile/RubyStatement/RubyExpr and nested parameter, argument, block, hash, and case carriers. Encode declaration versus executable statement-body roles in the schema. Derive deterministic walking from that schema, keep raw nodes opaque, and refactor RubyASTValidator recursion to consume it. Do not add semantic nodes, passes, phase machinery, printer behavior, or intentional generated-output changes in this slice.
acceptance_criteria: Every child-bearing constructor is covered without catch-all/default handling; traversal exposes deterministic child order and declaration/executable roles; validator uses the shared schema and rejects malformed required collections before printing; identity/sentinel/scope tests cover all child-bearing families; Ruby AST, inventory, snapshot, runtime, packaging, Rails, browser, and production gates remain green with generated bytes unchanged; exact-SHA canonical CI is green.
notes:
Reprioritized after reproducing the applicable findings from the Modern Reflaxe compiler architecture template against the live tree. Pattern B remains the declared architecture; this is Stage 1 output-inert infrastructure, not an IR or pass-manager rewrite.
Implemented the output-inert Pattern B traversal foundation. RubyASTChildren now exhaustively maps every RubyFile, RubyStatement, RubyExpr, method-parameter, callable-argument, block, hash-field, and case-branch child without catch-all handling; it owns declaration/executable body roles and derives deterministic preorder walking. RubyASTValidator recursion now consumes that schema and adds fail-fast null checks for required child collections/carriers. Exhaustive constructor, identity, sentinel, scope, malformed-shape, and ordering tests pass. The checked lowering inventory is 324 sites: its only two new entries are the explicit opaque RubyRawStatement/RubyRawExpr leaf cases in RubyASTChildren. Generated snapshots remain byte-identical. Exact final tree passed npm test (32 examples, packages, full compiler/runtime/Rails suites), formatting, npm/Ruby advisory and Gitleaks security gates, Rails 8.1.3 runtime, Playwright 15/15, and production 26 runs/231 assertions with eager load clean. Canonical exact-SHA CI remains the post-push closure gate.
close_reason: Implemented exhaustive RubyAST child/scope schema with validator adoption and complete local regression evidence; ready for exact-SHA canonical CI verification.

title: [REL-001] Bind gem packaging to the selected Ruby toolchain
description: Independent v1.10.0 byte verification found that build-gem-package.js resolves ruby and gem after changing into temporary staging directories. With rbenv, that loses the repository .ruby-version and silently selects the system RubyGems, producing different metadata bytes even though the hosted CI artifact is correct. Resolve the selected Ruby once at repository entry and use that exact executable for gemspec evaluation and RubyGems packaging.
design: Use RbConfig.ruby from the repository-selected interpreter, then invoke RubyGems through Gem::GemRunner under that absolute Ruby. Do not depend on rbenv-specific RBENV_VERSION or a cwd-sensitive gem shim. Preserve canonical gem bytes under the pinned Ruby/RubyGems toolchain.
acceptance_criteria: The gem builder resolves an absolute Ruby executable from repository context; gemspec evaluation and Gem::GemRunner use that same executable in temporary directories; a cwd-sensitive toolchain regression fixture proves ambient ruby/gem shims cannot change or intercept staged builds; reproducibility, gem package, release contracts, full CI, immutable patch release, and closure no-release evidence pass.
notes: Reproduced and fixed on 2026-07-18. The old builder lost repository Ruby selection after entering temporary staging and rbenv fell back from Ruby 3.4.10/RubyGems 3.6.9 to system RubyGems 3.0.3.1. Hosted v1.10.0 was correct under pinned CI; only local default rebuild metadata differed. The builder now resolves repository-context RbConfig.ruby once, reuses that absolute interpreter for executable gemspec evaluation, and invokes Gem::GemRunner instead of a cwd-sensitive gem shim. Hostile ruby/gem PATH shims prove the regression. Complete npm test, mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime, exact-SHA 14-job workflow 29663936094, and release job 88134002479 pass. Immutable v1.10.1 tag resolves to 639701ae779764573633970d14b694314a20ca4b; exactly four downloaded assets match API digests and sidecars, complete 712/334-entry manifests/provenance verify, and an unpinned clean local rebuild is byte-identical to both hosted packages. Closure is carried by the non-releasing evidence/bead commit whose canonical run must remain green.
close_reason: Implemented, fully verified locally and in exact-SHA canonical CI, published as immutable v1.10.1, and independently verified from downloaded release bytes.

title: [DEVEX-001] Add change-aware RailsHx development runner
description: The existing Rails-native watch commands are discoverable but recompile unconditionally every interval, run server/client as separate polling processes, and duplicate their initial builds under hxruby:start:watch. Add a responsive one-command development loop that builds once and recompiles only targets whose checked inputs changed.
design: Adapt the proven sibling haxe.elixir.codex behavior at the product boundary: one initial server/client build, HXML/classpath and Rails metadata input discovery, a portable change-aware snapshot loop, debounce/coalescing, one coordinated server/client watcher, recoverable rebuild failures, and one canonical hxruby:dev command while preserving existing aliases. Keep one-shot/CI compiles direct; do not introduce a partial managed haxe --wait server without the separate lifecycle, port, shim, crash, and performance evidence it requires.
acceptance_criteria: A packaged typed Ruby watcher discovers and validates relevant server/client build inputs; unchanged polls do not compile; bursts debounce; only affected targets rebuild; shared inputs rebuild both once; failures are reported and later changes recover; standalone watch compiles once; hxruby:dev and hxruby:start:watch build once then run Rails plus one watcher without duplicate builds; generated app Procfile/bin/docs use the ergonomic runner; todoapp dogfood avoids duplicate initial compile and debounces changes; focused generator/gem/watcher tests, full gates, exact-SHA CI, immutable release, and closure no-release pass.
notes: Implemented and released the bounded development loop on 2026-07-18. Packaged HXRuby::DevelopmentWatcher provides target-directed HXML/include/classpath/transitive Lix resolver discovery, one initial build, idle snapshots, debounce/coalescing, affected-target scheduling, shared-target rebuilds, and recoverable failures. Canonical hxruby:dev and coordinated hxruby:watch:all preserve aliases, remove duplicate initial builds/two-watcher layout, update generated Procfile/bin/AGENTS/docs, and dogfood rake todoapp:dev. Focused 9-run/73-assertion watcher evidence, complete npm test, all 32 examples, generated app/todoapp contracts, mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime, Playwright, production, packages, formatter, security, exact-SHA canonical workflow 29663936094, and immutable v1.10.1 asset/provenance verification all pass. Closure is carried by the non-releasing evidence/bead commit whose canonical run must remain green.
close_reason: Implemented, fully verified locally and in exact-SHA canonical CI, published as immutable v1.10.1, and independently verified from downloaded release bytes.

title: [ARCH-001] Validate structural Ruby lowering plans
description: GitHub issue #20 correctly identifies that RubyHx already has a TypedExpr to RubyAST pipeline, but ordinary Ruby syntax, callable decisions, and runtime-helper intent still lose structure before printing. Implement the bounded acceptance slice without creating a universal IR or combining it with Rails extraction.
design: Inventory every raw and print-reembed source site with category ownership and executable evidence. Reuse RubyIndex, RubyConditional, and RubyBegin; add statement sequences, member access, and Ruby case nodes. Compose RubyCallableShape, RubyBlockSemantics, and RubyKeywordSemantics into one per-method validated plan. Represent every hxruby helper call with a closed helper and semantic-intent pair validated before printing. Preserve generated output and keep remaining Rails and syntax extraction debt explicitly inventoried.
acceptance_criteria: Checked inventory is current; TArray, expression TIf, and expression TBlock are structural; switch/case is structural with runtime and snapshot parity; owned block/keyword lowering uses one validated typed callable plan; hxruby calls carry validated typed runtime intent; migrated paths have no raw or print-reembed detours; core, callable, profile, Rails, unsupported-expression, package, security, browser, production, exact-SHA CI, immutable release, and closure no-release evidence pass; docs explain boundaries; bead and GitHub issue close only after evidence.
notes: Implemented and released the bounded issue #20 slice on 2026-07-18. The checked inventory now owns 322 raw/print-reembed sites with exactly one final print boundary. Structural TArray/TIf/TBlock/TSwitch/enum access, RubyAST validation, one owned-method callable plan, and closed hxruby helper intent all pass focused runtime, diagnostics, snapshot, profile, and ABI evidence. The route-parity refresh is exactly 41 indentation-only replacements with no token or behavior change. Complete npm test, all 32 examples, mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime, Playwright, production, package, formatter, dependency/security audits, and exact-SHA canonical workflow 29663936094 passed. Immutable v1.10.1 was published from 639701ae779764573633970d14b694314a20ca4b by release job 88134002479; all four downloaded assets, sidecars, complete 712/334-entry manifests, provenance, and a fresh byte-identical local rebuild verify. Closure is carried by the non-releasing evidence/bead commit whose canonical run must remain green.
close_reason: Implemented, fully verified locally and in exact-SHA canonical CI, published as immutable v1.10.1, and independently verified from downloaded release bytes.

This is a bounded supported-surface audit, not a whole-Haxe-stdlib, whole-Ruby-stdlib, or combinatorial-version requirement.
acceptance_criteria: A machine-readable audit covers every marketed RailsHx component family; a CI guard verifies evidence commands/files, mandatory-suite wiring, and rejects supported RailsHx roots importing unfinished Haxe std parity candidates; readiness docs state the dependency-closure rule; focused/full relevant tests pass; any discovered missing required dependency is fixed or the affected claim is narrowed before closure.
notes:

title: Prove RailsHx runtime compatibility on Rails 8.1 before stable promotion
description: RailsHx remains production-ready beta on the exact Rails 7.2.3.1 evidence lane. Migrate the reference and generated runtime contract to a maintained Rails 8.1 patch before advertising RailsHx as stable or Rails 8 compatible.
design: Use one representative current Rails 8.1 runtime lane rather than a Rails/Ruby version cross-product. Generated Gemfiles keep only the real Rails >= 7.0 floor and do not impose an artificial future-major ceiling. The exact locked Rails 8.1 patch is the verified evidence lane; other Rails versions remain permitted with doctor warnings. Add no Rails-version-specific compiler behavior unless an executable compatibility failure requires it.
acceptance_criteria: Generated Gemfiles and locks intentionally admit Rails 8.1; the full supported Ruby matrix passes compiler/package and mandatory Rails runtime lanes; representative browser and production lanes pass; support_matrix.json, doctor diagnostics, README/docs, upstream support dates, and release contracts agree; exact-head canonical CI is green before RailsHx leaves beta.
notes:
Reconciled the Oracle recommendation with project context and owner preference: the matrix records evidence, not a claim that generated Ruby/Rails is incompatible elsewhere. Scope is the existing reference/runtime/browser/production suite on Rails 8.1.3, plus aligned docs and diagnostics; avoid duplicate fixtures and speculative compatibility machinery.
Implementation candidate upgrades the maintained reference and focused Rails runtime fixtures to Rails 8.1.3, removes only the artificial < 8.0 Bundler ceiling, and records Rails 8.1 as the supported beta line while leaving other Rails lines permitted with doctor warnings. No Rails-version compiler/runtime branch or compatibility shim was needed. Local evidence on Node 22.23.1 and Ruby 3.3.11: full npm test green; mandatory Rails runtime suite green; Chromium Playwright 15/15; production Rails tests 26/26 plus Zeitwerk/assets/archive green; Haxelib and gem consumers green; Ruby advisory scan reports the updated lock clean. A separate attempt under the developer machine's Ruby 3.4.10 reached its known global Psych 5.1.2/5.2.2 collision in unbundled Rails generators; canonical clean CI still must prove complete Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0 lanes at the exact commit before closure.
Exact-head canonical CI run 29337644863 completed successfully for 29c26a1f8109ef2cfb3a001783ce0b6bcb390eea. Mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime lanes passed on Ruby 3.3, 3.4, and 4.0; all three full compiler/package suites, Chromium, production dogfood, security, formatting, Node bounds, release contracts, and final publication passed. Immutable v0.7.0 resolves to the exact source SHA with four expected assets. Downloaded gem and ZIP SHA-256 values are 9b9a15ed41628b5291fbef6491cfadb9bc766fc245ee2b8bdec89756d3fe72bd and c8b280afedda488820a21264de6586413dc5891906ac6140fa3d8e152b6569bf; their sidecars bind version, tag, byte size, digest, and sourceSha to the same release.
close_reason: RailsHx now supports the Rails 8.1 beta line, exercised at Rails 8.1.3 across the complete canonical Ruby matrix, browser, production, package, and release gates; future Rails lines remain permitted with warnings rather than artificial hard ceilings. Immutable v0.7.0 is bound to the green exact SHA.

title: [RHX-1.0-009] Reject generator output symlink escapes
description: RailsHx generator containment is lexical. A manifest-owned output path replaced by a symlink can make Common.write_file follow the link and overwrite a writable file outside the app root.
acceptance_criteria: Shared generator writes, route extern writes, manifest recording, and cleanup reject output paths whose canonical existing target or ancestor escapes the declared root; regression tests cover owned and forced symlink writes plus safe in-root generation; diagnostics are actionable; the security and ownership docs record the boundary.
notes:
Temporary reproduction confirmed a normal manifest-owned Common.write_file followed a symlink and changed a sibling target outside the app root. The implementation now canonicalizes existing ancestors, rejects output symlink leaves even with force, uses no-follow writes, protects manifest and atomic route/client rewrites, and prevalidates all clean targets. Focused generator, Rails generator, route, gem, Haxelib, and release-contract gates are green; full/canonical verification remains.
Verification: npm test passed end to end after the shared containment fix. The final fail-before-manifest-mutation adjustment then passed test:generator-common (9 runs, 26 assertions), rails app and route generator gates, isolated gem and Haxelib package consumers, release contracts, Ruby syntax, and diff checks. Canonical main will be verified on the pushed commit.
close_reason: Closed the reproduced outside-root symlink write: shared generator, manifest, clean, route extern, and client rewrite paths now canonicalize containment and reject symlink leaves, with focused and full regression coverage.

title: [RHX-1.0-008] Publish stable maintenance, support, and companion ownership policy
description: Name release, security, compatibility, dependency, triage, documentation, and companion ownership, channels, support sunsets, and sustainable best-effort expectations.
acceptance_criteria: Public support and maintenance docs name roles and channels plus primary and backup ownership or single-maintainer risk; release and security runbooks and the compatibility decision process are explicit; dependency and version review cadence and companion routing are documented.
notes:
Live checkout confirms no project-owned CODEOWNERS, CONTRIBUTING, GOVERNANCE, MAINTAINERS, or SUPPORT document. Acceptance may truthfully disclose a single-maintainer best-effort model; do not invent backup staffing or response commitments.
Reconciliation narrows the external finding: exact-SHA release authority, existing-tag repair, private security intake, machine support dates, weekly Dependabot, CI gates, and contributor workflow already exist. The missing contract is public ownership and routing, not new governance machinery. Implemented one root SUPPORT.md naming Marcelo Serpa/@fullofcaffeine as the current sole maintainer, explicitly disclosing no backup or SLA, routing public/security/core/companion/upstream/generated-app responsibilities, and linking the existing normative release/security/version docs. README, docs index, development guide, SECURITY.md, production-readiness docs, and release contracts now point to and guard that policy. Focused support-matrix, release workflow/hosting/artifact contracts, npm audit, link existence, diff, and bead lint checks pass.
Exact-head canonical CI run 29323933724 completed successfully for 86f0c6c71954555630713ea497308106650f1a55. Every security/compiler/package/Ruby/Rails/browser/production/release-contract lane passed. The final release job found one docs commit, explicitly reported no release, and left immutable v0.5.1 as the latest tag, proving the support-policy change did not create artifact churn.
close_reason: Published and contract-tested a truthful best-effort single-maintainer support model with public/private intake, compatibility and dependency cadence, release/security authority, and core/companion/generated-app routing. Exact canonical run 29323933724 is green with a verified release no-op.

title: [RHX-1.0-007] Add Ruby advisory CI and a usable security disclosure policy
description: Scan locked Ruby dependencies, publish concrete private reporting and supported-security-version rules, and correct stale security documentation.
acceptance_criteria: Mandatory CI detects a known vulnerable Ruby lock fixture; high or critical npm and Ruby findings fail except reviewed expiring exceptions; a private channel and response, disclosure, and emergency ownership are documented; SECURITY.md paths are current.
notes:
Live verification on 2026-07-13: GitHub private vulnerability reporting is disabled and Dependabot security updates are disabled. SECURITY.md still names removed .github/workflows/security-gitleaks.yml, while the real pinned full-history gitleaks lane lives in ci.yml. Preserve gitleaks as positive evidence; add the missing Ruby advisory and actionable private-reporting operations.
Live reconciliation on 2026-07-14 confirms the gap is actionable: bundler-audit 0.9.3 with ruby-advisory-db commit 2a0988f85887d684adeb9ea002792533d862bf0f reports vulnerable versions in the sole committed Rails Gemfile.lock. Remediate the lock before gating it; keep the implementation to one pinned scanner, one known-vulnerable fixture, private GitHub reporting, and current SECURITY.md guidance.
Implementation verified locally on 2026-07-14: enabled GitHub private vulnerability reporting; pinned bundler-audit 0.9.3 and Ruby advisory DB update in canonical security CI; added a known-vulnerable lock fixture proving detection; added weekly Bundler updates for the committed reference app; corrected SECURITY.md channels, paths, supported-version wording, and best-effort single-maintainer scope. Remediated the actual lock findings to concurrent-ruby 1.3.7, crass 1.0.7, net-imap 0.6.4.1, nokogiri 1.19.4, sqlite3 2.9.5, and websocket-driver 0.8.2. Local scanner reports the tracked lock clean and the fixture vulnerable; full Rails runtime, scaffold CLI, production smoke (26 tests/217 assertions), Chromium Playwright (15/15), release contracts, npm audit, and bd lint pass. Exact-head canonical CI remains required before closure.
Exact-head canonical CI run 29319922528 completed successfully for fe6035e861ca221bdae4e8fcefa55c13a3c9702b. The mandatory security job logged bundler-audit 0.9.3, one clean tracked lock, successful detection of the vulnerable fixture, and no Gitleaks findings. All compiler/package, Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0, Rails runtime, browser, production, and release-contract jobs passed. Immutable v0.5.1 was published with the exact tag SHA and four expected digest-bound assets. GitHub private vulnerability reporting remains enabled.
close_reason: Ruby advisory scanning now gates exact-SHA publication, the committed Rails lock is clean, the detection fixture proves the scanner, private reporting is enabled and documented, weekly Bundler updates are configured, and canonical run 29319922528 published immutable v0.5.1.

title: [RHX-1.0-006] Add a maintained Haxe-first pure-Ruby reference project
description: Prove one cohesive non-Rails Haxe-first library and CLI lifecycle through the exact current package candidate, typed filesystem/JSON/error behavior, Haxe and Ruby callers, executable tests, and normal user guidance. Reuse existing compiler, stdlib, gem-interop, and package-install foundations; public old-release upgrade/rollback stays with haxe_ruby-i1g.
acceptance_criteria: A cohesive multi-file non-Rails library plus CLI keeps its domain and CLI implementation in typed Haxe; covers filesystem input, typed JSON output, explicit usage/missing-file errors, Haxe-origin and handwritten Ruby-origin behavior, and normal edit/compile/run/test guidance; compiles and runs on the supported Ruby matrix; and is consumed through the exact current candidate Haxelib ZIP installed into an isolated repository. One negative compiler contract owns a meaningful typed boundary. Immutable old-release upgrade and rollback remain owned by haxe_ruby-i1g rather than duplicated here.
notes:
Do not rebuild existing foundations: ruby_callable_abi already exercises a typed Tempfile boundary plus handwritten Ruby caller, and the Haxelib package gate installs a current archive into an isolated repository and runs an external hello-world consumer. The missing deliverable is one cohesive maintained non-Rails product lifecycle using those pieces.
Scope reconciliation: do not duplicate immutable-old-release download and rollback machinery here; haxe_ruby-i1g owns that. This workflow should compile through the current candidate Haxelib ZIP installed into an isolated repository, because that tests the exact code being released rather than an older hosted version. Reuse existing callable ABI, stdlib, package-install, and gem-interoperability foundations. Add only one cohesive multi-file non-Rails library/CLI lifecycle with typed domain logic, filesystem/JSON output, errors, Ruby-origin tests, generated artifact packaging/consumption, and the normal edit/compile/run commands.
Implemented examples/rubyhx_cli as a typed multi-file text-report library/CLI. Focused evidence passes: npm run test:rubyhx-cli covers filesystem-to-JSON behavior, usage/missing-file exit codes, all generated Ruby syntax, handwritten Ruby consumption, and an Int-to-String negative compile diagnostic; npm run test:examples-compile compiles all 31 examples; npm run test:haxelib-package installs the candidate ZIP in an isolated haxelib repo, compiles external copied CLI source through -lib reflaxe.ruby, and runs the generated CLI. Public wording now limits the maintained framework-independent workflow to library/CLI; service-shaped programs remain compiler foundations rather than a claimed reference workflow.
Final local verification: release contracts, all 31 example compiles, the new focused CLI contract, runtime Minitest (15 runs/151 assertions), generator common (9 runs/26 assertions), Rails app/adopt/YARD generators, Rails integration/interop, todoapp static smoke, snapshots, inventories, candidate Haxelib consumer, and gem consumer pass. The Ruby 3.4.10 installation on this workstation has a global psych 5.1.2/default 5.2.2 conflict; framework-independent Minitests now opt out of unrelated global plugins, and the Rails-backed generator plus remaining suffix pass under supported Ruby 3.3.11. Clean exact-SHA CI remains the authoritative 3.3/3.4/4.0 matrix proof before closure.
Exact-head canonical CI run 29329894570 completed successfully for 8b013d4e5914478ea442bae9b756fe2d77e2fdf8. All three Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0 full suites, mandatory Rails runtime lanes, Chromium, production dogfood, security, formatter, Node boundary, release-contract, and publication jobs passed. Immutable latest release v0.6.0 points its exact tag at that SHA; the Haxelib ZIP and hxruby gem plus both checksum sidecars are uploaded, and the downloaded sidecars bind version/tag/sourceSha/size/digest to the same commit and hosted bytes.
close_reason: Added and documented a cohesive typed Haxe library/CLI, exercised Haxe and handwritten Ruby callers, negative/error paths, and isolated current-candidate package consumption across the exact canonical Ruby matrix; immutable v0.6.0 is bound to the green source SHA.

title: [RHX-1.0-005] Define the 1.x public surface and prove upgrade/rollback
description: Define a sustainable 1.x public/internal and SemVer boundary using the repository's existing machine-checked inventories; fail closed on unsupported ownership-manifest state and local generated-output drift; prove representative upgrade and rollback from immutable public v0.4.0 ZIP/gem assets to artifacts built from the current Git tree.
design: Keep the stable contract federated: document which existing machine-checked inventories own packages, profiles, metadata, std APIs, callable/runtime ABI, tasks, compatibility, and ownership schemas instead of duplicating them into a second giant registry. Manifest schema v1 is the only historical schema, so reject unknown/malformed versions before mutation and defer a migration engine until a real v2 exists. Treat generated files as disposable output while refusing normal overwrite/clean when their recorded checksum drifted; existing --force remains the explicit overwrite path. Add one canonical, checksum-verified public v0.4.0 ZIP/gem upgrade-and-rollback rehearsal against the current Git-tree artifacts, not a version cross-product.
acceptance_criteria: A concise 1.x contract classifies public versus internal surfaces by pointing to the repository's existing machine-checked inventories; manifest readers and doctor reject missing, malformed, or unknown versions before writes/deletes; normal rewrite and clean preflight checksum drift without partial mutation while explicit generator --force can replace a changed output; one canonical CI lane downloads and checksum-verifies the immutable v0.4.0 ZIP and gem through public release URLs, upgrades representative Haxelib and ownership-manifest consumers to current Git-tree artifacts, rolls both back, and proves handwritten source remains unchanged. No generic migration engine or version-matrix cross-product is added before a real schema transition needs one.
notes:
Reconciled Oracle scope with live evidence and maintainer direction. The v0.4.0 public Haxelib ZIP compiles/runs its packaged hello-world source, upgrades to a current staged package, and rolls back locally. The v0.4.0 and current gems also round-trip the same v1 ownership manifest without changing handwritten source. Missing work is to encode that public-path rehearsal in CI, enforce schema/checksum mutation boundaries, and publish the lean SemVer classification. A general migrator, backup subsystem, diagnostic-code conversion, and monolithic registry would add machinery without a present schema transition or user failure.
Implemented locally: docs/public-contract.md classifies the 1.x boundary through existing machine-checked owners rather than duplicating them; root AGENTS now preserves forward-permissive Ruby/Rails compatibility and avoids unsupported CI cross-products. Manifest v1 validation rejects missing/malformed/unknown versions, duplicate/unsafe entries, and invalid checksums before mutation. Normal overwrite and all clean operations preflight recorded checksums; generator --force remains the deliberate replacement path. npm run test:public-upgrade downloads and verifies immutable v0.4.0 ZIP/gem bytes, upgrades representative Haxelib and v1 ownership consumers to current Git-tree artifacts, rolls back, and checks handwritten source. Local green: 15/52 generator common assertions, public upgrade rehearsal, release contracts/reproducibility, workflow contract, YAML/Node/Ruby syntax, Rails generator/app/adopt and routes/migration/model/controller/scaffold/template gates on Ruby 3.3. The Rails generator attempt on the developer Ruby 3.4 install hit an unrelated pre-existing psych 5.1.2/5.2.2 activation conflict; the same gates passed on clean Ruby 3.3 and canonical CI owns clean 3.4 evidence.
Canonical
close_reason: Published and canonically verified the federated 1.x contract plus immutable v0.4.0 public-path upgrade/rollback; exact-SHA run 29355887263 is green and immutable v0.8.1 is bound to c27168034cbf3348ab8a1bff0f7623a0c251a638.

title: [RHX-1.0-004] Prove source-correlated debugging and production failure workflows
description: Choose and exercise the supported error-to-source strategy for compiler, generated Ruby, Rails request, job and view, browser, and production asset failures.
acceptance_criteria: Maintained scenarios locate owned Haxe or HHX, or explicitly supported generated source; exception causes and backtraces remain useful; doctor or check reports version and provenance; docs state Ruby knowledge and source-map limits.
notes:
Existing foundations were undercounted by the external review: JS source maps, readable generated files, manifest output/source/SHA provenance, and doctor/check diagnostics exist. Manifest sources are generally generator-level rather than Haxe/HHX file-line mappings, HxException has no mapping layer, and required failure journeys remain absent.
Scope decision: keep the 1.0 debugging contract lean. Server-side Ruby/Rails failures use deterministic generated Ruby/ERB as the supported line-level debugging source, with ownership-manifest provenance back to the generating Haxe/RailsHx surface. Browser builds retain Haxe source maps. Do not add a Ruby source-map subsystem or pretend haxe.CallStack parity exists; add focused executable checks for backtrace/cause/provenance and document the boundary.
Implemented bounded debugging contract: generated Ruby/ERB is the supported server line-level source; Haxe compiler diagnostics own Haxe/HHX positions; client HXML retains source maps. Doctor now reports hxruby/tool versions, generated roots, and generator-level manifest provenance. Exception-flow smoke asserts an uncaught generated-Ruby frame; runtime minitest preserves native cause and Ruby raise site; generated app guidance and docs state limits. Focused exception/runtime/generator/release-contract tests pass; exact committed package and canonical CI evidence pending.
close_reason: Closed by c8af43bdb246785f873906c502aed53fb5df6852. Generated Ruby/ERB is the documented server line-level debugging source; browser builds retain Haxe source maps; doctor reports installed/tool versions, generated roots, and manifest provenance; executable tests preserve generated exception frames plus native cause/backtrace behavior. Local Node 22.23.1/Ruby 3.3.11 npm test and mandatory Rails runtime passed. Exact canonical run 29346906907 passed every Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0 full and Rails 8.1 runtime lane plus browser, production, security, package, and release jobs. Immutable v0.8.0 tag/assets/sidecars bind to the same SHA and downloaded digests match.

title: [RHX-1.0-003] Add stable compile/runtime/resource baselines and regression policy
description: Measure representative cold and incremental compile time, generated artifact size, Rails boot, request and job behavior, memory and allocations, and client output on a pinned runner.
design: Keep this lean: one Node command measures three representative compiler workloads (RubyHx CLI, RailsHx server/HHX, RailsHx client) plus generated CLI startup across three samples on the pinned Ubuntu CI lane. Record wall time, peak child RSS where the host time tool supports it, and generated bytes/files. Use generous absolute gross-regression caps; do not fail on small noisy timing deltas, add a benchmark service, duplicate request/job correctness tests, or make comparative speed claims. Existing Rails runtime/browser/production lanes remain the behavioral proof.
acceptance_criteria: A documented npm command emits human and machine-readable results for three repeated samples of representative RubyHx/RailsHx compiler and generated-startup workloads; pinned canonical CI runs it and blocks only generous absolute time/memory/output runaway caps; exact-SHA Rails runtime/browser/production jobs remain the behavioral evidence; public docs make no comparative speed claim and explain that small timing variance is non-blocking evidence.
notes:
Reconciled Oracle scope against the live tree: request/job/browser/production behavior is already covered by mandatory exact-SHA jobs. The missing product evidence is repeatable resource measurement, so this bead owns only a small viability baseline and gross-regression policy.
Canonical exact-SHA evidence: GitHub Actions run 29352535061 completed success for 75920cd5891642342f7ce12ae44c53d71b21db01 across all 14 jobs, including the new benchmark step in RailsHx production dogfood. Ubuntu 24.04 three-sample medians: RubyHx CLI compile 3273.3 ms / 154088 KiB / 55151 bytes / 81 files; RailsHx server compile 7840.3 ms / 177912 KiB / 61789 bytes / 33 files; RailsHx client compile 765.8 ms / 81628 KiB / 22645 bytes / 12 files; generated CLI startup 90.2 ms / 31660 KiB; Rails production boot 1433.4 ms median / 96748 KiB with a 1389.7-3130.4 ms range. No cap violations. The cold-boot spread confirms the decision not to add tight relative hosted-runner gates. The final exact-SHA release job passed and correctly left immutable v0.8.0 as the newest release for this chore-only change.
close_reason: Lean stable viability benchmark is documented, locally exercised, and green in exact-SHA canonical run 29352535061; broad caps passed and no comparative claim or benchmark service was added.

title: [RHX-1.0-002] Extract DeviseHx lowering behind a generic companion contract
description: Remove companion-specific type paths, metadata schemas, helper owners, HHX tags, route IR, and diagnostics from RubyHx/RailsHx compiler and std core while preserving DeviseHx behavior in the companion layer.
design: Keep this deliberately small: add no registry or plugin loader. Generalize only the existing handoffs into (1) a validated metadata-driven Ruby expression carrier for compiler-erased companion macros, (2) generic Rails controller filter/required-filter and test-include metadata, (3) a safe generic Rails class-macro carrier, and (4) one opaque typed route-extension declaration carrying validated Ruby plus manifest JSON. DeviseHx owns its scope/schema/module/params/HHX validation and expands to those carriers. Remove the four Devise-only HHX tags in favor of ordinary typed link_to/button_to plus AuthLinks. Preserve generated Ruby and route manifest semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Core compiler and std/rails sources contain no DeviseHx/Devise-specific package, schema, helper, tag, route-IR, or diagnostic vocabulary; a static guard enforces that boundary; a small fake companion exercises the generic carriers; existing DeviseHx compiler, negative, route, package, Rails runtime, browser, production, and todoapp gates pass with only intentional Haxe authoring changes; core compiles/packages without loading DeviseHx. No registry, plugin loader, or speculative extension lifecycle is added.
notes:
Reconciliation nuance: the source guard must target DeviseHx package paths, schemas, helper owners, tags, and diagnostics. Generic Rails route IR and ordinary devise_for emission may remain when they no longer name or require DeviseHx; do not ban Rails syntax by substring.
Live reconciliation corrected the review nuance: RailsRouteDecl/Extractor/Emitter/Manifest and rails.routing.RouteDecl were themselves Devise-shaped, so ordinary devise_for emission is not generic merely because it is valid Rails. This slice will make that existing route handoff opaque/generic while the DeviseHx macro continues to emit the same Rails line and manifest semantics.
Implemented the bounded extraction without a registry/plugin loader. Core now owns only generic checked expression, Rails filter/required-filter, class-macro, test-include, and opaque route-extension carriers; DeviseHx owns its scope/schema/module/params/HHX/route validation. The source guard rejects actual companion coupling while self-testing that ordinary Rails "devise_for :users" remains allowed. A fake Audit companion proves the generic expression/filter handoff is not Devise-shaped. Generated UserAuth contracts remain @:rubyNoEmit and use generic Rails metadata but carry no @:rubyAllowRaw or @:rubyExtensionExpr; raw authority is confined to the companion's internal RubyFragments facade. Reconciliation also found a gap beyond the review: Auth.signIn accepted options but discarded them. The companion now emits checked Devise force/store keywords, rejects unknown/dynamic bags, and keeps bypass behavior on explicit bypassSignIn. Local green: test:devisehx-core, test:devisehx-controller, test:routes-dsl, test:rails-adopt-generator, test:strict-boundaries, test:snapshots, test:todoapp-rails, compiler metadata docs, stdlib inventory, release contracts, Haxe format, and static syntax/policy checks. Package tests must be rerun after commit because they deliberately build the exact Git tree.
Post-commit exact-tree verification: Haxelib consumer passed with 662 packaged files and gem consumer passed. The aggregate npm test passed every gate through Rails adopt/YARD generators, then the developer Ruby 3.4.10 install hit its pre-existing psych 5.1.2/5.2.2 activation conflict in test:rails-generators. Under installed supported Ruby 3.3.11, rails-generators plus the entire remaining suffix (strict boundaries, SQL policy, snapshots, inventories, gap report, Haxelib package, gem package) passed. Working tree is clean at 3617c0911a94f58ae42a9afe073d526d435cfadd; clean canonical Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0 and Rails 8.1 evidence remains required before closure.
Canonical closure evidence: exact-head GitHub Actions run 29363805879 completed successfully for fceb8d35767d711ba3d7fd86c504e69d4b23d1b4. Node minimum/latest, security, formatter, release contracts, production benchmark/dogfood, Chromium, all Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0 full compiler/package suites, and mandatory Rails 8.1 runtime lanes passed. The exact-SHA final job published immutable v0.9.0; its tag resolves to the same SHA and the release contains exactly the Haxelib ZIP, hxruby gem, and both SHA-256 sidecars.
close_reason: DeviseHx package/schema/helper/tag/route knowledge now lives in the companion behind bounded generic carriers; core source guard and fake companion prove the boundary, all exact-SHA Ruby/Rails/browser/production/package gates are green, and immutable v0.9.0 is bound to the tested commit.

title: [RHX-1.0-001] Define and continuously test the RubyHx/RailsHx stable support matrix
description: Replace EOL or aspirational version wording with an exact Haxe, Ruby, Rails, Node, platform, adapter, browser, and package matrix plus support sunsets.
acceptance_criteria: README, docs, manifests, workflows, and generated Gemfiles agree; Ruby 3.2 is removed; Ruby 3.4 is tested; Rails 8.1 is runtime-tested or RailsHx stays beta; all claimed support pairs pass exact-RC compiler, runtime, and package lanes; versions below required tool/runtime minimums fail clearly while future or otherwise unverified Ruby/Rails environments warn without being declared incompatible.
notes:
Documentation slice completed: compatibility and production-readiness docs now state the current Rails beta runtime contract as >= 7.0 and < 8.0, identify the locked 7.2.3.1 reference version, and classify Rails 8.1 as planned but unsupported until the required runtime matrix passes. Release contracts bind that wording to every current Rails materializer. The broader stable-matrix acceptance remains open.
Follow-up reconciliation narrowed the Rails statement further: >= 7.0 and < 8.0 is only the accepted fixture dependency range. The current runtime evidence is Rails 7.2.3.1; other Rails 7 minors are not support claims without their own lanes. Root AGENTS.md now requires this review-versus-evidence distinction for all external reviews.
Live reconciliation confirms the accepted Rails Gemfile range is not a support matrix: only locked Rails 7.2.3.1 has current runtime evidence. No additional matrix claim should be inferred from Bundler resolution constraints.
Implementation candidate 5f618ec adds the packaged machine support manifest and fail-closed doctor diagnostics; aligns README/PRD/docs, gem requirements, generated runtime constraints, release pins, and CI; removes Ruby 3.2; adds Ruby 3.4; and tests Node 22.14.0 plus 22.23.1. Local exact-tree evidence on Ruby 3.4.10/Node 22.23.1: full npm test green; isolated Haxelib consumer shipped 649 files; isolated gem consumer and packaged doctor/tasks green; mandatory Rails 7.2.3.1 runtime seams green; Chromium Playwright 15/15; production Rails tests 26/26 plus Zeitwerk/assets/archive green; npm audit reports zero vulnerabilities. RailsHx remains beta on exact Rails 7.2.3.1 evidence; planned Rails 8.1 promotion is now owned by open bead haxe_ruby-nho0 rather than inferred from this matrix task. Exact-head canonical CI remains pending before closure.
Compatibility policy refined from the external review after local reconciliation: tested Ruby/Rails lanes are evidence and maintenance promises, not upper compatibility ceilings. The hxruby gem now requires only Ruby >= 3.3; future Ruby branches and non-MRI engines are permitted with doctor warnings. Loaded Rails versions outside the exact 7.2.3.1 beta evidence lane also warn rather than hard-fail. Haxe remains exact and Node remains within the tested 22.x build-tool range. This keeps Rails 8.1 promotion tracked by haxe_ruby-nho0 without claiming current generated Rails code is incompatible.
Canonical CI runs 29312719561 (69b5753) and 29314975749 (48a23c3) passed every compiler, Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0, Rails runtime, browser, production, security, formatter, Node-boundary, and release-contract lane, then exposed a release-only pin mismatch: Ruby 3.4.10 ships RubyGems 3.6.9 and ruby/setup-ruby correctly refused to downgrade it to 3.5.22. Follow-up fixes publication and repair to exact RubyGems 3.6.9, records that pin in the machine manifest, and binds it through support/release contract tests.
Exact-head canonical CI run 29317365130 completed successfully for afdac5a32d3a5bb15ca47295dec80aaf9e77eeb4. All compiler, Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0, Rails runtime, browser, production, security, formatter, Node-boundary, release-contract, and final publication jobs passed. The corrected RubyGems 3.6.9 release path published immutable v0.5.0; its tag resolves to the exact tested SHA and the expected ZIP/gem plus SHA-256 sidecars are present.
close_reason: Machine support/evidence matrix, warning-based forward compatibility policy, docs/packages/CI alignment, and exact-SHA v0.5.0 publication are proven. RailsHx remains beta on Rails 7.2.3.1 evidence; Rails 8.1 promotion remains tracked by haxe_ruby-nho0.

title: Close RubyHx/RailsHx stable 1.0 review blockers
description: Own the bounded P1 findings from docs/reviews/rubyhx-railshx-1.0-readiness-review.md before requesting stable-major approval.
acceptance_criteria: All RHX-1.0-001 through RHX-1.0-009 are closed or removed from public stable scope with the acceptance evidence named in the review; an independent exact-RC rerun finds no P0/P1; approvedStableMajors remains unchanged until that rerun.
notes:
Maintainer reconciliation rule: the independent Oracle review is an evidence source, not authority. Validate every finding against the live tree, canonical CI, hosted settings, published artifacts, and current primary sources; record contradictions, narrowed claims, and newly discovered gaps before closing each child.
Maintainer reconciliation against live code, hosted settings, package-consumer gates, and immutable v0.4.0 assets is recorded in the review. Findings remain P1 but now preserve existing foundations and narrow acceptance to actual missing proof; see each child bead notes.
All RHX-1.0-001 through RHX-1.0-009 findings are closed with their bounded evidence. Fresh independent GPT-5.6 Pro review of the exact post-blocker archive found no P0/P1, and maintainer reconciliation confirmed the result against the live tree, official upstream state, canonical runs 29366269417/29367890473, immutable v0.9.0, and focused local gates. The project is ready to request a separate stable-major approval; this epic closure does not grant it. approvedStableMajors remains empty.
close_reason: All nine P1 findings closed and the exact post-blocker independent review plus maintainer reconciliation found no new P0/P1; stable-major approval is now a separate decision.

title: Run independent RubyHx/RailsHx stable 1.0 evidence review
description: Execute the committed GPT 5.6 Pro review packet against an exact clean main commit after the product thesis/readiness documentation lands. Independently verify goals, market claims, architecture, compiler/runtime semantics, Ruby/Rails interop, authoring UX, gradual adoption, full-stack sharing, tests, security, performance, debugging, packaging/upgrades, docs, and maintenance rather than treating existing beta-readiness language as proof.
design: Use docs/rubyhx-railshx-gpt56-1.0-review.md in a fresh independent context. Do not implement fixes during the first pass. Produce docs/reviews/rubyhx-railshx-1.0-readiness-review.md with commit/toolchain/CI evidence, product and architecture assessment, public surface inventory, claim-evidence matrix, 15-dimension scorecard, severity-ranked findings, stable support proposal, SemVer/deprecation/upgrade recommendation, and measurable release path. Create or update one bounded bead per accepted actionable finding.
acceptance_criteria: The exact reviewed commit and environment are recorded; all 15 stable-readiness dimensions have PROVEN/PARTIAL/MISSING/OUTSIDE-SCOPE status with evidence; marketed claims have an allowed-wording evidence matrix; P0/P1/P2/P3 findings have bounded outcomes and acceptance gates; actual commands versus merely existing tests are distinguished; current canonical-main CI/release state is verified; the report and bead mutations are committed and pushed; no stable 1.0 claim is made unless the report supports requesting stable-major approval.
notes: Independent review completed against 08faba040457165b883ae5327315581979ea07db. Verdict: NOT READY: P1 STABLE-RELEASE BLOCKERS. Commit-ready report: docs/reviews/rubyhx-railshx-1.0-readiness-review.md. No P0 found; bounded P1 and P2 findings are tracked under haxe_ruby-1sd and linked follow-ups.
close_reason: Independent exact-commit review was recorded in docs/reviews/rubyhx-railshx-1.0-readiness-review.md and pushed at a4eefd5. Verdict and bounded P1/P2 findings are tracked under haxe_ruby-1sd. Stable-major approval remains blocked.

title: Define RubyHx/RailsHx 1.0 product thesis and deep readiness review
description: The repository documents many implemented capabilities and a production-ready beta surface, but it lacks one coherent Ruby-developer product thesis and an evidence-based, cross-dimensional review brief for stable 1.0. Clarify RubyHx versus RailsHx, generated-Ruby ownership, gradual adoption, typed critical-component use, full-stack code sharing, Ruby-adjacent Haxe ergonomics, honest tradeoffs, and the distinction between production-ready beta and stable 1.0.
design: Lead README with outcomes and adoption modes. Add a durable positioning document with claim boundaries and tradeoffs. Expand the production-readiness contract into explicit beta versus stable-1.0 dimensions. Add a GPT 5.6 Pro repository review brief that requires local evidence, architecture and API analysis, a claim-evidence matrix, severity-ranked gaps, measurable exit gates, and bead mutations rather than unsupported readiness opinions. Index and contract-test the documents.
acceptance_criteria: README gives Ruby developers a concise, accurate pitch and honest maturity statement; docs define the product layers, differentiation, gradual adoption modes, full-stack sharing claim, and tradeoffs; production readiness defines cross-dimensional stable-1.0 gates without falsely declaring them satisfied; a reusable GPT 5.6 Pro deep-review brief is committed and indexed; automated documentation contracts cover the new public narrative; relevant and full repository QA pass, changes are committed and pushed, and GitHub checks are green.
notes:
Implemented a concise 193-line README product landing page with a CI-enforced 240-line ceiling; added the durable Why RubyHx thesis, Getting Started, Packages And Installation, Repository Development, project-wide beta-versus-stable readiness dimensions, and the GPT 5.6 Pro evidence-review packet; indexed all docs and moved release/version/package assertions to their owning docs. Corrected the stale Node 20 prose to the actual 22.14.0 contract. Verified final npm test, mandatory Rails runtime, 15-test Chromium Playwright lane, todoapp production lane (26 tests/217 assertions, Zeitwerk, assets, archive), public precommit formatter/Gitleaks, diff check, and changed-doc local links. Follow-up independent review is haxe_ruby-kxv.
Follow-up positioning pass makes Haxe-first authoring a separate first-class path for framework-independent RubyHx and RailsHx: teams may keep nearly all owned source in Haxe/HHX while using generated Ruby, gems, Bundler, Rails, and deployment infrastructure. README, product thesis, onboarding, production-readiness dimensions, docs index, and independent 1.0 review packet now distinguish that claim from Ruby-first gradual adoption and retain the honest Ruby integration/operations/debugging boundary. Removed em-dash punctuation from repository prose and added a public Markdown contract to prevent recurrence. Final local verification: npm test; rake test:rails:runtime; rake todoapp:playwright (15/15); rake todoapp:production (26 runs, 217 assertions, Zeitwerk and release archive); npm run public:precommit; version/release contracts; diff and punctuation checks.
Expanded the product/readiness pass with two precise public contracts: RailsHx HHX is documented as a TSX-like typed server-view authoring layer that emits normal ActionView ERB, and the canonical browser lane is documented as Haxe -js typing plus the pinned Genes custom emitter, split ES modules, and Rails importmap rewrite. Added a Ruby stdlib architecture audit: broad versioned typed ruby.* coverage is the long-term goal, while std/ruby/_std remains the separate Haxe-semantic adapter layer. Typed native contracts should be shared where semantics match, not used to alias Ruby and Haxe APIs. Follow-up epic haxe_ruby-hjm owns the versioned core/stdlib/default-gem inventory, deterministic RBS pipeline, prioritized facades, and raw-native _std reuse audit.
Final extended verification used the supported Node 22.14.0/npm 10.9.2 toolchain: npm test passed with all 30 examples, std/runtime parity, Rails integrations, snapshots, inventories, and Haxelib/gem artifacts; rake test:rails:runtime passed; rake todoapp:playwright passed 15/15; rake todoapp:production passed 26 runs/217 assertions, Zeitwerk, assets, and release archive. Public precommit, release contracts, changed-document local links, README line ceiling, punctuation contract, and diff hygiene also passed.
close_reason: Completed and pushed the RubyHx/RailsHx product thesis, concise README, Haxe-first and Ruby-first positioning, typed HHX view contract, exact Genes client architecture, stable-1.0 evidence-review packet, and Ruby stdlib layering/coverage direction. Tracked independent review as haxe_ruby-kxv and broad typed Ruby stdlib work as haxe_ruby-hjm. Full pinned-toolchain local QA and GitHub CI run 29287421483 are green.

title: Vendor upstream lazy function field metadata fix
description: RubyHx ships and compiles against vendor/reflaxe, but upstream PR SomeRanDev/reflaxe#52 is not present locally. Reflaxe ClassFieldHelper reads ClassField.type directly, so a class loaded through Context.getType() and appended by BaseCompiler.filterTypes() can expose a valid method as TLazy(TFun), lose ClassFuncData, and emit Function information not found. Import the reviewed narrow upstream fix with provenance and prove the actual vendored path locally.
acceptance_criteria: vendor/reflaxe contains behavior equivalent to upstream PR #52 commit 024937acffd242f129265d969a840d3779f02bcd; both function extraction and overload cache identity unwrap only recursive TLazy wrappers without following typedefs/abstracts; a focused local regression fails on the predecessor vendor and passes through Context.getType plus filterTypes; package/release docs record patch provenance and replacement-after-merge policy; release/package contracts ensure the regression and vendor source ship; no Dynamic/cast/raw target escape is introduced; focused gates, npm test, examples, Haxelib/gem, Playwright, production, Rails runtime, public precommit, and GitHub CI pass.
close_reason: Vendored the narrow recursive TLazy function-field fix from Reflaxe PR #52 commit 024937acffd242f129265d969a840d3779f02bcd in ClassFieldHelper for both function extraction and overload cache identity. Added a real Context.getType/filterTypes regression that failed on the predecessor, source/release/package contracts, and complete README/package/vendor provenance plus upstream replacement policy. No Dynamic, cast, or raw target escape was added. Verified npm test (30 examples, snapshots, 639-file haxelib, gem), Todo Playwright 15/15, production 26 runs/217 assertions, mandatory Rails runtime, public precommit/Gitleaks, and GitHub CI run 29233425393 (all 12 jobs success) for commit 3b5092ef912de31e12abd6d6699d07b56e2ac261.

title: Fail closed on unsupported typed Ruby expressions
description: Replace the generic generated nil TODO fallback for unsupported typed expressions with actionable compiler diagnostics, after explicitly handling or documenting every currently reachable shape.
design: Inventory current fallback hits first. Add missing structured lowering where supported, retain explicit compiler-erased cases only with comments, and report the typed expression kind plus source position for everything else.
acceptance_criteria: No supported build emits TODO unsupported expression; negative fixtures prove diagnostics; all compiler-shell, examples, Rails, todoapp, Playwright, snapshot, and package gates remain green; the compiler correctness contract is documented.
close_reason: Replaced the generic typed-expression nil/TODO fallback with an exhaustive TypedExprDef decision: value-capable forms retain explicit lowerings, statement-only TVar/TFor/TWhile/TReturn forms fail if they reach value lowering, and unconsumed TIdent intrinsics fail with kind/name/source-position diagnostics. Removed the inline declaration TODO fallback in favor of an internal compiler error. Added a focused positive statement runtime fixture and a confined negative __unprotect__/TIdent boundary fixture, integrated it into npm test, and documented the compiler correctness/erasure contract in README, docs index, compatibility matrix, and docs/compiler-correctness.md. Verified full npm test, 30 examples, deterministic snapshots, Todo Playwright 15/15, production 26 runs/217 assertions, mandatory Rails runtime, packages, formatting, Gitleaks, no tracked TODO fallback markers, and diff hygiene.

title: Preserve callable ABI through method values and inheritance
description: Preserve block/keyword callable shapes when annotated methods become first-class Haxe function values and when methods are inherited, overridden, invoked through interfaces, or forwarded with super.
design: Emit adapters only for genuine method-value capture, evaluate receivers once, inherit the effective ABI from the declaration chain, and diagnose conflicts. Keep direct calls wrapper-free.
acceptance_criteria: Static/instance method values, side-effecting receivers, overrides, interfaces, super, recursion, modules, and concerns retain the same ABI from every static type; conflicts fail at compile time; generated output and rationale are documented; full QA gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented one effective Ruby callable ABI across method values, inheritance, interfaces, overrides, recursion, modules, concerns, native names, and same-method super forwarding. Added structured positional-to-keyword/block adapters with exactly-once receiver evaluation, fail-closed ABI and cross-method-super diagnostics, executable runtime/Ruby-origin coverage, deterministic snapshots, example coverage, and complete callable ABI/metadata/README documentation. Verified npm test, 15/15 Todo Chromium E2E, Todo production smoke (26 runs/217 assertions), mandatory Rails runtime, public precommit/Gitleaks, formatting, and diff hygiene.

title: Complete Ruby keyword native-name and rest ABI
description: Make @:rubyKwargs and field @:native symmetric for calls and Haxe-owned definitions, and lower final haxe.Rest parameters and spread calls to Ruby rest/splat syntax.
design: Project stored typed keyword carriers through their declared schema with single evaluation and optional-key preservation. Emit real required keywords plus a validated optional-key bucket for owned definitions. Use haxe.Rest where Haxe can express the shape; do not add speculative combined rest/block metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Inline/stored kwargs, optional keys, native predicate/bang/writer names, owned definitions, rest parameters, and spread calls have typed runtime/snapshot/negative coverage; unknown or malformed shapes fail closed; docs and examples are current; full QA gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented symmetric typed Ruby keyword calls/definitions, native method naming, Haxe Rest/splat lowering, nominal model-scoped Rails strong params, fail-closed diagnostics, executable snapshots/runtime/browser coverage, and current README/reference docs. Full npm test matrix, Rails runtime, todoapp Chromium E2E, production smoke, packaging, formatting, and Gitleaks pass.

title: Lower Haxe-authored Ruby block methods symmetrically
description: Make @:rubyBlockArg a symmetric call and definition ABI. Required direct-only callbacks lower to yield; optional or escaping callbacks lower to &block with .call and native forwarding. Preserve Haxe callback-local return semantics.
design: Use conservative escape analysis. Assignment, return, storage, positional passing, forwarding, or nested capture count as escape. Tail-safe inline callbacks become native blocks; callbacks with non-tail return become strict lambdas passed with ampersand. Do not expose Proc or yield as separate author choices.
acceptance_criteria: Static, instance, module, concern, patch, constructor, forwarding, optional, and Ruby-origin block scenarios behave correctly; early/nested returns cannot exit the enclosing Ruby method; output is direct and documented; focused and full QA gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented symmetric Haxe-owned Ruby block definitions with yield/capture analysis, safe inline return lowering, constructor/module/concern/patch/forwarding support, Ruby-origin runtime coverage, exact snapshots, and complete documentation; all local gates pass.

title: Model and validate Ruby callable shapes
description: Add structured Ruby AST nodes for callable signatures, keyword/splat/block arguments, native blocks, and yield. Centralize extraction and validation of @:rubyBlockArg, @:rubyKwargs, and field @:native contracts for extern call sites and Haxe-owned methods.
design: Keep legacy AST variants during migration, but route new callable work through typed nodes and a focused compiler module. Validate metadata arity, placement, parameter kinds/order, duplicate annotations, dynamic methods, and invalid native method names. Add positive printer tests and negative compiler diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: Structured nodes print valid handwritten-style Ruby; the validator is used by owned method compilation and annotated calls; invalid metadata and names fail with actionable positions; canonical metadata and callable-ABI docs explain every invariant; focused and full QA gates pass.
close_reason: Structured callable AST nodes, shared ABI validation, precise ActiveRecord write carriers, diagnostics, documentation, generated artifacts, snapshots, and full QA are complete.

title: Make Ruby callable and method ABI symmetric
description: Unify Ruby method calls and Haxe-owned method definitions behind one typed callable ABI covering native blocks, keyword arguments, native names, method values, constructors, forwarding, and inheritance. Keep Haxe authoring precise and generated Ruby direct, idiomatic, and understandable.
design: Use structured Ruby AST call/signature nodes plus one validated call-shape descriptor. Haxe authors use ordinary typed function and options parameters with documented metadata; the compiler chooses yield versus captured block internally. Implement in small dependency-ordered slices and keep Rails-specific behavior as downstream regression coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Calls and definitions agree for every supported shape; malformed declarations fail with source-positioned diagnostics; no Dynamic/cast/raw Ruby leaks into public APIs; executable Ruby-origin/Haxe-origin tests and snapshots cover the contract; README and canonical compiler documentation stay current; full examples, todoapp, Playwright, package, and Ruby 3.2/3.3/4.0 CI gates are green.
close_reason: Completed the symmetric Ruby callable ABI across structured call/signature AST, native blocks, keyword/rest arguments, native names, constructors, forwarding, method values, inheritance/super, and native Array map/select lowering. Added Haxe-origin and handwritten Ruby-origin executable coverage, source-positioned fail-closed diagnostics for unsupported typed expressions, canonical examples/snapshots, and comprehensive compiler/interop documentation. No new Dynamic/cast/raw-Ruby app API leaks; full local examples, Todo production, Playwright, Rails runtime, package, public-precommit, and Ruby 3.2/3.3/4.0 remote CI plus Gitleaks are green.

title: Bind tags and both hosted artifacts and add existing-tag-only repair
description: Create one release identity spanning tested commit, local and remote tag, GitHub Release, packaged source metadata, Haxelib ZIP, and hxruby gem. Verify both local artifacts before tag creation and both hosted assets after upload. Add protected workflow_dispatch repair accepting only an existing immutable v* tag.
acceptance_criteria: HEAD, local tag, origin tag, Release tag, ZIP metadata, and gem metadata resolve to one SHA and V; hosted upload state, exact two artifact names plus approved checksum/provenance assets, sizes, and SHA-256 values match local records and unexpected custom assets fail; repair never derives a version, creates/moves/deletes a tag, checks out main, or accepts mismatched bytes; it safely resumes tag/no-release, draft, partial upload, and final-verifier states; version tags have deletion/non-fast-forward protection and completed releases are immutable; dedicated creation identity is documented for multi-writer adoption.
close_reason: Live repair run 29221763110 proved workflow-tool overlay and exact tag rebuild succeeded, then exposed GitHub's persistent draft tag_name as internal untagged-<hex>. Added a narrow temporary-draft contract: authenticated lookup accepts that shape only for a draft whose name is exact vV; assets are fully verified before PATCH binding tag_name/target SHA/name; metadata and bytes are revalidated before immutable publication; completed releases never accept internal tags. State coverage now includes temporary draft binding and all focused workflow/hosting/artifact/audit gates pass.

title: Publish only the exact successfully tested RubyHx main commit
description: Turn normal publication into the final privileged job of the same push workflow graph that runs formatter, the full Ruby matrix, Rails runtime/browser/production dogfood, package contracts, security, and artifact checks. Remove normal branch-oriented workflow_dispatch; a separate manual lane will be repair-only. Use deterministic locked installs and reviewed toolchain pins.
acceptance_criteria: Only a push to main whose declared needs all succeeded can publish github.sha; PRs, forks, feature pushes, failed/cancelled lanes, arbitrary manual refs, and untested SHAs cannot publish; checkout is the exact SHA with full history; the privileged job imports no untrusted executable cache/artifact; permissions are minimal and release comments disabled unless justified; one fixed concurrency group serializes publication and repair; Actions use full commit SHAs, runner/Node/Ruby/Haxe/npm/semantic-release tooling is exact and locked, npm ci is used, and audit is clean; trigger-contract tests cover the event matrix.
close_reason: Completed exact tested-SHA publication with all same-run gates, minimal privileges, locked toolchains, deterministic artifacts, documented contracts, and hosted clean-run fixes. Live CI exposed three correctly blocked gaps: missing Haxe in release contracts, lix unavailable to Haxe child installs until local PATH was exported first, and semantic-release stable branches ignoring the immutable v0.1.0-beta.2 prerelease baseline. Added a narrowly scoped Git-derived promotion bridge that accepts only the configured newest merged major-zero prerelease, creates local-only v0.0.0 at the same commit, forces the first qualifying stable result to v0.1.0, anchors generated compare notes to the real beta tag, deletes the alias before any push, and becomes a no-op after stable lineage exists. A real temporary-remote test proves the exact remote tags are only v0.1.0-beta.2 and v0.1.0. Final Node 22.14.0/npm 10.9.2 npm test, 30 examples, snapshots, Haxelib 637 files, gem, Playwright 15/15, Todo production 26 runs/217 assertions, mandatory Rails runtime, exact transition/contracts/reproducibility, zero-vulnerability audit, formatter, Gitleaks, YAML, syntax, alias absence, and diff hygiene pass.

title: Make the Haxelib ZIP and hxruby gem reproducible exact artifacts
description: Replace system zip and worktree-plus-untracked input collection with canonical builds from the tested tracked commit. Define separate full content contracts for the Reflaxe Haxelib layout and the Ruby gem, including generated Reflaxe sources, Ruby/Rails files, vendor Genes, metadata, modes, and forbidden content. Build and smoke the exact files intended for upload.
acceptance_criteria: Fixed local paths produce exactly one ZIP and one gem; canonical ordering, timestamps, permissions, locale, timezone, umask, and pinned builders make two complete builds byte-identical under varied environments; untracked and dirty source inputs cannot enter either artifact; missing, altered, duplicate, extra, absolute, traversal, symlink, or unsafe-mode content fails; isolated haxelib installation/compilation and isolated gem installation/Rails task smoke consume the exact built artifacts; each artifact emits exact filename, bytes, and SHA-256 metadata/sidecar bound to V and source SHA.
close_reason: Built both fixed artifacts solely from the tested Git tree; added deterministic pinned ZIP and normalized gem construction, full content/mode manifests, exact SHA-256 identity sidecars, environment/dirty-input reproducibility and fail-closed negative gates, isolated Haxelib/gem consumers, and durable README/docs/AGENTS contracts. Full npm test, 30 examples, Playwright 15/15, Todo production 26 runs/217 assertions, mandatory Rails runtime, package checks, formatter, and Gitleaks pass.

title: Remove release commits and stage release versions into both artifacts
description: Remove @semantic-release/changelog, @semantic-release/git, sync-versions tracked writes, README current-version rewriting, and release-time git identity. Keep intentional development sentinels in package.json, haxelib.json, HXML, gemspec/version surfaces, and durable docs. Inject nextRelease.version, tag, and source SHA only into temporary Haxelib and gem staging trees.
acceptance_criteria: Normal semantic-release prepare leaves tracked files byte-identical and git diff empty; the Haxelib ZIP and gem expose V consistently to their consumers while checkout metadata remains a documented development sentinel; no glob can upload stale artifacts; GitHub Release notes are canonical and include categorized changes, compare/commit links, and version heading; CHANGELOG.md is frozen history or maintained through ordinary reviewed commits outside publication; version-sync guards validate sentinels and staged artifacts rather than a mutable current release.
close_reason: Replaced release-time tracked version rewrites and release commits with a validated version/tag/source-SHA identity staged only into temporary Haxelib and gem trees. Tracked npm/Haxelib/HXML/Ruby version surfaces now use the documented 0.0.0 development sentinel; sync-versions, changelog/git release plugins, README rewriting, and wildcard uploads are removed. Both fixed local artifacts embed consistent staged metadata, HXRuby::VERSION, and release-provenance.json, while package tests assert checkout diff immutability and consumer install/runtime behavior. GitHub release configuration uses exact fixed paths, versioned hosted names, a version heading, and generated notes with compare/commit links; CHANGELOG is reviewed frozen history. Full npm test, all 30 examples, staged Haxelib/gem consumers, Playwright 15/15, Todo production 26 runs/217 assertions plus eager load/archive, mandatory Rails runtime, formatter, Gitleaks, syntax, and diff hygiene pass.

title: Choose the 0.x release channel and implement a policy-only SemVer core
description: Replace default commit analysis and the instruction that all 0.x publication must stay manual with an explicit tested policy. Prefer normal 0.x releases from main because major zero already means initial development; if the project deliberately retains beta suffixes, model a real prerelease branch/channel and promotion path rather than letting main plus manual dispatch imply it accidentally. Use a locked standards-tested SemVer library and per-major stable approval.
acceptance_criteria: A recorded decision selects normal 0.x main releases or an explicit beta channel with branch and promotion semantics; fix, feat, breaking 0.x, approved 1.0 graduation, stable breaking, prerelease, and no-release commit sets are exercised through the real analyzer; unapproved 0.x breaking changes do not become 1.0.0; every stable major has an independent approval; invalid/unsupported SemVer forms and unknown majors fail closed; package metadata cannot influence tag-derived lineage; AGENTS policy is updated only after the automated path has matching regression and live evidence.
notes: User decision (2026-07-11): implement conventional SemVer behavior; do not treat this as an open-ended product-policy choice. Schedule after the current stdlib parity work.
close_reason: Selected normal stable 0.x releases from main and replaced default commit analysis with a documented policy wrapper over the official Conventional Commit analyzer. The policy derives lineage only from exact canonical v<SemVer> tags, uses directly pinned semver 7.8.4, maps unapproved major-zero breaking changes to minor releases, requires contiguous independent approvals for 1.0 and every later stable major, and fails closed on missing/mismatched tags, invalid/unsupported versions, unknown majors, and unapproved stable breaks. Added direct fix/feat/breaking/prerelease/no-release/negative coverage plus a real semantic-release dry run proving tag ownership despite contradictory package metadata; wired the gate into full and fast CI; updated README, docs index, canonical release policy, and evidence-gated AGENTS guidance. Verified npm test (30 examples, snapshots, packages), Playwright 15/15, Todo production 26 runs/217 assertions plus eager load/archive, mandatory Rails runtime, formatter, Gitleaks, focused policy/contracts, syntax, and diff hygiene.

title: Adopt a tested-commit SemVer release protocol for RubyHx
description: Adapt the proven ../haxe.rust release protocol to RubyHx and its two real artifacts: a Reflaxe Haxelib ZIP and the hxruby gem. Replace manual branch publication and release-time repository mutation with CI testing source commit S, deriving V from immutable tags plus a small policy, building deterministic artifacts from S with V injected only into staging, tagging S, publishing the exact artifacts, and verifying their hosted identities. Do not copy Rust-specific package layout.
acceptance_criteria: One simple documented protocol owns both artifacts; normal publication tags the exact successfully tested commit and never writes tracked version/changelog/prose files; major-zero and any deliberate prerelease channel behavior are explicit and standards-tested; both artifacts have exact content and digest contracts; partial hosted publication is repairable only for an existing tag; remote tags and completed releases are immutable; first live proof and a subsequent no-op run are recorded.
notes: Audit evidence (2026-07-10): package.json embeds default semantic-release analysis plus changelog/git plugins, tracked version/prose rewriting, and wildcard uploads for a ZIP and gem. .github/workflows/release.yml is manual branch publication: workflow_dispatch checks out current main, has no exact-SHA CI precondition, uses npm install, and grants a release token to repository code. scripts/release/build-haxelib-package.js deliberately includes untracked files and uses system zip; neither artifact is bound to hosted digest or source SHA. The only SemVer tag/release is v0.1.0-beta.2, while channel/promotion semantics are not modeled. Immutable Releases are disabled and no repository rulesets are configured. Existing version-sync and release-contract tests pass because they assert this predecessor design.
