close_reason: Completed all six tested-commit SemVer release protocol slices: conventional major-zero policy, staging-only identity, reproducible ZIP/gem artifacts, exact same-run tested-SHA publication, immutable hosted identity and existing-tag-only repair, plus fully migrated docs and live/no-release proof. Normal publication and repair are proven live, version tags are protected, native immutable releases are enabled, and the complete local/hosted QA contract is green.

title: Align Ruby compiler with Reflaxe generated layout conventions
description: Move reflaxe.ruby toward the layout and packaging conventions produced by reflaxe new: source std overrides under std/ruby/_std, haxelib.json reflaxe.stdPaths as the source of truth, local haxe_libraries classpaths aligned with source stdPaths, release packaging delegated to Reflaxe Run build for std flattening and .cross.hx generation, and package/generator smoke tests that split Ruby target consumers (-lib reflaxe.ruby) from RailsHx browser consumers (-lib railshx.client). Document the source-vs-package layout clearly and record any Ruby-specific deviations from textbook Reflaxe behavior.
close_reason: Completed Reflaxe-convention layout refactor: Ruby std overrides now live under std/ruby/_std with haxelib.json reflaxe.stdPaths, source hxml includes _std for the Ruby target, haxelib packaging delegates flattening to vendored Reflaxe Run build, RailsHx browser builds use the separate railshx.client library, generated apps/gem/package checks cover the split, and docs describe the shared std/type boundary and release package shape.

title: Keep generated Rails output handwritten-looking and isolate compiler metadata
description: RailsHx generated Ruby/Rails artifacts should look like idiomatic hand-written Rails code by default. Current output still exposes compiler internals such as __hx_rails_schema on ActiveRecord models and avoidable __hx* temporaries in app-facing code. Move required typed metadata to isolated RailsHx support surfaces/manifests or generated support modules, remove avoidable compiler temps, and require inline generated comments for any remaining scaffolding that is semantically necessary.
acceptance_criteria: Generated Rails models/controllers/views no longer expose avoidable __hx_* methods or compiler temp names; required schema/typed metadata lives in an isolated RailsHx namespace/manifest/support module; any unavoidable scaffolding has an inline generated comment explaining why it exists and what consumes it; snapshots and runtime tests enforce the cleaned output shape.
notes: Completed: generated Rails output now writes app-facing Rails artifacts to idiomatic Rails paths, removes runtime __hx_rails_schema / typed_column_count model pollution, keeps typedColumnCount as a compile-time literal helper, and avoids first-use __hx0 local suffixes in generated Ruby unless a real collision/internal compiler need remains. Added/updated smoke checks and snapshots to reject the old schema pollution and validate the cleaner generated shape. Verification: npm test passed green.

title: Implement typed ActionMailer parameterized mailer contracts
description: Add the first typed RailsHx parameterized-mailer slice. Mailer classes should declare a params typedef through metadata or a typed base/facade, mailer bodies should access params through typed helpers, and generated delivery wrappers should lower to ordinary Rails UserMailer.with(...).action delivery chains.
acceptance_criteria: A Haxe-authored mailer can declare required params and read them without Dynamic; generated Ruby uses ActionMailer params[:key] inside the mailer and UserMailer.with(key: value).action for delivery; Haxe compile tests reject missing/wrong parameter values and unknown params; unchecked dynamic params remain an explicit escape hatch; action-mailer smoke snapshots assert generated Ruby shape and docs show the supported API.
close_reason: Implemented @:railsMailerParams typed parameterized mailer contracts: the ActionMailer base build macro now generates withParams(...) for Rails .with(...) and generated p.<field> MailParam tokens from a params typedef, compiler lowering keeps params[:key] output clean, the action_mailer example uses the generated API, docs/inventory/compatibility were updated, and focused ActionMailer smoke passes.

title: Implement typed ActionMailer preview classes
description: Add RailsHx-authored ActionMailer preview support that emits normal Rails ActionMailer::Preview classes under test/mailers/previews. Preview methods should call typed mailer delivery wrappers so preview params/templates remain checked.
acceptance_criteria: @:railsMailerPreview or equivalent emits ActionMailer::Preview Ruby in the Rails preview path; preview methods can return typed mailer message deliveries; generated preview Ruby is snapshotted and ruby -c checked; REQUIRE_RAILS=1 action-mailer runtime lane loads or invokes at least one generated preview; unsupported Rails-owned preview adoption remains documented as an interop boundary.
close_reason: Implemented Haxe-authored ActionMailer previews: added rails.action_mailer.Preview, @:railsMailerPreview compiler-erased artifact emission under test/mailers/previews, safe preview path validation and duplicate detection, an action_mailer preview example using typed withParams, committed preview snapshots, ruby -c coverage, and Rails runtime loading/invocation in the action-mailer smoke.

title: Implement typed ActionCable connection auth and identifiers
description: Implement the typed RailsHx ActionCable connection/auth design from haxe.ruby-bee: @:railsCableConnection classes, identified_by declarations, typed connection identifiers, assign/access helpers, and reject_unauthorized_connection lowering.
acceptance_criteria: std exposes rails.action_cable.Connection and ConnectionIdentifier<T> with documented typed helpers; @:railsCableConnection emits normal ActionCable::Connection::Base Ruby with identified_by declarations from static final identifiers = { identifiedBy(...) }; connect() and helper methods compile normally; assign(identifier, value) lowers to self.<identifier> = value; Channel.connection(identifier) or equivalent exposes typed identifiers inside @:railsChannel classes without Dynamic; rejectUnauthorizedConnection() lowers to reject_unauthorized_connection; ActionCable snapshots include generated connection output; negative compile tests reject raw string identifiers and channels accessing undeclared identifiers; generated Rails ActionCable::Connection::TestCase runtime coverage asserts accepted and rejected connections; docs and compatibility/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActionCable connection support: Connection, ConnectionIdentifier<T>, ConnectionParam<T>, ConnectionDecl, CableConnectionDsl, ConnectionMacro, @:railsCableConnection Ruby emission, identified_by lowering, assign/requestParam/channel connection access lowering, ActionCable sample coverage, negative raw identifier checks, committed snapshots, runtime ActionCable::Connection::TestCase accepted/rejected coverage, docs, inventory, and package checks.

title: Add RailsHx compile-then-delegate Rails tasks
description: Add RailsHx Rake tasks that refresh generated Haxe/HHX artifacts before delegating to Rails tasks, and document that watch mode keeps target artifacts current during development.
acceptance_criteria: Generated apps expose compile-then-delegate tasks for db migrate/prepare/rollback, tests, and arbitrary Rails task delegation; docs explain raw Rails tasks remain valid when artifacts are current; package/generator smoke tests assert the task surface.
close_reason: Implemented compile-then-delegate RailsHx Rake tasks for db migrate/prepare/rollback, generic Rails task delegation, and tests; documented workflow and updated package/generator smoke coverage.

title: Polish todoapp Devise/admin UI buttons and spacing
description: Fix unstyled native buttons and awkward vertical spacing in the RailsHx todoapp Devise/admin surfaces. Ensure login/logout/guest/admin CRUD controls use the app button system, panels have intentional spacing at desktop and mobile widths, and RailsHx HHX remains the source of truth.
close_reason: Polished todoapp Devise/admin UI: styled Rails button_to outputs whether classes land on form/input/button, added user-frame breathing room, tightened admin panel spacing, and verified with todoapp static smoke plus Playwright browser gate.

title: Keep chat status live after Turbo stream replacement
description: The todoapp chat panel currently uses a manual client catch-up path: Haxe JS fetches a Turbo Stream snapshot, calls Turbo.renderStreamMessage, then waits with Async.delay(0) before rebinding hooks/status. That timer is a smell and is not needed in a classic Hotwire app. Replace this with the Rails-native shape: typed HHX <turbo_stream_from stream=...>, server-side TurboStreams.broadcast*To(...) rendering a typed HHX partial, and minimal Haxe JS only for progressive form/status polish where Rails does not already provide the behavior.
notes: Fixed by replacing todoapp custom ChatMessagesChannel/client catch-up with typed HHX turbo_stream_from plus server-rendered Turbo Stream broadcasts. Restored visible connection status by styling Turbo Rails native turbo-cable-stream-source[connected], not by adding custom subscription JS. Verified with todoapp smoke, Rails tests, and Playwright.

title: Expand todoapp_rails into a richer RailsHx dogfood app
description: Grow examples/todoapp_rails from a typed todo slice into a richer, beautiful RailsHx app that demonstrates production-relevant Rails surfaces: users, session/login flow, roles/user management, chatroom/realtime-style interactions, richer Turbo/importmap JS patterns, and well-tested Rails runtime/browser behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Todoapp remains beautiful and fast; source of truth stays Haxe/HHX for RailsHx-owned artifacts; first-party auth/session and roles are implemented without hard-coding Devise until the DeviseHx/gem-layer bead exists; later Devise integration can be added as an explicit gem-layer example; app illustrates typed models, associations, migrations, controllers, routes, strong params, HHX templates, components, Turbo frames/streams/client JS, Rails tests, Haxe-authored tests, and Playwright; README documents start/watch/prod/test flows; each new feature has Rails-style unit/request/browser coverage and comments in example source explaining the type-safety feature being exercised.
notes: Completed richer todoapp dogfood expansion across two slices. First slice added typed users, session/login flow, roles/user management, Haxe-owned user/session routes, HHX user switcher, Rails tests, and Haxe client session hooks. Second slice added typed ChatMessage model, CreateChatMessages migration snapshot, ChatMessagesController, Haxe-owned chat_messages route, ChatPanel HHX partial, Turbo Stream replacement flow, Haxe client chat hooks, seed data, CSS polish, Rails model/request tests, Playwright chat posting, and snapshot/static smoke coverage. True multi-browser ActionCable live broadcast is intentionally split to haxe.ruby-bjv.15 as optional deeper dogfood.
close_reason: Todoapp now demonstrates typed users/session/roles/user management plus a typed Turbo-backed chatroom with model, migration, controller, routes, strong params, HHX, Haxe JS hooks, Rails tests, Playwright, and snapshots. Optional true ActionCable live chat follow-up filed as haxe.ruby-bjv.15.

title: Add generator-assisted RailsHx gem-layer adoption
description: Design and implement a RailsHx installed-gem adoption lane so apps can generate typed Haxe contracts for Bundler-installed Ruby/Rails gems, with Devise as the guiding production example. Runtime ownership stays with the Ruby gem; RailsHx owns typed contracts, macros, validation, and generated Haxe package structure.
acceptance_criteria: A future bin/rails generate hxruby:adopt --gem or hxruby:gem-layer command discovers installed gems through Bundler, fails closed for missing/unsafe gem paths, reads deterministic metadata such as RBS/YARD/source/routes without executing arbitrary app code, emits review-marked typed Haxe contracts instead of unchecked Dynamic, supports reusable companion package extraction for popular gems, includes a DeviseHx design/example covering model modules, controller helpers, routes, and current_user typing, and documents the workflow with tests for missing gem, generated contract compile, and Rails runtime/parity seams.
notes: Implemented the deterministic first slice of gem-layer adoption: hxruby:adopt accepts --gem NAME --discover and --gem NAME --write contracts; discovery resolves installed gems through Bundler without requiring gem source, parses Ruby source with Ripper instead of execution, fails closed for missing Gemfile/missing gems/unsafe names, emits review-marked app-local Haxe contracts and docs, updates generated app docs, and extends rails-adopt-generator smoke coverage for discovery, contract compile, manifest entries, and failure cases. Remaining reusable companion-package/DeviseHx work moved to haxe.ruby-bjv.6.4. Before implementing that companion-package/DeviseHx portion, ask GPT 5.5 Pro for a focused design review using this repo, ../haxe.elixir.codex, ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/rails, ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/ruby, and target gem source/docs as references.
close_reason: Completed deterministic installed-gem adoption slice: --gem discovery and --write contracts are implemented, fail-closed, documented, and covered by rails-adopt-generator smoke tests. Reusable DeviseHx companion package hardening is split to haxe.ruby-bjv.6.4 with GPT 5.5 Pro pre-research required.

title: Generate RailsHx starter app skeleton and start tasks
description: Make the RailsHx install/app generator create a useful typed Rails starter skeleton and default Rake start/watch tasks, then document the workflow from README and RailsHx docs.
acceptance_criteria: Generated RailsHx app includes typed controller, HHX layout/page, Haxe-owned routes, route extern placeholder, client JS, CSS, importmap, Rake tasks, and bin helpers; installed app exposes bundle exec rake hxruby:start and hxruby:start:watch; generator smoke/package/release contract tests cover the skeleton and tasks; README/docs/examples explain the generated files and dev/prod workflows.
close_reason: Implemented RailsHx starter app skeleton, app-level start/watch tasks, route alias, docs, and generator smoke compile coverage.

title: Add one-command todoapp start task
description: Add a one-command Rake todoapp start workflow that prepares and starts the generated Rails app, plus an integrated watch mode that runs the Rails server and RailsHx watcher together.
acceptance_criteria: rake todoapp:start prepares and starts the todoapp server; WATCH=1 rake todoapp:start and rake todoapp:start:watch prepare once and run server plus watcher with cleanup on exit; docs and todoapp script hints use the new Rake commands.
close_reason: Added rake todoapp:start as the one-command prepare-and-server path, plus WATCH=1 rake todoapp:start, rake todoapp:start:watch, and rake 'todoapp:start[watch]' for an integrated server+watcher loop with process cleanup. Updated README/todoapp docs and script hints.

title: Expose RailsHx repo/todoapp workflows through Rake
description: Add root Rake entrypoints for RailsHx repo-facing Rails/todoapp workflows so Ruby/Rails users can run rake todoapp:* and rake rails:* instead of npm scripts. Keep npm for CI/Node-only internals.
acceptance_criteria: Root Rakefile exposes todoapp compile/prepare/server/watch/test/production/playwright tasks, Rails generator wrapper tasks, format/security/package helpers, and docs present Rake as the RailsHx/todoapp happy path while preserving npm as CI/internal glue.
close_reason: Added root Rakefile with RailsHx/todoapp/generator/format/security/package/test wrappers; updated README/todoapp/docs to present Rake as the Rails/RailsHx-facing UX while preserving npm as Node/CI glue; release contracts now verify the Rake-facing docs and root Rakefile.

title: Switch greenfield generator route defaults after gates pass
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. After route parity/tests/docs pass, update greenfield RailsHx generators to default to Haxe-owned routes while preserving Rails-owned route sync for existing apps and adoption.
acceptance_criteria: Greenfield RailsHx app/scaffold generators support --routes=haxe|snippet|rails|none, default to --routes=haxe only for newly generated RailsHx apps, and never silently mutate unowned config/routes.rb. Existing/adopted Rails apps default to --routes=snippet or --routes=rails. Scaffold route behavior depends on haxe.ruby-2pd.5 parity. Generated todoapp uses Haxe-owned routes and route externs generated from Rails output. Docs and help output explain the modes.
close_reason: Greenfield app and scaffold generators now support --routes=haxe|snippet|rails|none. App/install defaults to Haxe-owned routes while allowing snippet, Rails-owned, or no-route modes. Scaffold defaults to Haxe-owned AppRoutes for controller scaffolds, keeps Rails-owned helper extern behavior under --routes=rails, writes reviewable route instructions under --routes=snippet, and leaves route files untouched under --routes=none. Rails generator wrappers expose the same option, smoke tests cover all modes, docs/help explain the source-of-truth choices, and todoapp remains Haxe-owned with route externs generated from Rails output.

title: Update routing docs and examples
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Document Haxe-owned routing, Rails-owned routing, source-of-truth inversion, generated-artifact ownership, parity checks, and examples once the route DSL implementation lands.
acceptance_criteria: docs/railshx-routing-design.md, docs/railshx-roadmap.md, docs/railshx-generators-and-tasks-design.md, docs/railshx-generated-artifact-ownership.md, docs/railshx-controller-guide.md, docs/railshx-testing-strategy.md, README links, and examples/todoapp_rails route docs are updated. Examples explain which type-safety features are exercised, what IntelliSense exposes, and which Rails artifacts are emitted. Todoapp demonstrates Haxe-owned routes end-to-end while Rails-owned route sync remains documented for adoption.
notes: Todoapp now demonstrates mixed route ownership: AppRoutes.hx owns the greenfield routes, a documented Rails-owned routes_rails_owned.rb snippet models an existing Rails route, generated Routes.hx exposes legacyHealthPath(), and typed HHX consumes that helper from Haxe. Full routing docs/examples for every route mode remain open under this bead.
close_reason: Updated routing docs and examples for the implemented Haxe-owned/Rails-owned route contract. Added examples/rails_routes_dsl README, linked the focused route snapshot fixture from README/docs, aligned railshx-routing-design with generated headers/manifests and implemented evidence, documented route generator modes, and cross-linked routing from controller, roadmap, ownership, testing, and todoapp docs.

title: Add route snapshots and negative compile tests
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Add compiler snapshots, generated Routes.hx snapshots, negative compile tests, Rails request tests, Rails-owned fixture tests, and generated-artifact ownership tests for the routing DSL.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshots cover generated config/routes.rb and generated Routes.hx for root, verbs, typed targets, resources/resource, member/collection, namespace/scope/controller, optional/glob, constraints/defaults, mountExternal, and external controller refs. Negative compile tests cover missing/non-static routes, missing controller/action, invalid only/except action, newAction mapping errors, invalid aliases/paths/regexes/constants, member/collection outside resources, unchecked raw routes without define, duplicate output targets, and unowned config/routes.rb overwrite attempts. Rails request/runtime tests cover root dispatch, resource route, member/collection route, namespace, optional segment, and glob route.
notes:
2026-06-18 runtime route slice: expanded todoapp_rails Haxe-owned AppRoutes with resource collection/member routes, namespace helper output, optional segment route, and glob route. Added vanilla Rails RoutesTest coverage for root/resources/member/collection/optional/glob recognition, generated route helpers, and the Rails-owned legacy_health adoption route. Updated route extern fixture/snapshots and todoapp smoke checks. Real Rails todoapp test lane passes locally: 15 runs, 91 assertions, 0 failures.

2026-06-18 negative route DSL slice: added compile-failure coverage for missing routes field, non-static routes field, non-block routes initializer, invalid resources only/except actions, only+except conflicts, top-level member blocks, and Rails :new action mapping. Added compiler diagnostic that Haxe newAction route refs must be @:native("new") so generated Rails routes lower to :new; positive route DSL smoke now covers resources(... only: [newAction]) output.
close_reason: Completed route snapshot and negative compile test contract. Added a dedicated rails_routes_dsl snapshot fixture covering config/routes.rb and .railshx/routes.haxe.json for root, verbs, typed targets, resources/resource, member/collection, namespace/scope/controller, optional/glob paths, defaults/constraints, mountExternal, external controller refs, and newAction->:new mapping. Negative route DSL smoke covers missing/non-static/invalid routes declarations, missing controller/action, invalid only/except, newAction mapping, aliases/paths/regexes/constants, member/collection nesting, unchecked raw routes, duplicate outputs, and unowned overwrite attempts. Rails request/runtime coverage is present in todoapp_rails and passed locally.

title: Implement resources and scope edge coverage
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Fill out the first-slice Rails routing surface beyond basic root/verbs so the DSL is useful for real Rails apps without pretending to support arbitrary Ruby routes.
acceptance_criteria: Haxe-owned routing supports resource, resources, only, except, member, collection, namespace, scope path/module/as/defaults/constraints, controller blocks, optional segments, glob segments, param, simple defaults, simple constraints, checked mountExternal, and checked external controllers. Deferred surfaces such as concerns, shallow routes, drawExternal, redirect/direct/resolve, gem macros, arbitrary Ruby loops, and dynamic route generation are explicitly unsupported with diagnostics or documented follow-up beads.
close_reason: Route edge coverage is complete for this slice: Haxe-owned routing already supports resources/resource, only/except/param, member/collection, namespace/scope/controller, optional/glob paths, defaults/constraints, mountExternal, checked extern targets, and route manifests/parity. Added explicit RailsHx diagnostics and smoke coverage for deferred concerns, shallow routes, external route files, redirect/direct/resolve-style helpers, and gem macros so unsupported Rails surfaces fail clearly instead of as generic Haxe unknown identifiers.

title: Add Haxe-owned route sync and parity task
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Extend route helper sync so Haxe-owned mode compiles Haxe, asks Rails for the authoritative route table, regenerates Routes.hx, and compares Rails output with the Haxe route manifest.
acceptance_criteria: hxruby:routes supports rails-owned, haxe-owned, and auto modes. Haxe-owned mode runs Haxe compile first, runs bin/rails routes --expanded, regenerates Routes.hx using the existing generator path, and runs RoutesParity checks. Parity catches missing route, wrong target, wrong path, wrong verb, wrong explicit alias, and unsafe opaque raw routes. Rails-owned route helper sync remains backward compatible.
close_reason: Implemented hxruby:routes MODE=rails-owned|haxe-owned|auto. Haxe-owned mode compiles Haxe first, runs Rails routes, regenerates Routes.hx through the existing generator, and validates Rails output against .railshx/routes.haxe.json with the new RoutesParity checker. Parity smoke covers success plus missing route, wrong target, wrong path, wrong verb, and opaque raw-route rejection; Rails-owned route generator remains backward compatible.

title: Add compiler-owned route manifest and ownership recording
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Record Haxe-owned route output in a manifest and enforce generated-artifact ownership for config/routes.rb and route JSON artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Compiler emits .railshx/routes.haxe.json or .railshx/routes/main.json with source, output, class, declarations, positions, and opaque unsafe entries. config/routes.rb writes are allowed only when absent, RailsHx-header-owned, or manifest-owned. Existing unowned config/routes.rb fails closed. Multiple @:railsRoutes classes targeting one output fail. Manifest/header ownership tests cover absent, generated rewrite, manifest rewrite, unsafe path, and unowned collision.
close_reason: Implemented Haxe-owned route manifest and ownership recording: compiler emits .railshx/routes.haxe.json with source/class/output/declarations/positions/opaque raw entries, records config/routes.rb and the route manifest in .railshx/manifest.json with checksums, allows manifest/header-owned rewrites, rejects unowned config/routes.rb collisions, and rejects duplicate @:railsRoutes outputs. routes-dsl smoke covers these cases.

title: Extract route IR and emit config/routes.rb
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Add compiler extraction for @:railsRoutes classes and emit readable Rails-native config/routes.rb from the route IR while suppressing Ruby generation for the declaration host.
acceptance_criteria: RubyCompiler detects @:railsRoutes only in Rails mode, extracts exactly one static final routes declaration, lowers it through RailsRoutesExtractor/RailsRoutesEmitter/RailsRouteManifest, emits config/routes.rb with RailsHx header/source comments, preserves route declaration order, emits ordinary Rails routing DSL, and produces no AppRoutes Ruby class. Snapshots cover root, verbs, typed targets, resources, member/collection, namespace, controller, and checked external targets.
notes: First compiler emission slice landed: RubyCompiler consumes @:railsRoutes classes with static final routes, emits generated config/routes.rb, suppresses Ruby class output for the route declaration host, and snapshots todoapp routes.\n\n2026-06-18: Extracted route IR typedefs, route-tree validation, and Rails route rendering into reflaxe.ruby.rails.RailsRouteDecl/RailsRouteTarget, RailsRoutesExtractor, and RailsRoutesEmitter. RubyCompiler now owns only typed marker extraction and generated extra-file emission for this slice. Snapshot harness confirms generated Rails route output is stable. Remaining before close: route manifest ownership handoff with haxe.ruby-2pd.4 and any final broad fixture/snapshot additions needed around that manifest path.
close_reason: Route compiler extraction/emission is complete for this slice: RubyCompiler detects @:railsRoutes in Rails mode, extracts the static routes host, delegates route-tree validation to RailsRoutesExtractor, rendering to RailsRoutesEmitter, route manifest serialization to RailsRouteManifest, emits config/routes.rb, suppresses the declaration host Ruby class, and snapshots/smokes cover the generated route output.

title: Validate route refs and checked literals
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Implement compile-time validation for typed Rails route targets and checked literal escape hatches before broad route emission. Move common Rails route mistakes into Haxe diagnostics wherever metadata is available.
acceptance_criteria: to(Controller, action) validates controller type refs and action identifiers. @:railsExternalController externs validate checked existing Ruby controller contracts. externalTo literals validate controller#action shape, safe chars, no traversal, and no Ruby injection. routeName, resourceName, paramName, at, rubyConst, checked paths, simple regex constraints, only/except actions, member/collection context, and unknown options produce useful compile-time diagnostics. uncheckedRubyRoute requires -D railshx_allow_unchecked_routes.
notes:
First validation slice landed: RoutesDsl validates @:railsController/@:railsExternalController refs, @:railsModel resource refs, action identifiers, resources only actions, checked route/path/name literals, externalTo, rubyConst/at/mountExternal, and gated uncheckedRubyRoute diagnostics.

2026-06-18: Added compiler-side duplicate explicit route alias diagnostics across the full Haxe-owned route tree, including nested collection/member routes. routes-dsl smoke covers duplicate asName failure.

2026-06-18: Added checked defaults(...) and constraints(...) route blocks with rx(...) segment-regex validation. Regex constraints are literal-only, reject Rails-invalid anchors and unsafe delimiter/control chars, and lower to normal Rails defaults/constraints blocks. routes-dsl smoke covers generated Ruby, anchored-regex failure, and bad option-key failure. Adjacent routes-generator fixture was refreshed to match the current todoapp route extern surface. Remaining: resourceName/non-model resource ergonomics and any broader Rails-owned source-backed action validation.
close_reason: Implemented route validation/literal slice: typed controller/action refs, checked route/path/name literals, externalTo/rubyConst/at/mountExternal, duplicate alias diagnostics, defaults/constraints with rx validation, resourceName adoption path, member/collection context diagnostics, unknown option diagnostics, and gated uncheckedRubyRoute are covered by routes-dsl smoke.

title: Add Rails route DSL markers and public macros
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Add the public Haxe-owned Rails routing DSL surface: RoutesDsl macro facade plus route carrier/value types under std/rails/routing. The canonical API is @:railsRoutes class ... { static final routes = { ... }; }. Marker calls are compiler carriers, not a runtime router.
acceptance_criteria: Files exist for std/rails/macros/RoutesDsl.hx and std/rails/routing/{RouteDecl,RouteTarget,RouteName,ResourceAction,HttpVerb,RouteRegex}. Public examples support root, verb calls, to(Controller, action), resources/resource, scope blocks, routeName, paramName, rubyConst, externalTo, and uncheckedRubyRoute as typed/macro-facing APIs. DSL comments explain the advanced Haxe/macros boundary.
notes:
First vertical slice landed: std/rails/macros/RoutesDsl.hx plus RouteDecl/RouteTarget carriers support root(to(...)) and resources(Model, Controller, {only: [...]}) for the todoapp. Remaining acceptance items such as routeName/paramName/rubyConst/externalTo/uncheckedRubyRoute and broader route declarations remain open.

2026-06-18: Added checked external route target and mounted Rack app APIs: externalTo("legacy/posts#show"), rubyConst("Sidekiq::Web"), at("/sidekiq"), and mountExternal(...). Route DSL smoke covers generated Rails output plus invalid external target and unsafe Ruby constant diagnostics. uncheckedRubyRoute remains deferred/gated.
close_reason: Completed the public Rails route DSL surface: RouteDecl/RouteTarget plus RouteName/ResourceAction/HttpVerb/RouteRegex carriers, typed root/verbs/resources/scope APIs, checked externalTo/rubyConst/at/mountExternal escapes, gated uncheckedRubyRoute, route smoke coverage, Ruby syntax checks, and escape-hatch docs. Parent route epic remains open for validation, manifest, parity, and broader runtime slices.

title: Implement Haxe-owned RailsHx routes DSL
description: Design and implement a typed Haxe-owned RailsHx route DSL for greenfield apps while keeping Rails-owned config/routes.rb adoption fully supported. The DSL should feel close to Rails routes, emit normal config/routes.rb, then run rails routes to generate the canonical typed Haxe route externs.
acceptance_criteria: A @:railsRoutes or equivalent Haxe DSL supports root, resources only/except, member/collection, namespace, optional segment, glob, and mounted/engine-adjacent fixtures where practical; Haxe-owned route emission refuses to overwrite non-owned config/routes.rb; Rails-owned route sync remains supported; route helper externs are generated from rails routes in both modes; todoapp or a focused fixture demonstrates Haxe-owned routes; snapshots and Rails runtime request tests cover both Rails-owned and Haxe-owned routes; docs explain source-of-truth inversion and ownership markers.
notes: Route DSL progress: initial slice added get/post/patch/put/delete, routeName/paramName, resources only/except/param, and routes-dsl smoke. Follow-up slices added singular resource(...), nested resources/resource children via member({...}) and collection({...}), top-level member/collection diagnostics, Rails-native nested config/routes.rb rendering, namespace(...), scope(...), controller(...) blocks. Current slice adds options/head, typed match(..., [GET, POST]), and checked optional/glob route path literals. Remaining: route manifest/parity task, duplicate alias checks, unowned config/routes overwrite coverage, contextual action-only shorthand, mounted/external/unsafe routes, and broader runtime request tests.
close_reason: Completed the Haxe-owned RailsHx route DSL feature. Implemented @:railsRoutes with valid-Haxe static final routes blocks, typed controller/action refs, resources/resource, member/collection, namespace/scope/controller/defaults/constraints, optional and glob paths, checked external targets, checked mountExternal, unsafe raw route gating, generated config/routes.rb and .railshx/routes.haxe.json with ownership manifest entries, Rails-owned and Haxe-owned hxruby:routes sync/parity modes, route helper extern generation from Rails output, todoapp runtime route coverage, focused route DSL snapshots/negative tests, docs, and greenfield generator route modes. Remaining haxe.ruby-2pd.10 is optional dogfood to reimplement the deterministic parity core in Haxe-authored Ruby, not a blocker for the public route DSL feature.

title: Prepare haxe.ruby for public release
description: Audit and harden public-facing readiness: Git hooks/pre-commit guidance, CI soundness, README beginner flow, linked docs, and secret leak filtering. Compare sibling Reflaxe target repos for baseline public-readiness patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Repository has documented local pre-commit/public-readiness checks; CI includes secret-leak scanning or equivalent gitleaks coverage; README is beginner-friendly with clear install, quickstart, RailsHx, tests, and docs links; docs index/links are coherent for public users; security leak filter config is present and testable; relevant gates pass; changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: Added public-readiness docs/index, Ruby/Rails layering guidance, Haxe formatter config/checks, pre-commit hook installer, gitleaks config/workflow, Dependabot config, security docs, and refreshed snapshots for the current RailsHx operation-migration contract. Verified formatter, gitleaks, snapshots, todoapp, Rails interop, generators, and compiler shell gates.

title: Design current-schema validation for historical migration snapshots
description: Historical migration snapshots and current ActiveRecord model metadata can intentionally differ. For example, a current model may include a field that an older AddColumn snapshot introduced, while the migration compiler currently treats knownModels as the validation source for both current schema and new-column checks. Design and implement a validation mode that preserves compile-time diagnostics without falsely rejecting legitimate migration history.
acceptance_criteria: RailsHx distinguishes current model/schema validation from historical migration operation validation; AddColumn snapshots generated before/alongside current model fields can compile without disabling table/column safety entirely; docs explain when to use knownModels, externalTables, schema snapshots, or future migration-history metadata; todoapp or a focused smoke covers a current model field introduced by an older snapshot; diagnostics remain fail-closed for genuinely unknown tables/columns.
close_reason: Implemented current-schema vs historical migration snapshot validation: knownModels still validates known tables/read-column references, but AddColumn newness is checked against same-snapshot columns instead of today’s model schema, allowing historical AddColumn snapshots for current model fields while duplicate same-migration additions still fail. Added focused todoapp smoke coverage and docs.

title: Document RailsHx generators, tasks, and examples
description: Write user-facing docs and examples for RailsHx generator/task workflows, generated artifacts, schema adoption, migration snapshots, CI/deploy, and failure modes.
acceptance_criteria: Docs cover hxruby:install, routes, adopt, migration, model, controller, scaffold, doctor, check, clean, compile, watch, client compile, and production flows; each public generator guide shows command, generated Haxe source, generated Rails artifact, Rails command that consumes it, and at least one failure/diagnostic example; examples cover greenfield RailsHx model/scaffold, migration-only generation, existing Rails schema adoption, mixed Ruby/ERB interop, and generator help output; docs explicitly state that Rails owns db:migrate/test/zeitwerk/assets while RailsHx owns typed Haxe source and generated artifacts; examples/snapshots/gates prove docs are not fictional.
notes: Added docs/railshx-generator-workflows.md as the app-facing guide for generator commands, generated Haxe sources, Rails artifacts, Rails task handoff, diagnostics, ownership rules, health/CI gates, and focused examples. Linked it from README and docs index; deeper architecture remains in railshx-generators-and-tasks-design.md.
close_reason: Documented the RailsHx generator/task workflow with command, generated-source, generated-artifact, Rails runtime handoff, failure examples, CI gates, and example pointers for install, routes, model, migration, controller, scaffold, adoption, doctor/check/clean, watch, client compile, and production flows.

title: Add hxruby:doctor and hxruby:check tasks
description: Add RailsHx diagnostic and check tasks that compose Haxe compilation, generated artifact validation, Ruby syntax checks, and optional Rails-owned checks without replacing Rails runtime tasks.
acceptance_criteria: bundle exec rake hxruby:doctor checks Haxe availability, build files, app root, output roots, manifest readability, unsafe output paths, stale route extern hints, migration collisions, missing generated ownership headers, and client JS/importmap setup where applicable; bundle exec rake hxruby:check runs hxruby:compile, generated Ruby syntax checks, generated ERB/path presence checks, and optionally Rails-owned zeitwerk:check when Rails is available; hxruby:clean removes only manifest-owned generated artifacts; failure messages name the command to fix the issue; CI/smoke coverage includes success and representative failure cases without broad retesting of Rails internals.
close_reason: Implemented RailsHx hxruby:doctor, hxruby:check, and hxruby:clean. Doctor now checks Haxe/build files, Rails command availability, generated output roots, JSON manifest readability, manifest output drift/missing files, Haxe-owned route freshness, duplicate Rails migration timestamps/classes, and client JS/importmap setup hints. Check compiles, syntax-checks generated Ruby, validates manifest-owned generated artifacts, and optionally runs client/routes/zeitwerk gates. Clean removes only manifest-owned outputs. Added packaged-gem success/failure smokes and docs.

title: Add Rails schema adoption from db/schema.rb
description: Extend hxruby:adopt with fail-closed schema adoption that reads the current Rails schema and generates typed Haxe ActiveRecord contracts for existing Rails-owned tables without rewriting historical migrations.
acceptance_criteria: bin/rails generate hxruby:adopt --schema --discover reports tables, indexes, foreign keys, timestamps, inferred model names, and ambiguous cases without writing files; --schema --models Todo,User generates typed Haxe model contracts from db/schema.rb; parser handles create_table, column types/defaults/nullability, indexes, foreign keys, references, timestamps, and Rails-style pluralization where deterministic; unknown DB types fail unless --allow-dynamic, and generated Dynamic fields are review-marked; ambiguous associations generate columns plus TODO/review notes instead of unsafe belongsTo guesses; existing model files are not overwritten unless manifest-owned or --force is explicit; adopted models type-check RailsHx queries/templates/params and no Ruby migrations are rewritten.
notes: 2026-06-20: Implemented the first deterministic schema-adoption slice. `hxruby:adopt --schema --discover` now reads checked `db/schema.rb` input and reports tables, columns, timestamps, indexes, foreign keys, inferred model names, and review notes. `--schema --models Todo` emits manifest-owned typed ActiveRecord contracts from the current schema, refuses non-owned model collisions, fails unknown DB types unless `--allow-dynamic` is explicit, and marks Dynamic/foreign-key guesses with TODO comments instead of unsafe associations. Rails generator adapter, README, gradual-adoption docs, and rails-adopt-generator smoke coverage were updated. Follow-up same-day hardening added column-level discovery warnings, `t.references`/`t.belongs_to` adoption as typed `*_id` fields with review notes, and decimal precision/scale review preservation without pretending `@:railsColumn` supports precision metadata yet.
close_reason: Implemented checked Rails schema adoption from db/schema.rb: discovery reports current schema shape and review notes without writing, selected models generate typed manifest-owned ActiveRecord contracts, unknown DB types fail unless --allow-dynamic, reference/foreign-key ambiguity stays review-marked, non-owned files are protected, generated models type-check in the adoption smoke, and no historical migrations are rewritten.

title: Harden hxruby:controller and hxruby:scaffold generators
description: Add or harden Rails-native controller/scaffold generators so scaffolds compose typed model, migration snapshot, controller, HHX templates, route handling, route extern regeneration, and Rails tests without taking ownership of Rails runtime tasks.
acceptance_criteria: bin/rails generate hxruby:controller Todos index show creates typed controller Haxe source and optional HHX templates; hxruby:scaffold composes model, migration snapshot, controller, HHX templates, route instructions/patching, and test stubs; route handling supports --routes=haxe|snippet|rails|none, where --routes=haxe is default only for greenfield RailsHx apps after haxe.ruby-2pd.5 parity exists, existing/adopted Rails apps default to --routes=snippet or --routes=rails, and no mode silently overwrites unowned config/routes.rb; safe route patching/marker ownership remains opt-in and refuses missing/heavily customized files; scaffold help documents compile, db:migrate, hxruby:routes, and rails test flow; generated scaffold compiles, migrates, renders HHX, passes request/model tests, and keeps existing Ruby/ERB files untouched unless explicitly adopted.
notes:
Routing mutation from GPT 5.5 Pro design: scaffold route defaults must wait for haxe.ruby-2pd.5 route sync/parity. Greenfield RailsHx can eventually default to Haxe-owned routes; existing Rails apps stay Rails-owned/snippet by default.

Progress 2026-06-20: added standalone hxruby:controller generator (shared Ruby implementation, Rails generator adapter, repository/Rake/npm/script entrypoints), optional HHX template skeletons, scaffold reuse of the shared controller renderer, package/release contract wiring, and focused controller/scaffold/gem smokes. Remaining work: richer scaffold request/model test stubs, route-patch safety polish, and end-to-end scaffold runtime coverage before closing.

Progress 2026-06-20: scaffold now generates Haxe-authored Rails model test stubs by default under test_haxe/**, wires them into Main/build.hxml, emits ordinary Rails/Minitest under test/generated/**, exposes --skip-tests through script/Rails/Rake entrypoints, and smoke-tests the generated Haxe source, manifest entry, build opt-out, and Ruby test artifact. Remaining work: richer request/controller scaffold tests, route-patch safety polish, and end-to-end scaffold runtime coverage before closing.

Progress 2026-06-20: scaffold --controller now composes typed HHX view skeletons through the shared controller generator. Model-backed index actions render Template.of(IndexView) with typed Array<Model> locals, and smoke coverage asserts the generated Haxe view plus emitted Rails controller/ERB output. Remaining work: richer request/controller scaffold tests, route-patch safety polish, and end-to-end scaffold runtime coverage before closing.

Progress 2026-06-20: added scaffold runtime coverage. The smoke now materializes a minimal Rails app from generated scaffold output, syntax-checks generated Ruby, runs app-local bin/rails db:migrate and bin/rails test when Rails gems are available, and covers HHX index rendering plus create/strong-params request behavior.
close_reason: Completed controller/scaffold generator hardening: standalone controller generator with HHX templates, scaffold composition of typed model/migration/controller/HHX routes/tests, route modes, generated Haxe test stubs, generated Rails output assertions, and a scaffold runtime harness that migrates, renders HHX output, and runs Rails model/request tests.

title: Implement bin/rails generate hxruby:model
description: Add a Rails-native model generator that creates typed Haxe ActiveRecord contracts and, by default, a production-safe create-table migration snapshot inferred from the command attributes.
acceptance_criteria: bin/rails generate hxruby:model Todo title:string completed:boolean user:references creates src_haxe/models/Todo.hx with typed model metadata, field refs, association metadata where unambiguous, and a create-table Haxe migration snapshot unless --skip-migration is passed; generated migration does not live-depend on models.Todo after creation; options cover --skip-migration, --known-models, validation flags such as --validate title,presence and --validate email,uniqueness, package/output root, --pretend, and --force; re-running without ownership markers fails safely; generated Haxe compiles to normal ApplicationRecord subclass plus timestamped migration; Rails integration app can migrate and run model tests for the generated model.
close_reason: Implemented Rails-native hxruby:model generator with typed ActiveRecord source, association/validation metadata, snapshot migration delegation, Rails adapter, CLI script, docs, and compile/package smoke coverage.

title: Implement bin/rails generate hxruby:migration
description: Add the Rails-native migration generator that writes Haxe-authored migration snapshot source and relies on Rails to execute the generated ActiveRecord migration after hxruby:compile.
acceptance_criteria: bin/rails generate hxruby:migration AddStatusToTodos status:string creates safe Haxe migration source and compiles to add_column Ruby; CreateTodos title:string completed:boolean user:references creates a snapshot CreateTable/Reference migration; Remove*/Drop*/raw/data migration paths require explicit reversible or unsafe APIs; Rails-style attributes support string!, boolean, decimal{precision,scale}, references/belongs_to, index, uniq/unique, polymorphic where supported; options include --timestamp, --haxe-dir, --package, --migration-version, --known-models, --external-table, --from-schema, --pretend, and --force where safe; generator refuses existing timestamps/classes, unsafe paths, unknown tables unless external/schema/known metadata is supplied, and non-owned db/migrate collisions; docs/help show Haxe source, generated Ruby, and the Rails db:migrate flow.
close_reason: Implemented Rails-native hxruby:migration generator with shared Ruby implementation, Rails adapter, CLI script, Rails-style attribute parsing, snapshot MigrationOperation output, ownership/collision checks, docs, todoapp dogfood updates, and smoke/package coverage.

title: Add production Rails migration snapshot operations
description: Extend the RailsHx migration DSL from model-derived create-table artifacts into production-safe operation snapshots. Generator-produced migrations should be explicit historical records that do not drift when current model metadata changes.
acceptance_criteria: MigrationOperation supports CreateTable, Column, Reference, Index, AddReference, RemoveReference, RenameColumn, RenameTable, ChangeNull, AddCheckConstraint, RemoveCheckConstraint, and explicit unsafe ExecuteSql/DataMigration-style operations with rollback requirements; composite indexes, decimal precision/scale, column limits, references/belongs_to, nullable/default/index/unique options, and migration version configuration are represented; generated Ruby matches Rails create_table/add_reference/add_index/change_null/rename_* shapes; existing models:[...] compatibility remains but docs mark snapshot operations as production-preferred; negative tests cover duplicate timestamps, unsafe table/column/index names, unsupported references, missing rollback for destructive/raw operations, and old migration output not changing after model metadata edits.
close_reason: Implemented production snapshot migration operations with Rails-native lowering, version metadata, constraints, raw/data rollback checks, docs, and smoke coverage.

title: Define RailsHx generator ownership and manifest contract
description: Define and implement the generated-artifact ownership contract for RailsHx generators and compiler output. RailsHx must know which Rails files it owns, refuse unsafe overwrites, record source/output pairs, and preserve Rails-owned app files for gradual adoption.
acceptance_criteria: docs/railshx-generated-artifact-ownership.md or equivalent design docs define Rails-owned vs RailsHx-owned files, generated headers, manifest location, and force/repair policy; generators and compiler writes refuse to overwrite non-owned db/migrate, app/views, config/routes, app/models, app/controllers, assets, or generated output paths unless the file is manifest-owned or --force/repair is explicit; .railshx/manifest.json or equivalent records source/output/kind/checksum pairs for generated migrations/templates/tests/client assets; tests cover non-owned collision, manifest-owned rewrite, unsafe path rejection, and clean/remove behavior; AGENTS/docs link this contract from generator and production-readiness guidance.
close_reason: Implemented RailsHx generated artifact ownership: manifest-backed generator writes, safe manifest-owned rewrites, non-owned collision refusal, cleanup primitive for future hxruby:clean, compiler-side Rails extra-file collision checks for migrations/tests/initializer, docs, and generator smoke coverage.

title: Implement minimal Haxe-authored Rails Minitest output
description: Implement the first RailsHx typed Haxe test slice: @:railsTest classes and basic assertion helpers lowering to ordinary Rails Minitest files under a generated test root.
acceptance_criteria: std/rails/test exposes ModelTestCase and Assert helpers; @:railsTest("path") emits ordinary test/generated/**/*_test.rb Minitest files; generated output has snapshots; todoapp includes at least one Haxe-authored model test in parallel with vanilla Rails fixtures; vanilla Rails tests still run unchanged.
notes: Implemented compiler-erased @:railsTests define() DSL, Assert/Dsl/ModelTestCase facades, todoapp sample, snapshots, and docs.
close_reason: Implemented compiler-erased @:railsTests define() DSL, Assert/Dsl/ModelTestCase facades, todoapp sample, snapshots, and docs.

title: Implement Haxe-authored Rails request test helpers
description: Add typed Haxe authoring helpers for Rails request/controller tests that lower to ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest.
acceptance_criteria: RequestTestCase supports get/post/patch/delete, assertResponse, assertRedirectedTo, assertDifference/assertNoDifference, and typed params/route helper usage; generated output lowers to ordinary Rails request tests; todoapp includes a Haxe-authored controller/request test in parallel with vanilla Rails fixtures; snapshots and Rails runtime lanes cover the output.
close_reason: Implemented Haxe-authored Rails request test helpers: RequestTestCase lowers to ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest, get/post/patch/delete request helpers emit Rails-native calls, assertResponse/assertRedirectedTo/assertDifference/assertNoDifference lower to ordinary Rails assertions, typed RequestParams.model validates model-backed request param keys and emits Rails params hashes, todoapp includes a Haxe-authored request test, snapshots cover generated output, and runtime Rails todoapp tests pass. Verified npm run todoapp:test and npm run test:todoapp-rails.

title: Move todoapp Rails tests out of JS materializers
description: Normalize examples/todoapp_rails Rails test fixtures so Ruby tests live as ordinary Rails files instead of JS string templates inside materializer scripts.
acceptance_criteria: Todoapp Rails test files live under the example as normal Ruby fixtures; scripts/rails/todoapp.js and scripts/ci/rails-integration.js copy those fixtures into the disposable Rails app; generated-app tests and docs still pass; no large Ruby test bodies remain embedded in JS materializers.
close_reason: Moved todoapp Rails tests out of JS materializers into ordinary Rails fixture files under examples/todoapp_rails/rails/test. Both materializers now copy the fixture tree into the disposable Rails app. Verified no Todo/User/controller test classes remain embedded in JS, and todoapp/static/runtime lanes still pass.

title: Audit todoapp Rails-style dogfood test coverage
description: Retroactively audit examples/todoapp_rails as the canonical RailsHx dogfood app. The todoapp should behave like a small well-tested Rails app while also serving as an end-to-end compiler sentinel.
acceptance_criteria: Todoapp coverage is mapped across generated Rails model/unit tests, controller/request tests, Rails integration/runtime gates, production smoke, and Playwright E2E; missing feature-to-test-layer gaps are either implemented or filed as child beads; docs state which command proves each layer and future RailsHx features that the todoapp illustrates update the appropriate Rails-style test in the same change.
close_reason: Completed the retroactive todoapp dogfood coverage audit. The generated Rails app now has stronger Rails-style model/unit tests for validations, scopes, and associations; controller/request tests for ordered open-work rendering, completed-record filtering, typed strong params, ignored unpermitted fields, invalid submissions, and redirects; docs map compiler/static, Rails model/request, runtime, browser, and production layers to commands. Verified with test:todoapp-rails, test:rails-integration, REQUIRE_RAILS=1 test:rails-integration under rbenv Ruby 3.3.11, todoapp:test, release contracts, and diff check.

title: Design typed ActionCable connection auth and identifier APIs
description: Design typed RailsHx support for ActionCable connection classes, identified_by declarations, current-user/session/cookie auth seams, and reject_unauthorized_connection. Preserve Rails-native ActionCable output and expose typed connection identifiers to channels where practical.
acceptance_criteria: Docs and examples show the supported typed connection/auth path; generated Ruby remains normal ActionCable::Connection::Base/Channel::Base shape; Rails connection or channel tests cover accepted and rejected connections; unsupported auth patterns are explicitly documented with escape hatches or follow-up beads.
notes: Progress 2026-06-20: documented the intended Rails-native connection/auth design in docs/railshx-action-cable-guide.md, including @:railsCableConnection, static identifiers = { identifiedBy(...) }, typed ConnectionIdentifier<T>, assign/connection access, rejectUnauthorizedConnection(), generated Ruby shape, supported MVP, deferred auth patterns, and the Rails ActionCable::Connection::TestCase coverage requirement. Implementation/test work split to haxe.ruby-bee.1 and completed in the same session.
close_reason: Designed and implemented typed ActionCable connection auth/identifier APIs: docs/examples show @:railsCableConnection, typed identifiers/request params, assignment/access, rejection, generated Rails-native Ruby, deferred auth seams, and runtime connection tests for accepted/rejected connections.

title: Audit RailsHx smoke-only surfaces for snapshot-first coverage
description: Review RailsHx surfaces whose main generated-output contract is currently guarded mostly by smoke/runtime tests, then add or extend committed snapshots for canonical generated Ruby/ERB/JS shape. Keep smoke tests focused on negative typing, syntax, and Rails consumption seams rather than duplicating snapshot output checks.
acceptance_criteria: A testing audit lists RailsHx surfaces with snapshot coverage and gaps; ActionCable/Turbo/generator surfaces have explicit snapshot coverage or linked follow-up rationale; docs state snapshot-first policy and smoke/runtime supporting roles; npm run test:snapshots remains green.
notes: Snapshot-first smoke audit progressed: components, Turbo Streams, ActiveSupport instrumentation, and Rails engine/plugin output now have committed snapshot coverage. Their smoke scripts were reduced to justified non-snapshot checks: required files where useful, Ruby syntax, negative compile/type-safety diagnostics, optional runtime consumption seams, unsafe path rejection, and generator CLI behavior. Remaining smoke-heavy surface: Rails interop/adoption.
close_reason: Completed snapshot-first audit: ActionCable, components, Turbo Streams, ActiveSupport instrumentation, Rails engine/plugin, and Rails interop/adoption now have committed generated-output snapshots. Corresponding smoke scripts were trimmed to non-snapshot value: syntax, negative typing/fail-closed checks, runtime consumption seams, unsafe-path checks, materialization, and generator/package behavior. Testing strategy documents snapshot-first policy and justified smoke/check-only surfaces.

title: Design typed ActiveStorage direct uploads and blob facades
description: Design RailsHx typed APIs for direct-upload seams, blob signed IDs, blob metadata/content-type/filename facades, and generated helper ergonomics. Preserve Rails-native ActiveStorage behavior while giving Haxe users typed direct-upload and blob contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Docs and examples show the supported direct-upload/blob path; compile-time types distinguish signed IDs, blobs, filenames, content types, and attachment refs where practical; generated Rails runtime or browser coverage validates at least one direct-upload/blob helper path; unsupported ActiveStorage-owned details are linked to follow-up beads with rationale.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActiveStorage blob/signed-id/direct-upload facades, HHX file_field lowering, docs/examples, package inventory, and Rails runtime smoke coverage.

title: Harden typed ActiveStorage variants previews validations and attachable builders
description: Extend RailsHx ActiveStorage beyond signed-ID attach/read/purge with typed variants/previews, attachment validations, analyzer hooks, purge_later decisions, and builders for common hash attachables such as io/filename/content_type. Keep attachUnchecked explicit for rare Rails-owned shapes.
acceptance_criteria: Typed builders replace common unchecked attachable hash usage; examples cover one and many attachments; runtime Rails tests assert at least one variant/preview or validation path where supported locally; docs state analyzer/service assumptions and explicit escape hatches.
close_reason: Added typed ActiveStorage Attachable and Attachables facades for signed IDs and common io/filename/content_type hash attachables, lowered them to Rails-native attach(...) receiver calls, updated the active_storage example/docs/inventory/package metadata, and extended active-storage smoke/runtime tests to assert typed hash attachable output and Rails runtime attachment behavior. Variants/previews/validations/analyzer hooks remain follow-up work.

title: Harden ActionMailer attachments and mailer-job integration
description: Extend RailsHx ActionMailer beyond string attachments by designing typed builders for common Rails attachment hashes, inline attachments, content types, and mailer delivery through ActiveJob. Keep unchecked interop explicit for unusual ActionMailer-owned shapes.
acceptance_criteria: Typed attachment builders cover common hash/inline/content-type paths or are explicitly deferred; runtime Rails tests assert at least one non-string attachment case; docs show ActionMailer + ActiveJob delivery workflow; unchecked attachment interop remains named and documented.
close_reason: Added typed ActionMailer attachment values for string, Rails attachment-hash, and inline attachment paths; compiler lowering now emits Rails-native attachments[...] and attachments.inline[...] assignment, runtime ActionMailer tests assert a typed inline CSV attachment with mime type, MessageDelivery.deliverLater returns a typed ActiveJob enqueue handle, and docs/inventory/compatibility/security audit describe the supported ActiveJob boundary and unchecked interop escapes.

title: Design typed ActionMailer parameterized mailers and previews
description: Design and implement or explicitly defer typed RailsHx support for ActionMailer.with(...), params access, preview classes, and generated preview/test-helper UX. The API should lower to ordinary Rails parameterized mailers and previews while preserving typed Haxe locals/options.
acceptance_criteria: Docs and examples show the supported parameterized mailer/preview path; compile-time checks reject missing params/templates where possible; generated Rails runtime tests or preview smoke coverage exist under REQUIRE_RAILS=1; unsupported Rails preview or parameterized cases are linked to follow-up beads with rationale.
close_reason: Completed typed ActionMailer parameterized mailers and previews: docs/examples show @:railsMailerParams, typed .with(...) wrappers, params[:key] reads, Haxe-authored ActionMailer previews, generated preview snapshots/runtime coverage, and hxruby:mailer generator UX. Remaining richer attachment/job work is tracked separately under haxe.ruby-4tx.

title: Broaden RailsHx Turbo frame/stream browser coverage
description: Extend Turbo/Hotwire production coverage beyond the current todoapp submit/header path. Cover Turbo Frame helpers, stream morph/refresh semantics, additional server-side stream helper shapes, and browser-visible failure paths while keeping behavior-bearing names typed.
acceptance_criteria: Playwright or Rails runtime tests cover representative Turbo Frame and Turbo Stream flows beyond todo creation; stream targets/names remain typed or explicit escapes; unsupported Rails helper shapes are documented with follow-up beads.
close_reason: Added typed HHX turbo_frame support, todoapp frame dogfood flow, Playwright frame/fallback coverage, and docs/AGENTS guidance for reusable RailsHx Hotwire helpers.

title: Harden RailsHx ActionCable connection lifecycle and rejection semantics
description: Extend the ActionCable runtime lane beyond subscribe/stream/perform/broadcast. Cover connection identifiers, confirmed/rejected subscription behavior, stream stop/unsubscribe lifecycle, and typed reject/auth seams while preserving Rails-native ActionCable output.
acceptance_criteria: Generated Rails channel tests cover connection identifiers or stubs, reject/confirm behavior, and stream stop/unsubscribe lifecycle; unsupported auth/connection patterns are documented with explicit deferrals; tests run under REQUIRE_RAILS=1.
close_reason: Added committed ActionCable snapshots for generated channel output; expanded generated Rails ActionCable runtime tests for connection stubs/identifiers, subscription rejection, confirmed stream subscription, unsubscribe stream cleanup, perform, and broadcast; documented snapshot-first testing policy and deferred richer typed connection/auth APIs to haxe.ruby-bee.

title: Harden RailsHx ActiveJob adapters, serialization, and retry/discard runtime behavior
description: Extend the ActiveJob production lane beyond test-adapter queue/enqueue/perform assertions. Cover adapter-specific behavior where practical, serialization/deserialization contracts for typed perform args, retry_on/discard_on runtime semantics, queue naming policy, and failure diagnostics while preserving Rails-native ActiveJob output.
acceptance_criteria: Generated Rails runtime tests cover typed job serialization and at least one retry/discard behavior; queue names are centralized or explicitly documented as checked literals; unsupported adapter/runtime edge cases are documented with safe deferrals; tests run under REQUIRE_RAILS=1.
close_reason: Added generated RetryProbeJob runtime coverage for Rails retry_on re-enqueue behavior, ActiveJob serialization/deserialization assertions for typed perform args, docs for supported adapter/queue-name policy and deferrals, refreshed snapshots/gap report, and verified the ActiveJob Rails runtime lane locally. Follow-up adapter/discard hardening is tracked separately.

title: Harden RailsHx ActiveStorage runtime storage helpers and direct-upload seams
description: Move ActiveStorage from typed attachment refs/static lowering toward production runtime confidence. Cover attachment validations, blob/service assumptions, variants/previews, purge/analyze helpers, direct-upload seams, and typed builders for common attachable hash shapes.
acceptance_criteria: Generated Rails runtime tests prove attach/read/purge for one and many attachments under REQUIRE_RAILS=1; direct-upload and variant/blob helper gaps are implemented or explicitly deferred; unchecked attachable shapes have typed replacements or documented escape-hatch beads; docs state supported storage assumptions.
close_reason: Added generated Rails ActiveStorage runtime tests for one/many attachment signed-ID attach, download/read, and purge using ActiveRecord, ActiveStorage tables, SQLite, and test disk storage; wired test:active-storage into test:rails-runtime and release contracts; documented supported storage assumptions and deferred direct-upload/blob/variant/validation/builder work to haxe.ruby-33p and haxe.ruby-9sl.

title: Harden RailsHx ActionMailer previews, parameterized mailers, and delivery tests
description: Move ActionMailer from compiler/static coverage toward production runtime confidence. Add typed support or explicit deferrals for previews, parameterized mailers, delivery/test helpers, multipart defaults, attachment edge cases, and mailer/job integration.
acceptance_criteria: Generated Rails app has ActionMailer preview or delivery tests under REQUIRE_RAILS=1; parameterized mailer support is implemented or explicitly deferred with rationale; multipart and attachment behavior is covered by runtime assertions or beads; docs state supported production mailer path.
close_reason: Added generated Rails ActionMailer runtime tests for multipart mail, headers, string attachments, and deliver_now; wired test:action-mailer into test:rails-runtime and release contracts; documented supported production boundary and deferred parameterized/previews plus richer attachment/job work to haxe.ruby-z34 and haxe.ruby-4tx.

title: Harden SQL/string policy CI gate for RailsHx canonical sources
description: Strengthen npm run test:sql-string-policy so it checks canonical RailsHx source and docs for accidental raw query strings, verifies every allowed explicit SQL/string escape is documented in the escape-hatch audit, and keeps future Sql.* work auditable. The gate should keep raw ActiveRecord query fragments rejected unless a line carries a deliberate railshx:allow-raw-sql-example marker inside policy docs/examples.
close_reason: Hardened npm run test:sql-string-policy to scan canonical Haxe examples plus selected Markdown code fences for raw ActiveRecord query strings, requiring deliberate railshx:allow-raw-sql-example markers for documented negative examples. The gate now also asserts explicit escape hatches @:railsAllowRawErb, Template.external, Lock.custom, and externalTables are documented in the escape-hatch audit.

title: Add explicit RailsHx Sql escape hatch APIs
description: Define explicit, auditable SQL escape hatches for cases the typed Expr/Predicate builders cannot yet represent. Candidate APIs include Sql.raw/fragment/literal with scary names, strict docs, examples, and negative tests proving casual strings remain rejected on where/order/select/group/having. Prefer typed builders first; raw SQL must be opt-in and easy for audits/LLMs to find.
notes: Implementing explicit RailsHx SQL escape hatch APIs: Sql<TModel,TKind>, SqlWhere/SqlOrder phantom kinds, whereSql/whereNotSql/orderSql/reorderSql relation entrypoints, compiler lowering, smoke tests, docs, and SQL policy coverage.
close_reason: Implemented explicit RailsHx SQL escape hatch APIs: Sql<TModel,TKind> with SqlWhere/SqlOrder phantom kinds, whereSql/whereNotSql/orderSql/reorderSql, compiler lowering to Rails query methods, negative smoke tests for plain strings/wrong owner/wrong kind, docs, inventory, SQL policy gate updates, and refreshed snapshots.

title: Implement RailsHx Expr and Predicate core
description: Add rails.active_record.Expr<TModel,TValue> and Predicate<TModel> as typed Haxe-only tokens. Support Expr.field(...), Expr.lower(...), comparison predicate methods, Relation.whereExpr(...), Relation.whereNotExpr(...), and compiler lowering to Rails/Arel-backed Ruby. Keep casual strings fail-closed: no from String conversions, owner/value-type mismatches fail at Haxe compile time, and examples include negative compile tests.
notes: Implementing first concrete RailsHx typed expression slice from the SQL/string policy: Expr<TModel,TValue>, Predicate<TModel>, Relation.whereExpr/whereNotExpr, and typed lower(...) ordering. This bead is intentionally narrow; richer functions, aggregate aliases, having, and explicit Sql.* escapes are tracked separately.
close_reason: Implemented RailsHx Expr<TModel,TValue> and Predicate<TModel> tokens, model/relation whereExpr/whereNotExpr stubs, compiler lowering to Rails/Arel predicates, positive ActiveRecord examples, negative compile coverage for raw strings/wrong owners/wrong value types/non-predicate shapes, docs, inventory, and snapshots.

title: Design RailsHx typed query expression layer backed by Arel
description: Design a stable RailsHx Expr/Predicate query layer for SQL function ordering and predicates. Public Haxe API should expose typed Expr<TModel,TValue>, Predicate<TModel>, and narrow builders such as Expr.lower(Todo.f.title), not raw Arel. Document tradeoffs for model ownership, value typing, relation chaining, generated Ruby shape, and interaction with the SQL/string escape policy. Include examples for order, whereExpr, whereNotExpr, future select/group/having, and explicit Sql.* escapes.
notes: Design remains open for the broader expression layer. First implemented slice establishes the public direction: RailsHx Expr/Predicate are typed Haxe APIs, Arel is generated Ruby backend detail, casual strings stay rejected, and future work should add typed builders/aggregates/having before explicit Sql.* escapes. Use the active_record_model smoke and docs/railshx-query-guide.md as the current contract.
close_reason: Completed RailsHx typed query expression design: documented Expr<TModel,TValue>/Predicate<TModel> as the public API, Arel as generated Ruby backend detail, model/value type safety, SQL escape policy through Sql.unsafe*, generated Ruby shape, and constraints for projection/grouping/having follow-up work. Linked from query guide, SQL/string policy, projection/grouping design, and roadmap; sql-string-policy CI now guards the design doc.

title: Implement typed SQL function ordering via RailsHx Expr.lower
description: Implement typed function ordering such as Todo.order(Expr.lower(Todo.f.title).asc()) and Order.lower(Todo.f.title).asc(). Lower generated Ruby to model.arel_table[:column].lower.asc so app code avoids raw order strings like "LOWER(title) ASC". Add smoke tests and docs showing that lower only accepts String fields from the same model owner.
close_reason: Implemented typed SQL function ordering with Expr.lower(Todo.f.title).asc() and Order.lower(...).asc(), lowering to Models::Todo.arel_table[:title].lower.asc. Added smoke assertions, negative compile tests for raw order strings/wrong model/non-string fields, docs, inventory, and snapshots.

title: Integrate RailsHx ActiveRecord SQL/string escape policy
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-bjv.3.1. Apply the approved SQL/string-bearing API policy to ActiveRecord query APIs that cannot be fully represented by field refs, such as raw where/order/select/group/having fragments and Arel-like expressions.
acceptance_criteria: ActiveRecord raw SQL/string APIs are either replaced by typed builders or explicit audited escape hatches; strict-boundary tests reject casual strings/raw SQL in production examples; docs explain safe usage and generated Ruby shape.
notes: RailsHx SQL/string policy hardening now includes typed Aggregate expression builders, Group.countHaving aggregate having predicates, and Projection.group selected grouped aggregate result rows. Raw having/projection strings remain rejected in examples/tests; remaining query SQL policy work is richer grouped keys, joined aggregate projections, broader SQL function builders, and any explicit Sql.* escapes for gaps.
close_reason: Completed ActiveRecord SQL/string policy hardening: raw SQL remains behind explicit Sql.unsafe* escapes; typed criteria/field refs cover normal where/order/projection/group/having paths; added fluent FieldTools helpers such as Todo.f.title.lower().eq("ship") and Todo.f.id.count().gt(1), compiler lowering to Rails/Arel, positive/negative smoke coverage, docs, inventory/package coverage, and snapshots.

title: Add RailsHx nested association criteria and nested joins/includes
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-bjv.3.1. Extend typed ActiveRecord criteria and association refs beyond flat owner fields so RailsHx can express nested association criteria and nested includes/joins/preload/eager_load shapes without raw strings.
acceptance_criteria: Typed nested association criteria and nested association loading APIs are designed, implemented, documented, and covered by active_record_model compile/runtime-shape tests; wrong association owners and invalid nested fields fail during Haxe compilation.
notes: Implemented typed nested association loading slice: Association.nested(parent, child) with Haxe owner/target chain validation; model/relation preload and eagerLoad APIs; compiler lowering to Rails includes({user: :todos}), preload({user: :todos}), and eager_load({user: :todos}); active_record_model positive/negative smoke coverage and docs. Remaining under this bead: nested association criteria design/implementation for where/findBy/exists-style filters.
close_reason: Implemented typed nested association criteria for where/findBy/exists with nested Rails hash lowering, active_record_model docs/examples, snapshot coverage, and invalid nested field/type compile-failure smoke tests.

title: Add RailsHx production scope declaration DSL
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-bjv.3.1. Add a Rails-shaped typed scope declaration layer beyond ad-hoc static methods, including lambda-like scope builders, optional default_scope decisions, and generated Ruby scope shape.
acceptance_criteria: Typed scope declarations compose as ActiveRecord relations, preserve IntelliSense and return types, emit recognizable Rails scope/default_scope code where approved, and have positive/negative active_record_model tests.
close_reason: Implemented typed Rails scope declarations with @:railsScope and @:railsDefaultScope, Rails scope/default_scope artifact emission, active_record_model positive and negative coverage, snapshots, and docs.

title: Harden RailsHx generator and LLM-assisted inference security
description: Lock down generator-assisted Rails adoption so inferred contracts are deterministic, reviewable, and fail-closed.
acceptance_criteria: RBS/YARD/Ripper/Rails metadata inputs fail closed when missing or unsafe; discovery modes remain suggest-only unless explicitly writing checked wrappers; LLM assistance is documented as advisory patches only; generated Dynamic placeholders are marked for review; tests cover missing files, overwrite protection, and no execution of app code during inference.
close_reason: Completed generator/LLM-assisted inference hardening: adoption inputs that emit Haxe source now validate package names, Ruby constants, template paths, local names, and Haxe type strings; source/RBS/extension files must stay inside the app/output root and fail closed when missing; source-backed inference remains Ripper/RBS parsing only and does not execute app code; existing wrappers remain overwrite-protected; smoke coverage asserts unsafe inputs, missing files, overwrite protection, Dynamic review comments, and parser-only behavior. LLM assistance remains documented as advisory only.

title: Harden RailsHx template/path escape hatch enforcement
description: Make all RailsHx template/path escape hatches auditable and fail-closed by default.
acceptance_criteria: Template.of/layout/existing remain the default checked paths; Template.named/external and @:railsTemplate string paths are literal-only, safe-shape checked, and documented as lower-level escapes; missing external files fail closed where discovery is possible; unchecked escapes require comments/docs and negative tests; canonical samples avoid unchecked paths.
notes: Template/path hardening progressed: Component.of/existing validate slot names at macro time, Component.existing and Template.existing have missing-file negative coverage, and renderTemplateWithLayout now requires a typed Layout token. Template.layout(ViewClass) remains the default class-backed layout path; lower-level literals must use checked Layout.named(...); raw layout strings are rejected in todoapp smoke. Remaining: broader unchecked Template.named/external policy docs/tests and any additional generated-template path surfaces.
close_reason: Completed template/path escape hardening: Template.of/layout/existing remain the checked defaults; Template.named/external and @:railsTemplate paths are literal-only and safe-shape checked; Template.existing/Component.existing fail closed against app/views or rails/app/views; raw layout strings and unsafe paths have negative coverage; unchecked Template.external remains documented as a lower-level escape. This slice also rejects raw backslash paths across Template, Component, ViewMacro, MailerMacro, and HHX compiler lowering, with todoapp smoke regression coverage.

title: Harden RailsHx Dynamic boundary policy and replacements
description: Audit app-facing RailsHx Dynamic usage and replace ordinary production paths with typed facades, abstracts, or generated contracts.
