acceptance_criteria: Dynamic usage is classified as runtime-required, compiler/internal, interop escape, or replacement target; canonical samples avoid app-level Dynamic except documented JS/Rails runtime boundaries; route params, Turbo details, ActionCable subscriptions, ActiveSupport subscriptions, and generator-produced externs have typed replacements or child beads.
notes: Implemented typed Rails route helper params with rails.routing.RouteParam. Added relation-level ActiveRecord find Dynamic hardening: Relation.find now uses Int/String overloads instead of Dynamic, rejects object-shaped IDs in active_record_model negative compile coverage, and emits direct Rails find(...) calls without helper runtime. Added Turbo client boundary hardening: TurboTypedEvent/TurboSubmitEvent expose EventTarget targets, Turbo.addFetchRequestHeader(...) wraps runtime-owned fetchOptions mutation, todoapp client no longer uses app-level Dynamic for submit targets/header mutation/storage catches, and turbo/todoapp smokes cover the helper. Added ActionCable client consumer hardening: Consumer.create() returns a nominal Consumer handle, Consumer.subscribe/TodosCableClient.subscribe require Consumer instead of Dynamic, raw object consumers fail in action-cable smoke, and docs/audit/inventory record the boundary. Added ActionCable perform hardening: Subscription is now an opaque JS handle facade, perform(...) requires typed Action<TData> tokens and matching payloads, performUnchecked(...) is the explicit legacy interop escape, and action-cable smoke rejects raw action strings/wrong payloads. Added ActionMailer options/attachments hardening: MailAddress and MailLayout replace Dynamic recipient/layout fields, MailRenderOptions types render kwargs, Attachments.add accepts typed string content, unchecked mailer escapes are explicit, action-mailer smoke rejects raw recipient objects/non-string attachments, and compiler keyword cleanup keeps abstract-backed kwargs Rails-shaped. Added ActiveSupport subscription handle hardening: Subscription is now opaque without implicit from Dynamic, unsubscribe({}) fails in instrumentation smoke, and docs/audit/inventory document the handle boundary. Added ActiveStorage attachable hardening: One.attach accepts String, Many.attach accepts Array<String>, raw Rails attachable payloads require explicit attachUnchecked(...), invalid object-shaped default attach fails in active-storage smoke, and docs/audit/inventory document the escape. Added controller Dynamic boundary hardening: direct ActionController render/redirect helpers now use RenderOptions/RedirectOptions with Status-typed status kwargs; redirectTo(String) remains the Rails path/location helper; redirectToOptions(...) handles Rails option-hash redirects; raw string statuses fail in action-controller negative compile coverage. Added request format Dynamic hardening: request().format() now returns typed RequestFormat instead of Dynamic, common MIME predicates are typed, and invalid assignment to String fails in action-controller negative compile coverage. Remaining Dynamic work includes generator-inferred review placeholders, broad Rails-owned render payloads such as json/locals/layout, and deeper controller lifecycle surfaces tracked by production gap beads.
close_reason: Completed Dynamic boundary hardening pass: app-facing Dynamic uses are classified in the escape-hatch audit, canonical RailsHx APIs use typed facades/abstracts/generated contracts, and remaining runtime-owned/generator/controller surfaces are documented under production follow-up beads. This slice also removes ActiveJob helper Dynamic returns: performNow preserves perform return type, performLater returns rails.active_job.Base, and active-job smoke now proves valid and invalid return typing.

title: Harden RailsHx SQL/string escape hatch enforcement
description: Turn the SQL/string-bearing policy into enforcement for RailsHx APIs that can carry SQL or behavior-bearing strings.
acceptance_criteria: ActiveRecord/migration/template/Turbo APIs classify strings as typed default, checked literal, or explicit escape; raw SQL fragments use named auditable APIs; strict-boundary tests reject accidental SQL/string drift in canonical samples; docs link each allowed escape to a safer typed alternative or follow-up bead.
notes: Initial enforcement slice complete via haxe.ruby-k25. Explicit SQL escape hatch API slice complete via haxe.ruby-elx. Turbo string-hardening complete for StreamTarget/StreamName. ActionCable string-hardening complete for Stream<TPayload>/SubscriptionParam<TValue>. Template/path hardening includes checked existing-template/component files, macro-validated component slot names, and typed Layout tokens rejecting raw layout strings. Controller lifecycle rescue constants now default to typed exception refs via rescueFrom(Type, handler); rescueFromNamed("Ruby::Constant", handler) is documented as a safe Ruby constant path escape and still validates the handler. Remaining work: broader migration string classifications and follow-up SQL surfaces such as select/group/having once those APIs are expanded.
close_reason: Completed SQL/string escape-hatch enforcement pass: ActiveRecord raw SQL routes through typed Sql.unsafeWhere/unsafeOrder relation APIs, canonical examples reject casual raw query strings, template/path/Turbo/ActionCable/controller string surfaces are classified and guarded, rescueFromNamed remains checked by safe Ruby constant paths, and migrations now validate externalTables as safe Rails table identifiers while preserving typed knownModels/operation checks. Remaining richer select/group/having/aggregate design work stays tracked by haxe.ruby-bjv.10, haxe.ruby-deg, and haxe.ruby-6ri.

title: Design RailsHx typed policy for SQL and string-bearing APIs
description: Define when RailsHx APIs may accept strings/symbols/SQL fragments and when they must require typed refs/builders. This covers ActiveRecord where/order/select/group/having/from/joins, migrations, templates, routes, Turbo targets, and external Rails paths.
acceptance_criteria: A documented policy classifies each string/SQL-bearing surface as typed default, checked literal, or explicit escape hatch; escape hatches are named and auditable; implementation beads for affected APIs reference the policy; strict-boundary tests reject accidental raw SQL/string drift in canonical examples.
close_reason: Documented RailsHx SQL/string-bearing policy with typed default, checked literal, and explicit escape-hatch classes; linked affected implementation beads; added npm run test:sql-string-policy to reject raw query strings in canonical examples.

title: Close RailsHx Turbo, Turbo Streams, and ActionCable production gaps
description: Harden realtime/progressive RailsHx APIs: typed Turbo client helpers, server-side streams, stream targets/names, ActionCable channel params/payloads, subscriptions, and browser/runtime tests.
acceptance_criteria: Turbo frame/stream/client APIs cover common Rails/Hotwire flows without behavior-bearing string drift; ActionCable channels and JS subscriptions have typed params/payloads; Playwright and Rails runtime/channel tests prove representative flows; unsupported raw stream/channel names are explicit escape hatches.
notes: 2026-06-15: Added generated Rails ActionCable runtime lane to npm run test:action-cable. The lane materializes a Rails 7.2.3.1 app, uses ActionCable::Channel::TestCase against the generated channel, and asserts subscription confirmation, typed stream subscription, perform :ping transmissions, and broadcast payloads. It is wired into npm run test:rails-runtime via REQUIRE_RAILS=1. Local non-runtime mode still skips when Rails bundle is unavailable and mandatory mode has Ruby-version preflight. Remaining realtime production gaps are tracked explicitly as haxe.ruby-tke (ActionCable connection identifiers/reject/confirm/stream lifecycle) and haxe.ruby-7bx (broader Turbo frame/stream browser coverage).
close_reason: Closed realtime production-gap umbrella by adding generated Rails ActionCable::Channel::TestCase runtime coverage for subscribe/stream/perform/broadcast, keeping typed Turbo/ActionCable compile/browser gates intact, wiring ActionCable into npm run test:rails-runtime, and filing explicit follow-up beads for deeper ActionCable lifecycle/auth and broader Turbo frame/stream browser coverage.

title: Close RailsHx ActionMailer, ActiveJob, and ActiveStorage production gaps
description: Move background jobs, mailers, and storage from initial typed slices to production-usable RailsHx surfaces with runtime coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Mailer previews/tests, multipart templates, parameterized mailers, job adapters/test helpers, retry/discard semantics, attachment validations, direct-upload/storage helpers, and runtime Rails tests are covered or explicitly deferred with beads; APIs remain Rails-native and typed at Haxe boundaries.
notes: Closed production ActiveJob slice haxe.ruby-71o: generated Rails runtime lane now asserts typed argument serialization/deserialization and retry_on re-enqueue behavior in addition to queue/enqueue/perform. Adapter-specific discard/failure diagnostics remain tracked by follow-up ActiveJob adapter/discard bead.
close_reason: Closed production-gap umbrella by adding a generated Rails ActiveJob runtime lane with ActiveJob::TestHelper queue/enqueue/perform assertions under REQUIRE_RAILS=1, wiring it into npm run test:rails-runtime, documenting the runtime strategy, and filing explicit follow-up beads for remaining ActiveJob adapter/serialization/retry-discard work (haxe.ruby-71o), ActionMailer previews/parameterized/delivery tests (haxe.ruby-if3), and ActiveStorage runtime/direct-upload/storage helpers (haxe.ruby-cfj).

title: Close RailsHx HHX, ActionView, forms, and component production gaps
description: Broaden typed HHX/ActionView coverage for production Rails templates: form builders, collection rendering, layouts, content_for/yield, helpers, partials/components, errors, and interop with Rails-owned ERB.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical templates use HHX by default; common Rails form/helper/component patterns have typed APIs; collection rendering and locals are checked; content_for/layout/slots are covered; Rails-owned ERB interop remains explicit and checked; todoapp/dogfood and negative compile tests cover the new helper surface.
notes: Added typed ActionView form search_field support: HHX <search_field> and H.searchField(...) lower inside form_with to Rails form.search_field with checked field refs and helper attrs. Todoapp now dogfoods the search_field form builder control with ERB and negative compile smoke coverage. Also fixed strict-example drift from typed request MIME/variant tokens by moving raw Rails constants behind non-inline @:rubyAllowRaw std facades and compiler token lowering, then refreshed stale action_controller_params/todoapp snapshots.
close_reason: The documented RailsHx production-ready beta surface now covers the HHX, ActionView, forms, templates, helper tags, and component path required for the closed readiness gate. Remaining broader component ergonomics, stricter assigns, helper-slot breadth, and full-stack Hotwire contracts are tracked explicitly in haxe_ruby-7ls and haxe_ruby-jgn instead of keeping this P1 bucket open.

title: Close RailsHx controller, routing, and request lifecycle production gaps
description: Harden typed ActionController and routing coverage for real apps: before/after/around filters, redirects, respond_to, request variants, rescue handling, auth-friendly hooks, route/controller validation, and request specs.
acceptance_criteria: Controller APIs remain Rails-shaped and typed; route helpers and render/redirect/head/respond_to surfaces reject wrong arguments; request lifecycle hooks are method-backed where possible; errors/rescue and auth-oriented extension points are documented; Rails runtime request tests cover representative flows.
notes: Closed common controller request/response lifecycle blocker: added typed ordered MIME negotiation via rails.action_controller.Mime and request.negotiateMime(...), added typed request.setVariant([RequestVariantToken.*]) assignment, extended action-controller smoke with generated Ruby assertions and negative compile fixtures, refreshed std inventory/gap report/docs. Remaining auth/streaming/custom-CSRF breadth is non-blocking follow-up work.
close_reason: Closed

title: Close RailsHx model, validation, association, and migration production gaps
description: Harden model metadata and migration APIs for production Rails apps: validations, callbacks, associations, enums, schema evolution, indexes, constraints, reversible operations, and schema-history behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Common Rails validations/callbacks/association options are typed and documented; migration operations cover production schema changes including constraints/indexes/foreign keys/reversible paths; destructive or raw migration escapes are explicit; generated migrations pass Rails runtime and production dogfood checks.
notes:
Added a small Haxe-authored Rails request-test ergonomics slice. rails.test.Request now has compiler-erased responseBody() and responseMediaType() helpers lowering to ordinary Rails response.body/response.media_type. Todoapp Haxe request tests now assert rendered body content, completed route output, and file-route media/body responses from Haxe, with smoke expectations covering the generated Minitest. Updated stdlib inventory and Haxe-authored testing docs. Added root AGENTS hard rule that GitHub Actions on the pushed branch must be green before a slice is complete. Local gates passed: npm run test:todoapp-rails; npm run test:snapshots; npm run test:stdlib-inventory; npm run test:gap-report; npm run test:sql-string-policy; git diff --check. Next after push: verify GitHub Actions is green.
Follow-up after GitHub CI exposed runtime gaps on 12eaa3c: Rails validation regexes now lower ^/$ anchors to Rails-safe \A/\z inside @:validates metadata; scope-only HHX <field_errors> now guards form.object before reading errors; todoapp runtime migration no longer uses adapter-specific ValidateForeignKey/ValidateCheckConstraint steps in the SQLite-backed canonical app; Playwright user-role tests now use selectOption for generated <select> controls. Local gates passed: npm run format:haxe:check; npm run test:active-record-model; npm run test:todoapp-rails; npm run test:todoapp-production; npm run test:todoapp-playwright; npm run test:snapshots; npm run test:stdlib-inventory; npm run test:gap-report; npm run test:sql-string-policy; npm run test:rails-runtime. Next after push: verify latest GitHub Actions CI/Gitleaks are green.
close_reason: The documented RailsHx production-ready beta surface now covers the model, validation, association, and migration production path through completed child and follow-up work; the root readiness gate is closed and docs identify future breadth as beta evolution. Remaining migration breadth is tracked explicitly in haxe_ruby-cnu instead of keeping this P1 bucket open.

title: Close RailsHx ActiveRecord query/scope/transaction production gaps
description: Implement the production ActiveRecord query surface beyond the current relation slice: typed scopes, transactions, locking, association-aware criteria, nested joins/includes, raw SQL policy, and safe escape hatches.
acceptance_criteria: Typed scope builders compose like Rails scopes; transactions and locking have Rails-shaped typed APIs; association-aware criteria and nested joins/includes are covered; string/SQL-bearing APIs follow the approved typed-or-explicit escape-hatch policy; active_record_model and Rails runtime/dogfood tests cover representative production queries.
notes: Implemented typed transaction and pessimistic locking slice: Todo.transaction(block, options), TransactionOptions/TransactionIsolation, relation/model lock(?Lock), compiler lowering to Rails transaction/lock calls, active_record_model docs/smoke/snapshots. Added relation-level find hardening: Relation.find now uses Int/String overloads instead of Dynamic while static model find remains primary-key typed. Remaining production gaps are tracked by sibling epic beads haxe.ruby-bjv.8 (nested association criteria/includes), haxe.ruby-bjv.9 (scope DSL), and haxe.ruby-bjv.10 (SQL/string policy integration).
close_reason: Closed the RailsHx ActiveRecord production query spine: typed scopes/default scopes, transactions, pessimistic locks, relation chains, nested association criteria/loading, fluent typed field predicates/aggregates, explicit SQL/string escape policy, active_record_model smoke coverage, docs, and snapshots are in place. Future less-common APIs such as unscope/only/except/references/strict_loading, multi-model projections, and richer grouped keys should be tracked as non-blocking follow-up breadth.

title: Make Rails runtime CI mandatory and broad
description: Harden Rails runtime confidence so production readiness does not rely on optional local skips.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs npm run test:rails-runtime as a required gate on supported Ruby/Rails lanes; missing Rails gems are hard failures in CI; runtime failures identify compiler/materialization/migration/request/browser stage; docs explain local vs CI behavior.
close_reason: Rails runtime CI is mandatory across the supported Ruby matrix, release contracts guard the lane, and runtime/browser smoke logs identify failing stages.

title: Add deployable RailsHx dogfood production app
description: Create or promote a RailsHx app that exercises production build/deploy concerns, not only smoke fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: A deployable app compiles Haxe/HHX/client JS, runs migrations, passes rails test, passes zeitwerk:check and assets:precompile, documents production build steps, and has a CI or scripted smoke proving the release artifact contains generated files.
close_reason: Added a CI-backed RailsHx production dogfood lane that compiles server/client Haxe, runs Rails migrations/tests, passes Zeitwerk/assets precompile, and validates a release archive with generated artifacts.

title: Audit and close RailsHx typed API production gaps
description: Inventory Rails/RailsHx API gaps that block realistic production apps and convert them into typed implementation beads.
acceptance_criteria: Gap audit covers ActiveRecord advanced queries/scopes/transactions/validations/migrations/controllers/templates/jobs/mailers/Turbo/ActionCable/ActiveStorage; each P0/P1 production blocker has a bead; raw string/SQL-bearing APIs have explicit typed or escape-hatch policy before implementation.
close_reason: Documented RailsHx typed API production gap audit, linked it from readiness/compatibility docs, and created P1 implementation beads for each production-blocking API surface plus SQL/string policy.

title: Audit production escape hatches and security policy
description: Make unsafe boundaries explicit before production use: raw Ruby, raw ERB, raw SQL/string clauses, Dynamic, unchecked template paths, external Ruby contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Strict-mode docs and tests cover raw __ruby__, raw ERB, SQL-ish strings, template/path filesystem checks, Dynamic boundaries, and generator inference; canonical samples avoid unsafe escapes; every allowed escape hatch is named, documented, opt-in, and has follow-up beads for typed replacements where appropriate.
close_reason: Documented RailsHx escape-hatch/security audit, linked it from production docs and AGENTS, verified existing strict/path/raw-ERB/adoption gates, and created P1 follow-up beads for SQL/string, Dynamic, path/template, and generator/LLM hardening.

title: Harden RailsHx generator and dev/prod UX
description: Ensure creating, adopting, developing, watching, testing, and producing RailsHx apps feels better than vanilla Rails plus manual Haxe ceremony.
acceptance_criteria: Install/scaffold/adopt/route generators are Ruby-native and tested; generated apps include clear bin/rake/dev/prod workflows; watch/client watch integrate with Rails conventions; errors are actionable; production compile/precompile flow is documented and smoke-tested.
close_reason: Added RailsHx production rake/bin workflow, generator/package/release smoke coverage, and updated dev/prod docs.

title: Implement typed Ruby mixin extension interop
description: Add first-class typed Ruby include/extend/prepend consumption for haxe.ruby. Support extension contract metadata that injects typed instance/static methods into Haxe classes and externs, emit include/extend/prepend for Haxe-owned classes, and cover positive/negative smoke tests. Follow-up monkey-patch using support and generator-assisted adoption separately.
close_reason: Implemented typed Ruby mixin extension contracts with include/prepend/extend emission, examples, docs, snapshots, and smoke coverage.

title: Validate RailsHx migration table and column references
description: Add compile-time validation for typed RailsHx migration operation table/column/index references where metadata is known. Cover unknown table/column diagnostics, DropTable positive reversible coverage, index reference validation, and docs for when external Rails-owned tables remain unchecked.
close_reason: Implemented typed RailsHx migration table/column/index/FK validation with knownModels/externalTables coverage and docs.

title: Validate RailsHx migration ordering and runtime execution
description: Add deterministic migration ordering checks, broader rollback/reversibility validation, and a Rails db:migrate runtime gate for generated RailsHx migrations when app gems are available. Cover invalid timestamp/order cases, foreign-key ordering, irreversible operation policy, and clear skip/require behavior for local environments without Rails gems.
notes: Implemented RailsHx migration registry validation: duplicate timestamps now fail, known foreign-key source/target tables created by later migrations fail, belongs_to-generated foreign keys participate in ordering checks, and rollback-hostile top-level ChangeColumn/RemoveColumn/DropTable operations must be wrapped in explicit Reversible(up, down). Rails integration now syntax-checks the generated update migration and continues to run db:migrate/test when app gems are available or required. Todoapp sample now models reversible change_column/foreign-key rollback and smoke tests cover duplicate timestamp, foreign-key order, and irreversible-operation diagnostics.
close_reason: RailsHx migration ordering, rollback-shape policy, and runtime-gate coverage implemented and covered by npm test.

title: Implement RailsHx reversible migration operations
description: Extend the typed RailsHx migration DSL with explicit reversible/up-down blocks, foreign-key operations, migration ordering checks, richer rollback-shape validation, Rails-native output, and positive/negative smoke coverage.
notes: Implemented typed reversible and foreign-key migration operations: MigrationOperation now includes ChangeColumn, AddForeignKey, RemoveForeignKey, ForeignKeyAction options, and Reversible(up, down). Compiler lowering emits Rails-native change_column, add_foreign_key/remove_foreign_key, and reversible do |dir| blocks from literal Haxe arrays. Todoapp sample/snapshots/smoke cover the output. Remaining migration ordering checks, broader rollback validation, and mandatory Rails db:migrate runtime execution are split into haxe.ruby-wpi.5.4.
close_reason: Typed reversible and foreign-key migration operations implemented and covered by npm test.

title: Implement initial RailsHx alter/drop/index migration operations
description: Add typed RailsHx migration operations beyond create-table-from-model metadata: alter table/add/remove/change columns, add/remove indexes and foreign keys, drop table, reversible/up-down shape where practical, Rails-native output, and positive/negative smoke coverage.
notes: Implemented first typed RailsHx migration operation lane: Haxe-authored operations:Array<MigrationOperation> lowers AddColumn/RemoveColumn/AddIndex/RemoveIndex/DropTable into Rails-native ActiveRecord change statements. Added todoapp sample coverage, generated snapshots, stdlib inventory/package coverage, and a negative smoke that rejects non-literal operation arguments. Remaining reversible/up-down, foreign-key operations, migration ordering, and richer rollback validation are split into a follow-up bead.
close_reason: Initial typed alter/drop/index migration operation DSL implemented and covered by npm test.

title: Validate RailsHx migration metadata
description: Add negative compile coverage for @:railsMigration metadata and tighten validation where needed: timestamp/className/models shape, unknown options, non-model references, duplicate migration outputs, duplicate model tables, and deterministic generated file names.
close_reason: Added RailsHx migration metadata registry validation and negative smoke coverage for duplicate tables, duplicate files, non-model refs, bad timestamps, and unknown options.

title: Support RailsHx through/source associations
description: Add typed RailsHx validation and emission for hasMany/hasOne through/source association options. Validate that through references a model-owned association where resolvable, source is literal and Rails-compatible, emit normal Rails association macros, and add positive/negative smoke coverage.
close_reason: Added typed through/source association option validation and Rails emission with positive and negative ActiveRecord smoke coverage.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActiveRecord callbacks
description: Add typed RailsHx model lifecycle callback metadata/wrappers for Rails callbacks such as before_validation, before_save, after_create, and after_commit. Validate callback method references at compile time, emit idiomatic Rails callback macros, document escape-hatch policy, and cover positive/negative smoke tests.
close_reason: Added method-level RailsHx callback metadata validation, Rails callback emission, docs, and positive/negative ActiveRecord smoke coverage.

title: Support advanced RailsHx association options
description: Extend RailsHx association metadata beyond basic targets/FKs with typed Rails-native options such as dependent, optional, className, foreignKey, inverseOf, and through/source where appropriate. Validate resolvable model/field shapes, emit idiomatic Rails association macros, and add smoke coverage.
close_reason: Added typed association option validation/emission for optional, foreignKey, inverseOf, className, and dependent, with ActiveRecord smoke coverage and docs.

title: Emit typed RailsHx enum metadata
description: Add @:railsEnum model field metadata for Rails ActiveRecord enum declarations. Validate enum metadata at compile time, emit idiomatic Rails enum macros, update the ActiveRecord example, and cover invalid enum shapes in smoke tests.
close_reason: Added @:railsEnum compile-time metadata validation, Rails enum emission, ActiveRecord example coverage, and negative enum smoke tests.

title: Validate RailsHx validation option metadata
description: Add compile-time ModelMacro validation for @:validates option objects: allow Rails-native validation keys with literal/structured values, reject unknown keys and invalid value shapes, and cover diagnostics in ActiveRecord smoke tests.
close_reason: Added compile-time RailsHx validation target, option shape, unknown option, and Validation<T> checks with negative smoke coverage.

title: Validate RailsHx association metadata and foreign keys
description: Add compile-time ModelMacro validation for ActiveRecord association metadata: association fields must target model marker types when resolvable, belongsTo associations must have a matching railsColumn foreign key with compatible Int-like type, and diagnostics must be covered by negative smoke tests.
close_reason: Added compile-time RailsHx association target and belongsTo foreign-key validation with negative ActiveRecord smoke coverage.

title: Preserve RailsHx ActiveRecord criteria typing across Relation chains
description: Make Relation preserve the generated model criteria type across where/order/limit chains so assigned or widened relations still reject unknown fields and wrong value types. Keep Rails-native output and add negative smoke coverage after order/limit and typed variable assignment.
close_reason: Relation now preserves inferred criteria typing across where/order/limit chains, with assigned-relation negative coverage.

title: Make RailsHx runtime lanes explicit and mandatory in CI
description: Add an explicit RailsHx runtime test command/job that forces Rails integration and interop smoke tests to install/run Rails instead of silently skipping. Keep default npm test friendly for local compiler loops and document the Ruby/Rails setup.
close_reason: Added explicit RailsHx runtime command and mandatory CI job, with forced-mode bundle install behavior and docs.

title: Add RailsHx typed includes joins and scopes
description: Build on Relation<T> with typed association-aware includes/joins and model scopes. Use model/association metadata for compile-time validation and lower to normal ActiveRecord includes/joins/scope calls.
close_reason: Added typed ActiveRecord association refs plus includes/joins lowering and wrong-owner negative coverage.

title: Complete RailsHx Relation where/find/findBy typing
description: Extend the initial Relation<T> slice with relation-level typed where criteria plus typed find/findBy APIs. Preserve Rails-native output and add negative tests for unknown fields and wrong value types beyond model static where.
close_reason: Implemented typed model/relation where criteria plus typed find/findBy lowering and negative coverage.

title: Add RailsHx model fields/f aliases for typed field refs
description: Expose generated model field refs through Todo.fields.title and Todo.f.title while keeping Todo.titleField as a compatibility alias. Update todoapp/docs/tests to prefer the nested fields/f API.
close_reason: Added Todo.fields/Todo.f typed field-ref aliases, updated RailsHx todoapp/docs/tests, and kept generated Rails output native.

title: Make RailsHx template references compile-time checked
description: Replace unchecked RailsHx template path strings with compile-time checked template references where possible. Validate Template.named, Template.external path shape, @:railsTemplate paths, and layout names against RailsHx-owned template classes/generated artifacts or explicit external-template declarations.
acceptance_criteria: RailsHx-owned missing/renamed partial/template references fail at Haxe compile time; external templates remain explicit through Template.external and validate safe path shape; todoapp uses typed template/layout references where available; negative tests cover missing template, wrong locals, and unsafe path cases.
close_reason: Implemented checked RailsHx template references with Template.of/layout/existing, path validation, sample conversions, docs, generator update, and negative tests.

title: Make RailsHx form fields and strong params derive from typed model/schema fields
description: Remove duplicated string field names across RailsHx HHX form helpers and ParamsMacro.requirePermit by adding typed model/schema field references or checked field-name macros that lower to Rails snake_case strings/symbols.
acceptance_criteria: Todo form field names and strong params can be authored through typed model/schema field references; unknown fields fail at compile time; generated Rails output remains idiomatic field names and permitted params; negative tests cover unknown fields and wrong model scope.
close_reason: Implemented typed RailsHx ActiveRecord field refs/model params keys for HHX forms and ParamsMacro, with unknown-field and mixed-model negative coverage.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActionView template layer
description: Build a typed Rails template layer inspired by ../haxe.elixir.codex's HXX/HEEx implementation. Provide Haxe-authored typed templates/components that lower to Rails-native Action View artifacts and helpers rather than leaving app views as untyped ERB islands.
acceptance_criteria: Design and implement a RailsHx template DSL/API with typed assigns/locals, typed route/form/helper access where feasible, compile-time validation for field/assign references, generated Rails-native templates or renderable Ruby components, strict escape-hatch policy for raw ERB, docs comparing the approach to HXX/HEEx, and a todoapp sample using the typed template path.
notes: 2026-06-11: Added typed external Rails template interop via Template.external(path):Template<TLocals>. ViewMacro and HHX partial/component lowering now accept Template.external alongside Template.named, preserving typed locals checks while emitting normal Rails render calls and not owning/emitting the external ERB artifact. Added rails_interop_app proving Haxe->legacy ERB, Haxe->legacy Ruby externs, legacy ERB->generated HHX partials, and legacy ERB->generated Haxe service calls. Verified npm run test:rails-interop, REQUIRE_RAILS=1 npm run test:rails-interop under rbenv Ruby 3.3.11, npm run test:todoapp-rails, npm run test:stdlib-inventory, REQUIRE_RAILS=1 npm run test:rails-integration, npm run test:strict-boundaries, ci:version-sync, and ci:release-contracts.
close_reason: Closed the initial RailsHx typed ActionView layer: HHX-first @:railsTemplateAst templates, typed HtmlNode/HtmlAttr/H lower-level AST, typed locals projection, Template.of/layout/existing/external references, checked owned/external template paths, typed partial/component/slot composition, route/link/form helper tags, raw ERB guarded by @:railsAllowRawErb, todoapp coverage, rails_interop_app gradual-adoption coverage, docs, negative tests, snapshots, and package/inventory coverage are in place. Remaining broader ergonomics/framework expansion belongs to follow-up RailsHx tasks rather than this initial layer parent.

title: Add RailsHx end-to-end Rails integration app and CI gate

title: Structuralize Ruby exception lowering and typed catch dispatch
description: After the exhaustive child contract lands, migrate the core TTry/TThrow exception family from raw/print-reembedded Ruby into structural RubyAST while preserving Haxe exception wrapping, Ruby rescue behavior, value position, and cleanup ordering.
design: Treat exception flow as one vertical Pattern B feature. Add only earned structural Ruby begin/rescue and raise syntax, update the authoritative child schema, and compile pre-filter Haxe catches into one StandardError rescue with ordered typed dispatch over the unwrapped Haxe value. Dynamic binds the unwrapped value; exact haxe.Exception binds an HxException.caught adapter that exposes the typed Haxe accessor while retaining the original Ruby carrier; unmatched catches use bare re-raise so Ruby preserves identity and backtrace. TThrow evaluates once through HxException.wrap, and rethrowing a wildcard adapter restores the native exception. Both runtime operations travel through the existing closed RubyRuntimePlan under ExceptionBoundarySemantics. Keep the printer syntax-only and update the checked lowering inventory. Haxe TTry has no cleanup/ensure payload, so do not add an unowned ensure field or a general control-flow IR.
acceptance_criteria: TTry/TThrow ordinary syntax no longer uses raw Ruby or prints child AST for re-embedding; every new constructor/carrier is covered by RubyASTChildren, validator, printer, identity/sentinel/scope tests, and inventory assertions; ordered String/Int/Dynamic and exact haxe.Exception catches behave correctly; native StandardError crosses the boundary, nonmatching catches bare-reraise with useful backtrace, and thrown expressions evaluate once; statement and value-position try/throw shapes have snapshot and Ruby runtime evidence; all maintained gates and exact-SHA canonical CI are green before closure.
notes:
Reprioritized as the next semantic family after the output-inert RubyAST child/traversal foundation.
Live-tree review refined the original scope. Reflaxe captures pre-filter TypedExpr, and current renderTry emits only catches[0], so byte-for-byte structuralization alone would preserve an untested semantic gap. The slice now owns ordered typed dispatch and unmatched re-raise. Ruby ensure was removed from the immediate design because Haxe TTry carries no cleanup body and no current producer earns that target field.
Focused execution refined the wildcard ABI: binding a native StandardError directly as haxe.Exception cannot satisfy generated get_message access. HxException.caught now provides the typed adapter, while HxException.wrap unwraps it for identity-preserving rethrow. The existing RubyRuntimePlan was extended instead of adding a second exception plan; its validator owns helper intent and receiver selection. The checked raw/print-reembed inventory fell from 324 to 318 sites.
Architecture-template audit confirmed Pattern B is still the least-powerful sufficient design: source catch types live only inside the one-way 133-line RubyExceptionLowering service, which returns ordinary structural RubyAST plus explicit request-local runtime requirements. No durable semantic IR, pass-manager migration, metadata side channel, printer repair, or unearned ensure node was added. RubyCompiler is now 14,568 lines/789 functions; the checked raw/print-reembed inventory is 318 sites.

Final-tree local evidence is green: complete npm test (all 32 examples, exception/runtime/parity/diagnostic/snapshot suites, 714-file Haxelib ZIP and gem); Rails 8.1.3 mandatory runtime including components, controller params, mailer, job, storage, cable, request integration, and Rails-owned interop; Playwright 15/15; production 26 runs/231 assertions plus eager load and release archive; Haxe formatter 644/779; npm audit 0 vulnerabilities; ruby-advisory-db 1,224 advisories with tracked lockfile clean and vulnerable control detected; full-history gitleaks clean. Exact-SHA canonical CI and hosted-release evidence remain pending before closure.
Shipped in c7e75cf1fdd16b6591cdcbeb4b198d833f263b72 and immutable v1.10.2. Canonical workflow 29676480880 passed all 14 jobs, including release job 88167861015. The exact four hosted assets were independently downloaded and hashed; sidecars and embedded provenance bind version/tag/tested SHA, complete manifests verify 714 ZIP and 334 gem payload entries, and a clean Ruby 3.4.10/RubyGems 3.6.9 rebuild reproduced all four hosted files byte-for-byte. Durable evidence is recorded in docs/release-live-evidence.md.
close_reason: Structural Ruby exception lowering and ordered typed catch dispatch shipped in immutable v1.10.2 with full local, exact-SHA CI, runtime/browser/production, package, security, manifest, provenance, and byte-reproduction evidence.

title: [HOTWIRE-001] Infer typed ActionCable client subscription channels
description: Replace app-facing ActionCable channel-name strings with a typed compile-time channel reference inferred from an @:railsChannel contract. Preserve Rails and ActionCable runtime ownership: generated browser JavaScript must still call the standard consumer.subscriptions.create(...) API, and generated Ruby channel output must remain unchanged.
design: Audit whether a direct channel class reference, generated ChannelRef<TParams, TPayload>, or focused macro can expose the @:railsChannel native identifier to the JavaScript target without compiling Ruby-only runtime code into the client. Prefer an erased typed reference that carries both subscription params and received payload types, validates @:railsChannel metadata, infers the emitted Rails constant name, and lowers to the existing ActionCable identifier object. Keep an explicitly named checked external-channel escape for Rails-owned channels if the existing string API must remain for compatibility; do not use Dynamic, Any, cast, untyped, raw JavaScript, or open strings in the new canonical app-facing API.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical client code subscribes through a typed channel reference with no repeated channel-name string; the reference is inferred from an @:railsChannel class and preserves TParams/TPayload; wrong params, wrong received payloads, non-channel classes, and unchecked strings fail Haxe compilation; generated JavaScript remains ordinary consumer.subscriptions.create({channel: ...}, callbacks); generated Ruby channel output is unchanged; ActionCable runtime, Genes client compilation, package consumers, examples, docs, browser/production, security, complete local regression, and exact-SHA canonical CI pass before closure.
notes: Completed in d5b87fd906e5833d617fb069e12b5eed99e9fa01 with tested source b1fba23972223a1da0a469c21402375c224192e0 and immutable v1.9.0. title: [STDLIB-008] Add typed Ruby Regexp and MatchData facades
description: Add a bounded app-facing native Ruby regular-expression slice without conflating it with portable Haxe EReg semantics. Provide precise ruby.Regexp and ruby.MatchData contracts for common compilation, matching, capture, offset, name, and escaping workflows, then reuse them inside std/ruby/_std/EReg.hx only where generated output and Haxe behavior remain exact.
design: Curate supported signatures from the pinned Ruby/RBS contract and supported MRI sources. Keep construction string-based with typed options, direct native Regexp/MatchData calls, precise Null results for optional matches and captures, and no Dynamic, Any, cast, untyped, raw Ruby, or wrapper runtime in public facades. Omit arbitrary encoding flags, global backreference state, union over heterogeneous values, open option integers, mutable match state, and unchecked named capture access. Treat Haxe EReg as a separate semantics layer: retain target-local code wherever Haxe global replacement, split, matchedLeft/right/pos, or zero-length progress rules differ, and only substitute typed facades when snapshots and runtime parity prove identical output and behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Public Regexp and MatchData facades compile with precise types and emit ordinary Ruby constants, construction, match, capture, offset, name, and escape calls; focused runtime and generated-shape evidence passes on supported Ruby 3.3, 3.4, and 4.0 lanes; negative compilation rejects wrong option, pattern, capture, and direct unsafe boundaries; catalog, provenance, std inventory, package consumers, docs, and component audits remain truthful; any EReg reuse preserves all existing Haxe semantics and generated snapshots; all maintained examples, complete npm test, Rails runtime, browser, production, formatting, security, packages, release contracts, and exact-SHA canonical CI pass before closure.
notes: Completed in 2cca60633bbff57a175f93524131547ef5cab3c9 and immutable v1.8.0. Added precise require-free ruby.Regexp, ruby.MatchData, ruby.MatchOffset, RegexpOptions, and RegexpCompileOptions contracts; EReg reuses only typed escaping and its internal MatchData carrier where Haxe semantics remain exact. Focused generated-shape/runtime/negative evidence passes MRI 3.3.11, 3.4.10, and canonical Ruby 4.0; catalog, inventory, parity, component audit, package ZIP/gem consumers, 32 examples, full compiler suites, Rails 8.1.3 runtime, Playwright 15/15, production 26 runs/231 assertions, formatting, npm/ruby advisory audits, and Gitleaks pass. Canonical workflow 29630132266 passed all 14 jobs at exact SHA, including release job 88044807989. Published v1.8.0 is immutable with exactly four independently downloaded and hashed assets; embedded manifests verify 704 ZIP and 332 gem payload entries plus exact provenance. title: [STDLIB-007] Add modern Time parsing and Rails zoned-time facades
description: Complete the modern supported temporal path without reviving deprecated Ruby DateTime: add a require-backed strict ruby.TimeParsing facade while preserving require-free core ruby.Time, then add Rails-owned typed access to Time.current, Time.zone, ActiveSupport::TimeZone, and ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone under std/rails. Keep the Ruby and Rails ownership layers explicit and emit ordinary Time and ActiveSupport calls.
design: Map ruby.TimeParsing to native Time behind require 'time' for strict iso8601 and explicit-format strptime only. Map rails.active_support.RailsTime to Rails-extended native Time and add direct native TimeZone/TimeWithZone externs behind deduplicated requires for 'active_support' and 'active_support/time'; the base require initializes conversion configuration that the time extension reads outside a booted Rails app. TimeZone owns lookup, local/epoch/strict ISO/RFC3339 construction, now, name, and offsets; TimeWithZone owns components, zone identity, formatting, UTC/native-Time conversion, and seconds arithmetic. Do not construct TimeWithZone directly. Preserve core ruby.Time as require-free. Omit permissive Time.parse/TimeZone.parse, mutable Time.zone=, use_zone blocks, DateTime, Rational/subseconds beyond Float, Duration/calendar arithmetic, open timezone objects, unchecked options, and broad delegation.
acceptance_criteria: The new public facades contain no Dynamic, Any, cast, untyped, raw Ruby, or runtime wrapper; generated Ruby contains deduplicated require "time" and require "active_support/time" only when their owning facade is used and otherwise preserves require-free ruby.Time; focused shape/runtime tests prove strict Time parsing and Rails current/zone lookup, local/epoch/ISO/RFC3339 construction, components, offsets, formatting, UTC conversion, and arithmetic; negative compilation rejects wrong scalar types, direct TimeWithZone construction, omitted permissive parsing, and ruby.DateTime; docs explicitly recommend Time/Date and Rails Time.current/Time.zone over DateTime and distinguish Rails datetime columns from Ruby DateTime; catalog, std inventory, component dependency audit, package consumers, full local regression, mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime on Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0, browser, production, security, packaging, and exact-SHA canonical CI pass before closure.
notes: Implemented the modern supported temporal path: require-free core ruby.Time, strict require-backed ruby.TimeParsing, and direct RailsTime, TimeZone, and TimeWithZone externs. Runtime reproduction proved that ActiveSupport 8.1.3 needs both active_support and active_support/time outside a booted Rails app for TimeWithZone to_time configuration, so the Rails temporal types intentionally own both deduplicated requires. Full npm test and the exact Rails runtime, Playwright, production 26 runs and 231 assertions, formatting, package, reproducibility, npm audit, gitleaks, and fresh ruby-advisory-db gates passed. The fresh advisory database exposed sanitizer findings in the tracked todoapp lock; loofah 2.25.2 and rails-html-sanitizer 1.7.1 remediate them and the vulnerable control fixture still detects findings. Canonical exact-SHA workflow 29619029159 passed all 14 jobs at cdb9080c4ea4bbd8d95b682cd196dce314f237da and published immutable v1.7.0. title: [STDLIB-006] Add bounded typed Ruby Time and Date facades
description: Add a coherent bounded Ruby-semantic time/date catalog slice: a direct native ruby.Time core facade and a require-backed ruby.Date default-gem facade, while preserving the separate Haxe-semantic std/ruby/_std/Date.hx contract. Cover common construction, components, formatting, UTC/local conversion, epoch/day arithmetic, and strict parsing without widening into open Numeric, Rational, unchecked options, timezone-database, calendar-reform, or DateTime contracts.
design: Keep ruby.Time core-only and require-free: now/at/local/utc factories, component and offset accessors, non-mutating UTC/local copies, epoch conversion, strftime, and precisely named seconds arithmetic/difference mapped directly to native operators. Keep ruby.Date behind require "date": civil construction, today, strict iso8601/strptime parsing, calendar components/predicates, formatting, and integer-day movement. Use reviewed RBS plus supported Ruby sources, Haxe-idiomatic names with @:native mappings, no Dynamic/Any/cast/untyped/raw Ruby/runtime wrapper. Do not make the public facades pretend to be Haxe Date. Audit std/ruby/_std/Date.hx for exact typed reuse only if generated output and Haxe semantics remain unchanged; otherwise document the retained boundary. Defer DateTime, permissive parse, Rational/subsecond values, reform starts, named timezone databases, mutating zone conversion, enumerators/blocks, open Numeric coercions, and unchecked option bags.
acceptance_criteria: ruby.Time and ruby.Date compile as precise direct native contracts and emit ordinary Time/Date calls with only Date requiring "date"; focused fixtures prove construction, components, formatting, UTC/local behavior, epoch/day arithmetic, strict parsing, nullability, and negative type boundaries; catalog/docs/package inventories record reviewed provenance and bounded omissions; Haxe Date and DateTools parity remains unchanged; focused tests pass on MRI 3.3/3.4/4.0 canonical lanes; all maintained examples, complete npm test, mandatory Rails runtime, browser, production, package, formatting, security, release-contract, and exact-SHA canonical CI gates pass before closure.
notes: Implemented direct typed ruby.Time and require-backed ruby.Date facades with no Dynamic/Any/cast/untyped/raw Ruby, strict negative compile boundaries, native MRI runtime evidence, reviewed RBS/Ruby provenance, collision-safe portable Haxe Date output as HxDate in hx_date.rb, package consumers, and catalog/docs updates to 22 domains and 31 facades. Full local compiler, all 32 examples, mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime, Playwright 15/15, production 26 runs/231 assertions, ZIP/gem, formatting, security, release-contract, and reproducibility gates passed. Initial implementation workflow 29560749968 failed closed before publication on an over-broad require-order change; fix 449770dcee471fc349db69149f8b48ffa43e3644 restored load-path-first feature resolution. Canonical workflow 29564630637 passed all 14 jobs and release job 87841193089 published immutable v1.6.0. Independently downloaded assets and sidecars match exact bytes/SHA; embedded provenance matches the tested SHA and manifests verify 694 ZIP plus 323 gem payload entries. Durable evidence is in docs/release-live-evidence.md.
close_reason: Bounded Time/Date slice implemented, fully regressed, published from exact green canonical CI as immutable v1.6.0, and independently verified.

title: [STDLIB-005] Add a typed Ruby Set facade
description: Add a bounded public ruby.Set<T> facade for the supported Ruby 3.3, 3.4, and 4.0 matrix. Preserve Ruby eql?/hash element semantics, generic element typing, precise mutation/nullability, and direct native set algebra without presenting the surface as portable haxe.ds behavior.
design: Expose one generic @:native("Set") extern with array-only optional construction; size/empty/membership; add/delete/clear plus nullable add?/delete? results; same-element Set merge/replace/subtract; union/intersection/difference; subset/superset/intersection predicates; typed native block iteration and Bool-narrowed delete/keep filters; and explicit Array<T> conversion. Require set for Ruby 3.3/3.4 compatibility even though Ruby 4.0 promotes Set to core. Pin official ruby/rbs v4.0.3 core/set.rbs, ruby/set v1.1.0 and v1.1.1, and ruby/ruby v4.0.0 set.c provenance. Omit open Enumerable inputs, variadic constructors/merge, type-changing transforms, classify/divide/flatten, identity-comparison mode, mutable-element reset, subclass/CoreSet contracts, implicit Haxe iterator conversion, raw operators, and unchecked values.
acceptance_criteria: The public facade contains no Dynamic, Any, cast, untyped, raw Ruby, runtime wrapper, or broad Enumerable boundary; generated Ruby has exactly one require set and direct Set.new/receiver calls/native blocks with no wrapper; generic construction/membership/mutation/nullability/algebra/relations/filtering/conversion and Ruby duplicate semantics pass Ruby 3.3, 3.4, and 4.0; negative compilation rejects mixed element types, open Array merge, implicit Array conversion, and omitted identity APIs; catalog/provenance/inventory/package/release contracts are mandatory; all maintained examples, complete npm test, Rails 8.1.3 runtime, browser, production, formatting, security, packages, and exact-SHA canonical CI pass before closure.
notes: Implemented the bounded generic native ruby.Set<T> facade with optional Array<T> construction; typed size, emptiness, membership, mutation and precise nullable add?/delete? results; same-element merge/replace/subtract and set algebra; subset/superset/intersection relations; typed native blocks for iteration and filtering; and explicit Array<T> conversion. Public code contains no Dynamic, Any, cast, untyped, raw Ruby, wrapper runtime, broad Enumerable boundary, or implicit iterator conversion. Generated Ruby emits exactly one require set plus direct Set.new, receiver calls, and native blocks. Negative compilation rejects mixed construction/mutation, Array merge, implicit Array conversion, and identity APIs. Official provenance is ruby/rbs v4.0.3 core/set.rbs SHA-256 afd74d61411991a0691e892152565593debbee304b3a25b792f208c4673dd79b; ruby/set v1.1.0 and v1.1.1 source SHA-256 c77fd2da96b4556a5b6de722a829228df0eae9f87c2de3c972124593dd5956aa and bb376256e24b623381fa0098e4c3e6f46da9255244aca0d0076a0cded1d5b0b9; and ruby/ruby v4.0.0 set.c SHA-256 21b153cdf5b87facefadd8639fd10d5eb5c75652fa2dfb7f4f455a2fc77ff97f. Full local regression passed focused Set behavior on Ruby 3.3.11/set 1.1.0 and Ruby 3.4.10/set 1.1.1, all 32 maintained examples with output/runtime contracts, complete npm test, mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime, Chromium 15/15, production 26 runs and 231 assertions plus eager load/assets/archive, formatting, full-history Gitleaks, Haxelib/gem consumers, and artifact reproducibility. Exact implementation SHA 4fb3bfef55f6c5382fddfb569f8fa21527078bd2 passed canonical CI run 29542618466 with all 14 jobs across Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0; release job 87772812982 published final immutable v1.5.0. Independent downloads matched all four API sizes/digests and canonical sidecars; complete manifests verified 691 Haxelib payload entries and 321 gem payload entries containing ruby.Set. Open Enumerable inputs, variadic construction/merge, type-changing transforms, classify/divide/flatten, identity mode, reset, subclass/CoreSet contracts, implicit iteration, raw operators, and unchecked values remain explicitly unsupported.
close_reason: Bounded typed native Set facade, explicit unsupported boundaries, cross-Ruby runtime evidence, all local regression gates, exact-SHA 14-job CI, package manifests, and immutable v1.5.0 publication are verified.

title: [STDLIB-004] Add a typed Open3 capture facade
description: Add a bounded public Open3 catalog facade for direct-exec capture across supported MRI Ruby 3.3, 3.4, and 4.0. Preserve stdout, stderr, and process status as precise types while preventing shell interpretation and avoiding heterogeneous Dynamic tuples.
design: Expose ruby.Open3, ruby.Open3Executable, ruby.Open3Capture, and ruby.Open3Status. Open3Executable must lower to Ruby Process.spawn direct-exec array form [path, argv0]; Open3.capture accepts typed rest String arguments and emits direct Open3.capture3(executable, *arguments). Open3Capture owns a private fixed-position adapter for the official [String, String, Process::Status] tuple and exposes only stdout, stderr, and status. Omit command-line shell strings, environment/process option hashes, stdin_data/binmode keywords, capture2/capture2e duplicate shapes, popen streams, pipelines, and unchecked argument bags until separate typed lifecycle contracts exist. Pin official ruby/rbs v4.0.3 and ruby/open3 v0.2.1 provenance.
acceptance_criteria: Public inputs/results contain no Dynamic, Any, cast, untyped, raw Ruby, unchecked shell strings, or runtime wrappers; generated Ruby has one require open3, direct Open3.capture3 calls, a native argv splat, and fixed tuple reads; executable array form proves no shell interpolation; stdout/stderr/status/nullability and nonzero/success behavior pass MRI Ruby 3.3, 3.4, and 4.0; focused compile, shape, runtime, negative source/security, catalog, inventory, package, and release-contract evidence is mandatory; all 32 maintained examples, complete npm test, Rails runtime, browser, production, formatting, security, packages, and exact-SHA canonical CI pass before closure.
notes: Implemented the bounded typed ruby.Open3 direct-exec capture facade with ruby.Open3Executable, ruby.Open3Capture, and ruby.Open3Status. Public inputs/results contain no Dynamic, Any, cast, untyped, raw Ruby, runtime wrapper, or unchecked shell string. Generated Ruby emits one require open3, direct Open3.capture3([path, argv0], *arguments), and fixed tuple/status reads; the literal security argument reached the child process without shell interpolation. Focused compile, shape, runtime, negative-source, catalog, inventory, package, and release-contract checks passed on Ruby 3.3.11/Open3 0.2.1 and Ruby 3.4.10/Open3 0.2.1; canonical CI owns Ruby 4.0 evidence. Official provenance is ruby/rbs v4.0.3 open3.rbs SHA-256 66457894b07516cf0df9f8abbc3ec5b0bd9d61f3ffc3f6c8f5c073aba16400c2 and ruby/open3 v0.2.1 source SHA-256 values 551b8a2bc027d004fa38e4a8915e7ecffd80c8ab223ce79eb6335d8b91b8f27b and 1d225c4d3e005b543a9d2a3ea7c3dea2a18bdc06c9c649acae848a0cb3dc86cd. Full local regression passed all 32 maintained examples with expected-output/runtime contracts, complete npm test, mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime, Chromium 15/15, production 26 runs and 231 assertions plus eager load/assets/archive, formatting, Gitleaks, Haxelib/gem consumers, and artifact reproducibility. Exact implementation SHA 7cfc67c49485c7993404cb5347371984891b2de3 passed canonical CI run 29527916051 with all 14 jobs across Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0 and release job 87728835448 published final immutable v1.4.0. Independent downloads matched all four API sizes/digests and canonical sidecars; complete manifests verified 690 Haxelib payload entries and 320 gem payload entries with all four Open3 contracts. Shell command lines, environment/process option hashes, stdin/binmode keywords, capture2/capture2e, popen streams, pipelines, and unchecked argument bags remain explicitly unsupported.
close_reason: Bounded typed Open3 direct-exec capture facade, security boundaries, cross-Ruby runtime evidence, all local regression gates, exact-SHA 14-job CI, package manifests, and immutable v1.4.0 publication are verified.

title: [STDLIB-003] Add a typed CSV facade
description: Add the first public CSV catalog facade for default-option and bounded typed-option parsing and generation across supported MRI Ruby 3.3, 3.4, and 4.0. Preserve nullable CSV fields explicitly and emit ordinary CSV module calls with a deduplicated csv require.
design: Expose ruby.CSV plus semantic row and option typedefs. Provide direct methods for single-line parsing, whole-string parsing, file reads, block-based file iteration, single-row generation, and multi-row generation. Typed keyword carriers may expose only stable string-preserving options such as separators, quote behavior, field limits, blank handling, stripping, and liberal parsing. Omit headers, tables, converters, arbitrary field objects, IO inputs, encodings, open modes, and unchecked keyword splats because they change result types or widen the boundary. Record official ruby/rbs v4.0.3 provenance and documentation/runtime review without claiming whole CSV coverage.
acceptance_criteria: All public inputs and results are precise and contain no Dynamic, Any, cast, untyped, or raw Ruby; nullable fields and empty input are truthful; generated Ruby contains one require csv and direct CSV calls/blocks/keywords with no wrapper; focused Haxe compile, generated-shape, MRI runtime, negative source-policy, catalog, inventory, package, and release-contract evidence is mandatory; the runtime contract passes Ruby 3.3, 3.4, and 4.0; all maintained examples, complete npm test, Rails runtime, browser, production, formatting, security, packages, and exact-SHA canonical CI pass before closure.
notes: Implemented the bounded typed ruby.CSV catalog facade with nullable string rows, precise parse/generate option carriers, direct CSV module calls, deduplicated require emission, block-based file iteration, generated-shape/runtime/negative tests, official ruby/rbs v4.0.3 provenance, catalog and gap inventory updates, and exact Haxelib/gem package checks. Headers, tables, converters, arbitrary field objects, IO inputs, encodings, open modes, and unchecked keyword splats remain explicitly omitted. Focused runtime evidence passed Ruby 3.3.11 with CSV 3.2.8 and Ruby 3.4.10 with CSV 3.3.2; exact-SHA canonical CI supplied Ruby 4.0 evidence. Final local regression passed the complete npm test suite with all 32 maintained examples, mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime, Chromium 15/15, production 26 runs and 231 assertions plus Zeitwerk/assets/archive, Haxe formatting, Gitleaks, package consumers, release contracts, and diff hygiene. Canonical CI run 29516435128 passed all 14 jobs for exact SHA 26d65f1f5d46ebe0e6a4b98ffc412986dbe3bcb5 and published immutable v1.3.0. Independent downloads matched all four hosted API digests and sidecars; full manifests verified 686 ZIP payload entries and 316 gem payload entries with all four CSV contracts.
close_reason: Typed CSV facade, bounded scope, cross-Ruby runtime evidence, all local regression gates, exact-SHA 14-job CI, package manifests, and immutable v1.3.0 publication are verified.

title: [STDLIB-002] Extract deterministic RBS-to-Haxe generator core
description: Establish the reusable deterministic RBS-to-Haxe generation foundation needed by the Ruby stdlib catalog without claiming new facade domains. Extract the strict precise-or-omitted RBS parser from the Rails adoption generator into a RubyHx-wide module, add a Rails-independent extern renderer and maintainer-facing fixture command, and preserve existing adoption output exactly.
design: Keep this slice deliberately conservative: support only the already-proven scalar, nilable scalar, Symbol, nested Array, constructor, instance-method, and class-method subset. Unsupported declarations remain explicit review-marked omissions; never synthesize Dynamic, Any, cast, untyped, or raw Ruby. Canonicalize declaration/method ordering and LF output for byte-reproducible generation. The adoption generator must delegate to the shared parser/renderer boundary while retaining its current public CLI, diagnostics, and committed snapshot byte-for-byte. Do not mark CSV, Open3, Set, or whole-library coverage implemented in this bead.
acceptance_criteria: A packaged RubyHx-wide RBS parser/generator can produce a precise Haxe extern from checked fixture input without Rails; identical input produces byte-identical output and source-order permutations produce the documented canonical output; malformed, missing, unsafe, overloaded, keyword/block/open, and unsupported types fail closed or become explicit review-marked omissions according to the strict contract; generated output contains no Dynamic, Any, cast, untyped, or raw Ruby; the existing Rails adoption RBS snapshot remains byte-identical while delegating to shared code; the generator has focused unit/smoke coverage, package inclusion checks, and documented bounded scope; all maintained examples, npm test, Rails runtime, browser, production, package, release-contract, and exact-SHA canonical CI gates pass before closure.
notes: Implemented in ffec2b5993e557bc72ea6fe9a18dd3a4623db9fa and published as immutable v1.2.0. Extracted the existing strict adoption parser into packaged HXRuby::Rbs::SourceParser; adoption delegates to the shared parser and renderer in source-order compatibility mode with its committed snapshot unchanged. Added a Rails-independent stdout-only checked-root CLI, canonical LF renderer, source-order permutation evidence (including unsupported review markers), Haxe name/argument collision failures, exact declaration/module boundaries, missing/traversal/symlink/malformed failures, no Dynamic/Any/cast/untyped/raw Ruby in generated output, Haxe compilation, direct MRI runtime dispatch, catalog/docs/package checks, and unpacked ZIP/gem generator smokes. Local evidence: Ruby 3.3.11 and 3.4.10 focused gates; all 32 maintained examples; complete npm test; mandatory Rails 8.1.3 runtime; Chromium 15/15; production 26 runs/231 assertions; formatting and gitleaks. Exact-SHA CI run 29485362922 passed all 14 jobs across Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0 and release job 87585643211 published v1.2.0. Downloaded asset digests/sidecars/manifests verified; ZIP has 682 payload files, gem 312. CSV/Open3/Set remain planned and are not claimed by this bead.
close_reason: Strict deterministic RBS generator foundation is implemented, fully regression-tested, exact-SHA CI green, packaged, and published as immutable v1.2.0; later facade domains remain separate parent work.

title: [STDLIB-001] Add versioned Ruby stdlib coverage inventory and typed URI facade
description: Establish the first bounded post-1.0 stdlib-catalog slice: package and enforce a machine-readable domain coverage inventory tied to the stable support matrix, distinguish core, standard-library, default-gem, bundled-gem, and platform-specific ownership, and add a precise typed ruby.URI facade as the first new catalog domain.

Every entry declares supported Ruby branches, distribution category, coverage status, require/constant ownership, facade path, and focused evidence. Validate the current runtime branch against the packaged inventory while keeping newer unverified branches forward-permissive. Hand-curate the URI subset from deterministic upstream RBS plus Ruby source/docs: direct native calls, a nominal URIValue, precise nullability, no Dynamic/Any/cast/raw Ruby/runtime wrapper, and explicit omissions for open enumerable, variadic, encoding, mutation, and scheme-specific shapes. A general RBS-to-Haxe generator remains later parent-epic work.

Every implemented public ruby.* facade is accounted for or explicitly classified. ruby.URI and ruby.URIValue compile with precise signatures, emit require "uri" plus direct URI/receiver calls, and pass runtime/shape tests on MRI Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0. Public docs explain coverage, omissions, RBS provenance, and target ownership. notes:

title: Build a versioned typed Ruby core and stdlib facade catalog
description: RubyHx should offer broad typed Haxe access to Ruby core and standard-library APIs while preserving a separate Haxe std semantics layer. Inventory the supported MRI Ruby 3.3, 3.4, and 4.0 surfaces from lib/hxruby/support_matrix.json; distinguish core, stdlib, default gems, bundled gems, and platform-specific APIs; establish deterministic RBS-backed contract generation; curate unsupported Ruby shapes without broad Dynamic; and implement coherent facade domains with generated-shape and runtime evidence.
design: Keep public ruby.* contracts Ruby-semantic and direct-to-native. Keep std/ruby/_std Haxe-semantic. Prefer typed native contracts first when reusable, allow _std to consume them only on exact semantic matches, and add narrow adapters for Haxe/Ruby differences. Generate conservatively from deterministic RBS, then compile, review, document, snapshot, and test across the supported Ruby matrix. Start with a coverage manifest and URI, followed by CSV, Open3, Set, time/date, and other prioritized domains. Audit existing raw-native _std seams for safe typed reuse without changing output.
acceptance_criteria: A machine-checked versioned coverage inventory consumes the support manifest; deterministic generation is reproducible and introduces no broad Dynamic or unreviewed signatures; public docs distinguish implemented coverage from the long-term whole-stdlib goal; representative domains have precise Haxe contracts, idiomatic direct Ruby output, requires, examples or focused fixtures, and MRI Ruby 3.3/3.4/4.0 runtime evidence; _std reuse preserves Haxe semantics and never leaks Ruby-only types through portable APIs.

The Rails temporal slice adds three Rails-owned facade files outside that Ruby catalog. The epic acceptance criteria are satisfied; future consumer-backed domains and typed _std reuse audits belong in separate follow-up beads rather than silently widening this completed contract.

title: Make RBS service adoption strict and Dynamic-free
description: The deterministic --rbs service lane currently widens unsupported and application-specific RBS types to Dynamic, even though formal metadata should fail narrow rather than leak unsafety into generated externs. Align it with the strict YARD contract: generate only fully understood signatures and omit uncertain methods with actionable review diagnostics.
design: Keep the existing non-executing, app-root-confined RBS input boundary and parser ownership. Map the supported scalar/nilable subset precisely, extend only conventional container/symbol types with deterministic syntax, and treat untyped, Object, domain types, malformed/complex signatures, overloads, or unsupported arguments/returns as whole-method omissions. Preserve RBS > YARD > source precedence. Generated strict RBS externs must contain no Dynamic, cast, untyped, or raw Ruby. Update user/generated-app/security/compatibility documentation and focused compile/negative/release contracts.
acceptance_criteria: --service Foo --rbs sig/foo.rbs emits precise constructors, instance methods, and class methods for supported signatures; unsupported or application-specific signatures are omitted with actionable review comments; generated RBS-backed externs contain no Dynamic, cast, untyped, or raw Ruby; missing/unsafe/unparseable RBS remains fail-closed; source precedence remains RBS then YARD then Ruby shape; focused generator output, negative, manifest, compile, snapshot/release guards plus full required QA and green pushed CI pass.
notes: Implemented strict precise-or-omitted RBS service adoption. Supported scalar/nilable/Symbol/recursive Array types generate precise extern methods; domain/open/unsupported/overloaded/complex signatures are omitted with actionable review comments, and constructor returns must be void. Canonical RBS paths now reject symlink escapes; malformed declarations fail closed. Added a committed RBS extern snapshot, generated-Haxe compile calls, no-Dynamic/cast/raw/untyped checks, missing/symlink/malformed negatives, release-contract guards, and README/gradual-adoption/interop/workflow/gem-layer/compatibility/roadmap/security/generator-design/generated-app documentation. Local QA green: npm test with 30 examples and both packages, public precommit/Gitleaks, Todo Playwright 15/15, production 26 runs/217 assertions plus eager load/archive, and mandatory Rails runtime.
close_reason: Implemented strict Dynamic-free RBS service adoption. Supported scalar/nilable/Symbol/recursive Array signatures generate precise externs; unsupported/open/domain/overloaded/complex methods are omitted with actionable review diagnostics; RBS paths are canonicalized against symlink escapes and malformed structure fails closed. Added committed snapshot, Haxe compile/no-escape/negative/manifest/release guards and complete README/workflow/gradual-adoption/interop/compatibility/roadmap/security/generated-app documentation. Local npm test, 30 examples, both packages, public precommit/Gitleaks, Todo Playwright 15/15, production 26 runs/217 assertions, and mandatory Rails runtime passed. GitHub CI run 29266409499 passed browser, production, Rails runtime, full Ruby 3.2/3.3/4.0, audit, formatter, release contracts, and exact-SHA release verification for fef422bd303f3ddae461230c9b34df01210228c3.

title: Auto-discover deterministic YARD contracts in gem adoption
description: The generic hxruby:adopt --gem NAME --write contracts lane currently parses only Ruby method shape and emits review-marked Dynamic placeholders even when the installed gem already carries precise YARD signatures. Reuse the deterministic non-executing YARD parser across Bundler-resolved gem lib sources so app-local gem layers automatically gain precise contracts without requiring users to locate and pass each --yard path manually.
design: Resolve the gem through the app Bundler definition, canonicalize its lib root, and reject Ruby-file symlink escapes. Parse each file without loading it. A constant with at least one actual YARD signature tag uses the strict YARD contract for all of its methods: supported positional signatures are typed, while undocumented, unsupported, or complex methods are emitted only as review comments. Constants with no YARD signature tags retain the existing review-marked Ruby-shape skeleton. Aggregate reopened constants deterministically and give YARD precedence across files. Document the per-constant metadata source in the generated gem-layer review guide.
acceptance_criteria: Generic --gem NAME --discover reports auto-detected strict YARD constants; --gem NAME --write contracts generates precise compiling externs from supported YARD tags without executing gem source or requiring --yard; every YARD-backed generated contract contains no Dynamic, cast, raw Ruby, or untyped fallback, and uncertain methods are omitted with actionable review comments; constants without YARD signature metadata retain the existing clearly marked Ruby-shape fallback; Bundler gem lib paths are canonicalized and symlink escapes fail closed; reopened constants merge deterministically with YARD precedence; generated documentation explains metadata ownership and per-constant source; focused output/snapshot/manifest/negative/compile/package contracts, npm test, examples, Playwright, production, Rails runtime, public precommit, and GitHub CI pass.
notes: Implemented automatic non-executing YARD selection for Bundler-resolved gem lib sources. Strict constants are precise-or-omitted across same-file/cross-file reopenings; conflicting documented definitions become load-order review diagnostics; source-only constants retain the pre-existing marked skeleton. Added canonical lib/source checks with symlink-escape rejection, per-constant generated provenance docs, committed extern/doc snapshots, source-execution trap, manifest/negative/no-escape assertions, generated-Haxe compilation, release guards, and README/compatibility/workflow/gradual-adoption/gem-layer/security/generated-app documentation. Local verification green: npm test with 30 examples and packages, public precommit/Gitleaks, Todo Playwright 15/15, production 26 runs/217 assertions plus eager load/archive, and mandatory Rails runtime.
close_reason: Implemented automatic deterministic YARD discovery for Bundler-resolved gem lib sources without executing gem code or requiring manual --yard paths. YARD-backed constants are strict precise-or-omitted across same-file and cross-file reopenings; conflicting documented definitions become load-order review diagnostics; source-only constants retain the existing explicit review skeleton. Canonical path checks reject lib/source symlink escapes. Added per-constant generated provenance docs, committed extern/doc snapshots, source-execution trap, manifest/negative/no-Dynamic-cast-raw-untyped assertions, generated-Haxe compilation, release guards, and complete README/compatibility/gem-layer/workflow/gradual-adoption/security/generated-app documentation. Local npm test, 30 examples, packages, public precommit/Gitleaks, Todo Playwright 15/15, production 26 runs/217 assertions, and mandatory Rails runtime passed. GitHub CI run 29257859795 passed all browser, runtime, full Ruby 3.2/3.3/4.0, audit, dogfood, and exact-SHA release jobs for commit bcfedcb88a1c62af61aa45df6476b9b9ef52768b.

title: Add deterministic YARD service contract adoption
description: RailsHx adoption can infer service externs from Ruby source and an initial RBS subset, but the compatibility contract still lists YARD metadata as unimplemented. Add a bounded deterministic --yard source lane that parses documentation comments without executing Ruby and feeds the existing typed service-contract renderer.
acceptance_criteria: hxruby:adopt accepts one or more --yard Ruby source paths only with explicit --service constants and rejects missing/unsafe/unparseable files; the first subset handles nested class/module constants, constructors, instance/class methods, positional required/optional args, both conventional YARD @param layouts, @return, precise scalar/nilable/Array types, and Boolean literal unions; supported signatures generate precise compiling Haxe with no Dynamic/cast/raw escape; missing, mismatched, free-form, unsupported union/container, or complex Ruby argument shapes are omitted behind actionable review comments rather than guessed; Rails generator and Rake entrypoints forward YARD inputs; focused positive/negative/manifest/compile/package contracts pass; README, compatibility, gradual-adoption, generator-workflow, and gem-layer docs state the deterministic subset and limitations; npm test, examples, Playwright, production, mandatory Rails runtime, public precommit, and GitHub CI are green.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic, non-executing YARD service adoption with safe-path and explicit-service validation, nested constants, precise supported scalar/nilable/Array/Boolean types, review-only omission of unsupported signatures, Rails/Rake forwarding, manifest/compiler/package contracts, and comprehensive README/workflow/compatibility/gem-layer documentation. acceptance_criteria: The example compiles under the examples gate, has committed generated snapshots and runtime/Ruby-origin coverage, Array lowering removes obsolete helpers without semantic drift, and README/design/ownership/support documentation is complete.
