close_reason: Enabled upstream Map.unit.hx in Ruby unitstd lane with typed Ruby Hash-backed map helpers, ObjectMap identity semantics, focused map runtime checks, docs, inventory, snapshots, and package artifacts.

title: Enable EReg.unit.hx in Ruby unitstd lane
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-bjv.20. Enable EReg.unit.hx in npm run test:unitstd-ruby. Exact blockers: implement Ruby Regexp-backed EReg state with Haxe-compatible match/matched/matchedLeft/matchedRight/matchedPos semantics, global versus non-global replace/split/map behavior, capture group handling, escaping, and failure-state behavior. Add focused runtime tests and update manifest/docs before enabling.
close_reason: Enabled upstream EReg.unit.hx in the Ruby unitstd lane with a Ruby Regexp-backed EReg implementation, focused Ruby semantics checks, manifest/docs updates, and local npm test plus public precommit verification.

title: Enable Date.unit.hx in Ruby unitstd lane
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-bjv.20. Enable Date.unit.hx in npm run test:unitstd-ruby. Exact blockers: replace the current Date.cross.hx stub with Ruby Time/Date-backed semantics for now/fromTime/getTime/fromString, decide and document local timezone versus UTC behavior against Haxe expectations, cover date component extraction and formatting, and add runtime/unitstd tests before moving the manifest entry to enabled/adapted.
close_reason: Enabled upstream Date.unit.hx in the Ruby unitstd lane with a Ruby Time-backed Date implementation, documented local/UTC timezone semantics, and verified npm test plus public precommit.

title: Enable Array.unit.hx in Ruby unitstd lane
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-bjv.20. Enable test/upstream_unitstd/upstream/Array.unit.hx in npm run test:unitstd-ruby. Exact blockers: copy/adapt upstream helper dependencies such as unit.IntWrap deterministically, preserve Haxe array identity for normal ==/!= while keeping explicit structural fixture assertions where the upstream harness uses Assert.same/aeq semantics, triage array mutation/order methods under Ruby-native Array lowering, and handle in-expression assertions without parser/runtime drift. Update manifest/docs and add focused compiler/std/runtime tests before enabling.
close_reason: Enabled upstream Array.unit.hx in the portable Ruby unitstd lane, added the IntWrap helper, and fixed Ruby lowering/runtime gaps for Array mutation, dynamic Array/String calls, anonymous object field reads, comparator method references, sparse resize assertions, and key/value iterators.

title: Enable staged high-leverage upstream unitstd fixtures for Ruby
description: Broaden the Ruby unitstd parity lane beyond the baseline fixtures by enabling or deliberately adapting the manifest-tracked high-leverage upstream Haxe fixtures: Array.unit.hx, Date.unit.hx, EReg.unit.hx, Lambda.unit.hx, and Map.unit.hx. Each fixture should be enabled only after the Ruby compiler/std/runtime semantics it exercises are understood and either fixed or explicitly documented as an unsupported target gap.
acceptance_criteria: For each staged fixture in test/upstream_unitstd/manifest.json, either enable the checked-in upstream fixture in npm run test:unitstd-ruby or split a narrower follow-up with the exact semantic blocker; tests and snapshots cover any std/runtime/compiler fixes; manifest statuses and reasons are updated; docs/stdlib-ownership.md and test/upstream_unitstd/README.md reflect the new baseline; CI remains green.
close_reason: Enabled Lambda.unit.hx in the Ruby unitstd lane, documented the new baseline, and split Array/Date/EReg/Map into focused follow-ups haxe.ruby-bjv.25 through .28.

title: Fix Ruby lambda statement-body local lowering
description: While implementing Reflect.makeVarArgs coverage, a Haxe lambda with a block-local accumulator compiled to a Ruby lambda body that referenced the final local without emitting its declaration or loop statements. Repro shape: Reflect.makeVarArgs(function(values:Array<Dynamic>) { var total = 0; for (value in values) total += (value : Int); return total; }) generated a Ruby lambda body like ->(values) { total }. Preserve block-local declarations/control flow inside Ruby lambda bodies, then add focused coverage so statement-bodied lambdas used as values do not collapse to the final expression.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audit Array Lambda Map Ruby direct lowering
description: Audit Array, Lambda, and haxe.ds map lowerings for cases that can safely emit direct Ruby instead of routing through HXRuby helpers. Keep helpers for real Haxe semantic gaps such as negative boundaries, mutation contracts, structural equality, iterator behavior, and key identity.
acceptance_criteria: Audit notes identify each helper as direct-lowerable or semantically required; at least one safe direct lowering is implemented if found; tests prove unchanged Haxe behavior; snapshots are updated where expected; unitstd manifest reasons are updated where relevant.
close_reason: Implemented safe direct Ruby lowerings for Array.concat/contains/copy, documented Array/Lambda/Map helper audit decisions, refreshed generated route parity and stdlib snapshots, and verified with full npm test.

title: Document Ruby stdlib facade authoring patterns
description: Document how to add and consume typed Ruby stdlib facades such as ruby.File, ruby.Json, ruby.Kernel, and future ruby.Pathname or ruby.CSV surfaces; clarify when to use externs, facades, macros, direct lowering, or HXRuby helpers.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include package naming, API shape, direct Ruby lowering guidance, runtime-helper policy, examples, and test expectations; docs link from the relevant README or gap guidance; examples compile or are covered by smoke tests.
close_reason: Added Ruby stdlib facade authoring guide covering package naming, API shape, direct Ruby lowering, HXRuby helper policy, examples, and test expectations; linked it from docs and std README; verified inventory/gap/precommit gates.

title: Implement sys.FileSystem and sys.io.File for Ruby target
description: Implement Ruby-backed sys.FileSystem and sys.io.File for RubyHx using Ruby File, Dir, and FileUtils APIs. Keep Haxe exception and path behavior documented where Ruby differs.
acceptance_criteria: sys.FileSystem and sys.io.File inventory entries are implemented; tests cover exists/isDirectory/create/delete/read/write/stat-like behavior selected for the initial contract; generated Ruby uses Ruby stdlib APIs directly where safe; gap report is green; relevant runtime/std tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented sys.FileSystem and sys.io.File as erased Ruby std facades backed by direct Ruby File, Dir, and FileUtils calls; added Bytes.getString compatibility, compiler suppression for lowercase sys.* runtime shells, stdlib MVP runtime coverage, direct-output smoke assertions, and refreshed inventory/gap report to zero missing stdlib entries.

title: Implement haxe.Json for Ruby target
description: Implement haxe.Json for RubyHx. Prefer Ruby's JSON library for parsing/printing where compatible, but preserve Haxe null, number, string escaping, array/object, and error behavior through a small adapter if needed.
acceptance_criteria: haxe.Json is implemented under the documented std/_std ownership path; inventory/gap report are updated; tests cover parse/stringify success and failure cases plus representative upstream JSON behavior; generated Ruby requires Ruby JSON idiomatically; npm run test:stdlib-inventory, test:gap-report, test:runtime-minitest, and relevant snapshots pass.
close_reason: Implemented haxe.Json under std/_std using Ruby JSON.parse/generate/pretty_generate with require metadata, added native Ruby JSON facade methods, updated stdlib MVP coverage for parse/stringify/pretty output plus parser and replacer failure probes, refreshed inventory/gap report and stdlib snapshots, and verified focused gates plus public precommit.

title: Implement Reflect for Ruby target
description: Implement the missing Reflect surface for RubyHx with typed Haxe-facing APIs and Ruby-native lowering where behavior matches. Preserve Haxe semantics for anonymous object/hash boundaries, field access, method closures, and metadata-dependent behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Reflect has std inventory and gap-report entries marked implemented; focused runtime/smoke tests cover field, method, object, and null/error behavior; generated Ruby uses direct reflection where safe and compact HXRuby helpers only for semantic gaps; npm run test:stdlib-inventory, test:gap-report, test:runtime-minitest, and relevant std/unit tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented Ruby Reflect std facade and runtime helpers. Verified: npm run test:runtime-minitest; npm run test:stdlib-mvp; npm run test:unitstd-ruby; npm run test:examples-compile; npm run test:stdlib-inventory; npm run test:gap-report; npm run test:snapshots; npm run public:precommit.

title: Enable Std.unit.hx parity for Ruby
description: Adapt or enable upstream Haxe Std.unit.hx in the Ruby unitstd parity lane. Use the recent StdNumericParsing fixture as the bridge for numeric parser behavior, then cover Std.string, isOfType, downcast, enum stringification, and random behavior without weakening Haxe semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Std.unit.hx is enabled or split into documented adapted fixtures in test/upstream_unitstd/manifest.json; parser cases are covered by upstream or local adapted assertions; runtime/compiler changes preserve Haxe semantics while emitting direct Ruby where safe; npm run test:unitstd-ruby, test:runtime-minitest, test:stdlib-mvp, and relevant snapshots pass.
notes:
Enabled the adapted upstream Std.unit.hx parity fixture in the Ruby unitstd lane. Added support fixtures for Haxe toString, enum, class inheritance, and interface checks; added macro support for upstream unspec markers and in-expression assertions. Ruby output now emits real Ruby superclass/module relationships for Haxe-owned classes and interfaces so Std.isOfType works without a broad wrapper runtime. Std.string now uses HXRuby.stringify for semantic cases including null, enums, and Haxe toString while keeping HXRuby compact and purpose-driven. Refreshed snapshots and todoapp smoke expectations. Local gates passed: format:haxe:check, test:unitstd-ruby, test:runtime-minitest, test:stdlib-mvp, test:runtime-core, test:stdlib-inventory, test:gap-report, test:snapshots, test:m1, test:m2, public:precommit, test:todoapp-rails.
Follow-up from GitHub CI on b918c7d: native Rails layout should not emit config/initializers/hxruby_autoload.rb just because a Rails artifact requires HXRuby core. The compiler now relies on precise require_relative runtime requires for native artifacts, smoke tests reject the legacy initializer, and the todoapp snapshot manifest was refreshed. Additional local gates passed: test:active-job, test:rails-integration, test:rails-runtime, test:snapshots, public:precommit.
Follow-up from the amended CI run: production dogfood also needs the generated todoapp Rails application shell to ignore app/lib/railshx/runtime with Zeitwerk when app/lib is autoloaded/eager-loaded. The materializer now adds a normal Rails initializer in config/application.rb for that ignore rather than restoring the legacy hxruby_autoload initializer. Local gate passed: test:todoapp-production.
Follow-up from the full Ruby/Haxe matrix: updated remaining Ruby interop/extension smoke expectations that still assumed Std.string lowered to .to_s(), and regenerated the route parity Ruby artifact so it uses HXRuby.stringify for Haxe string semantics. Local gates passed: test:ruby-call-shapes, test:ruby-interop, test:ruby-extensions, test:routes-generator, and the post-route npm-test tail through test:gem-package.
close_reason: Std.unit.hx parity is enabled in the Ruby unitstd lane with semantic stringify/isOfType support and passing local gates.

title: Design optional RailsHx test-framework adapter DSL
description: Design an optional Haxe-authored test DSL adapter layer for the app's selected Ruby/Rails test framework, not specifically RSpec. Rails/Minitest remains the default adapter because Rails ships with it, but the abstraction should allow adapter packages such as rails.minitest, rails.rspec, or future framework-specific gems. Canonical syntax must remain valid Haxe and compiler-erased, lower to idiomatic target tests, preserve typed RailsHx assertions/fixtures/routes/models where possible, and keep framework-specific output behind explicit adapter metadata/generator configuration. Include design docs comparing Minitest/RSpec adapters, snapshot tests for generated test artifacts, negative compile tests for invalid DSL usage, and generator/docs flow for detecting the app's chosen test framework without forcing a dependency.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add upstream Haxe unitstd runtime parity harness for Ruby target
description: Design and implement a low-priority Haxe stdlib portability validation layer for haxe.ruby by reusing a curated subset of upstream Haxe tests/unit/src/unitstd specs, compiling them through haxe.ruby, and executing the generated Ruby/Minitest or plain Ruby runtime lane. This is separate from RailsHx feature work and should validate that portable Haxe stdlib/domain code behaves as Haxe expects when emitted to Ruby.
acceptance_criteria: Audit ../haxe.elixir.codex test/upstream_unitstd, test/haxe_exunit/stdlib_parity, scripts/test-haxe-exunit-stdlib.sh, docs/08-roadmap/stdlib-parity/*, and scripts/sync-upstream-unitstd-specs.sh as the precedent; create a checked-in curated upstream unitstd subset with provenance/licensing notes and deterministic sync script; add a focused command such as npm run test:haxe-ruby-stdlib or test:unitstd-ruby that compiles Haxe-authored std parity tests to Ruby and executes them; document snapshot vs runtime responsibilities, with snapshots owning generated Ruby shape and unitstd runtime tests owning Haxe std semantics; include initial specs for high-leverage modules already implemented in haxe.ruby such as Array, StringTools/String, Math, Date, EReg, Lambda, Map/StringMap/IntMap/ObjectMap, haxe.io.Bytes, and Std; update docs/stdlib-ownership.md, docs/gap-report-guidance.md, and the production-readiness evidence once the lane exists; keep this P4 until RailsHx production gaps are further reduced.
notes: 2026-06-30: Captured the compact-HXRuby stdlib lesson in AGENTS.md and tightened existing stdlib lowering. Haxe Math.exp no longer emits a Ruby Math wrapper, preserving Ruby's native Math.exp instead of routing through HXRuby; Math.isNaN lowers directly in the generated shell; Std shell methods now use correct semantic helpers only where needed and direct Ruby for int/random. Removed the unused :exp entry from HXRuby's math helper table and refreshed snapshots. Local gates passed: format:haxe:check, test:runtime-minitest, test:runtime-core, test:stdlib-mvp, test:unitstd-ruby, test:m1, test:m2, test:stdlib-inventory, test:gap-report, test:snapshots, public:precommit, ci:release-contracts.\n\n2026-06-30: Tightened Std.parseInt/Std.parseFloat after reviewing the Math/HXRuby lesson. The compiler no longer emits Ruby-first Integer/Float rescue shortcuts because Ruby parses whole strings while Haxe Std parsing returns valid numeric prefixes. HXRuby.parse_int/parse_float are now compact semantic shims: regex the Haxe-valid prefix, then delegate conversion to Ruby. Added focused unitstd Ruby checks plus direct runtime assertions, documented the shell-vs-compiler-special-lowering rule in AGENTS.md, refreshed snapshots, and verified with test:runtime-minitest, test:stdlib-mvp, test:unitstd-ruby, test:runtime-core, test:m1, test:m2, test:todoapp-rails, test:stdlib-inventory, test:gap-report, ci:release-contracts, and public:precommit.
close_reason: Established the Ruby upstream unitstd runtime parity lane with checked-in provenance-tracked fixtures, deterministic sync script, npm run test:unitstd-ruby, docs for snapshot-vs-runtime responsibilities, production-readiness evidence, and passing focused gates. Baseline fixtures cover IntIterator, Math, String, StringBuf, StringTools, adapted Std, and haxe.io.BytesBuffer; staged high-leverage Array/Date/EReg/Lambda/Map fixture expansion is tracked separately as haxe.ruby-bjv.20.

title: R&D: plan typed and optimized Ruby stdlib coverage
description: Research how haxe.ruby should track, manage, prioritize, implement, and support the whole Ruby standard library as a Haxe-typed and Ruby-optimized authoring layer. This is not immediate implementation; it should design the inventory, coverage tiers, typed extern/facade strategy, macro/generator opportunities, runtime-helper policy, optimization policy, conformance testing, docs, and long-term maintenance model for Ruby stdlib support.
acceptance_criteria: Produce an R&D report that inventories Ruby stdlib domains and prioritizes coverage tiers; defines which APIs should be typed externs, Haxe-owned facades, macros, abstracts, or optimized compiler lowering; identifies where Ruby-native direct calls should replace HXRuby runtime helpers; proposes tests using local Ruby docs/spec references and generated smoke fixtures; defines compatibility/version policy across supported Ruby versions; and splits concrete implementation beads for the first useful stdlib slices.
notes:
Low-priority R&D. Use ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/ruby and local Ruby docs/source/spec references where useful. Compare current Haxe std/runtime helpers against Ruby-native APIs and identify opportunities for direct optimized lowering without breaking portable semantics. Do not start broad stdlib implementation until this report produces a staged bead plan.
R&D report added in docs/ruby-stdlib-rd.md. First implementation/documentation follow-ups split as haxe.ruby-bjv.12.2 through haxe.ruby-bjv.12.7.
close_reason: R&D report added at docs/ruby-stdlib-rd.md and follow-up beads haxe.ruby-bjv.12.2 through haxe.ruby-bjv.12.7 created.

title: R&D: investigate RailsHx to Phoenix/Elixir migration adapter path
description: Research whether RailsHx/Haxe can support an optional compile-time adapter/translation layer that lets parts of a RailsHx app, or eventually a whole RailsHx app, target Phoenix/Elixir. The goal is not immediate implementation: investigate whether Haxe can preserve idiomatic Rails authoring while enabling gradual migration to Phoenix/Elixir, mixed Ruby/Elixir components, or parallel idiomatic Haxe.Elixir authoring through adapters/translations. This should compare compile-level translation, generated boundary adapters, runtime interop, framework semantic gaps, data/schema/query portability, templates/components, controllers/routes, jobs/mailers/realtime, and gradual migration workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Before implementation starts, the user must run a GPT 5.5 Pro research pass and return insights plus proposed bead/plan mutations. The R&D output should document feasibility, recommended architecture options, risks, non-goals, required collaboration with haxe.elixir.codex/PhoenixHx, and concrete follow-up beads if the idea is worth pursuing.
notes:
Blocked by the task's own acceptance criteria: implementation/design work must wait until the user runs the requested GPT 5.5 Pro research pass and returns feasibility notes plus proposed bead/plan mutations. Prompt was provided in-session on 2026-07-01.
2026-07-02: Detached from the haxe.ruby-bjv production-readiness epic. This remains optional post-readiness R&D and is still blocked on user-supplied GPT 5.5 Pro feasibility notes; it should not block the RailsHx production-ready beta gate.

title: Audit std helpers that can lower directly to idiomatic Ruby
description: Several std/Math and std/runtime helpers may delegate to HXRuby even when Ruby already has an equivalent receiver/module method, e.g. Math.abs -> value.abs. Audit similar cases and inline/direct-lower them when Ruby semantics match Haxe. Keep HXRuby helpers only where they encode Haxe-specific behavior, coercion/NaN/-0.0 edge cases, cross-version Ruby compatibility, or runtime reuse that materially improves correctness.
close_reason: Audited std Math helpers and direct-lowered Math.abs to value.abs and Math.isFinite to value.finite? because Ruby receiver semantics match Haxe for typed Float values. Kept HXRuby helpers where they still encode Haxe behavior, including min/max NaN propagation, domain-error-to-NaN math, pow, round, and isNaN for Integer-safe checks. Removed unused math_abs/math_finite? runtime helpers and added stdlib smoke assertions for the direct Ruby shapes. Verified with npm run test:runtime-minitest, npm run test:stdlib-mvp, UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 npm run test:snapshots, npm test.
